papers.title: Discovering Ordinary Differential Equations with LLM-Based Qualitative and Quantitative Evaluation
papers.summary: Discovering governing differential equations from observational data is a fundamental challenge in scientific machine learning. Existing symbolic regression approaches rely primarily on quantitative metrics; however, real-world differential equation modeling also requires incorporating domain knowledge to ensure physical plausibility. To address this gap, we propose DoLQ, a method for discovering ordinary differential equations with LLM-based qualitative and quantitative evaluation. DoLQ employs a multi-agent architecture: a Sampler Agent proposes dynamic system candidates, a Parameter Optimizer refines equations for accuracy, and a Scientist Agent leverages an LLM to conduct both qualitative and quantitative evaluations and synthesize their results to iteratively guide the search. Experiments on multi-dimensional ordinary differential equation benchmarks demonstrate that DoLQ achieves superior performance compared to existing methods, not only attaining higher success rates but also more accurately recovering the correct symbolic terms of ground truth equations. Our code is available at https://github.com/Bon99yun/DoLQ.

ai.tldr: 提出DoLQ方法，利用大语言模型进行定性和定量评估，从观测数据中发现常微分方程。
ai.motivation: 现有符号回归方法仅依赖定量指标，缺乏领域知识，难以保证物理合理性。
ai.method: 采用多智能体架构：采样器提出候选系统，参数优化器调整方程精度，科学家智能体利用LLM进行定性与定量评估并综合结果迭代搜索。
ai.result: 在多维常微分方程基准上，DoLQ比现有方法成功率更高，能更准确恢复真实方程的符号项。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户从数据中自动发现符合物理规律的微分方程，减少人工建模工作量。

idea_spark.idea: 将LLM的定性评估与定量优化结合，迭代引导搜索空间，提升符号回归的物理合理性。
idea_spark.risk: LLM可能产生幻觉或偏见，导致错误评估；依赖LLM的定性判断可能引入主观性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类科学家反复验证假设的过程启发，用LLM模拟专家对候选方程进行多维度评判。

en.tldr: Proposes DoLQ, a method that uses LLM-based qualitative and quantitative evaluation to discover ordinary differential equations from observational data.
en.motivation: Existing symbolic regression methods rely solely on quantitative metrics and lack domain knowledge, failing to ensure physical plausibility.
en.method: Employs a multi-agent architecture: a Sampler Agent proposes system candidates, a Parameter Optimizer refines equation accuracy, and a Scientist Agent uses an LLM to conduct qualitative and quantitative evaluations and synthesize results to iteratively guide the search.
en.result: On multi-dimensional ODE benchmarks, DoLQ achieves higher success rates and more accurately recovers the correct symbolic terms of ground truth equations compared to existing methods.
en.help_to_user: Helps users automatically discover physically plausible differential equations from data, reducing manual modeling effort.

idea_spark.idea: Combining LLM qualitative evaluation with quantitative optimization to iteratively guide the search space, improving physical plausibility in symbolic regression.
idea_spark.risk: LLMs may hallucinate or introduce bias, leading to incorrect evaluations; reliance on qualitative judgment may introduce subjectivity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the iterative hypothesis validation process of human scientists, using LLMs to simulate expert multi-dimensional judgment of candidate equations.

papers.title: Neural Operators as Efficient Function Interpolators
papers.summary: Neural operators (NOs) are designed to learn maps between infinite-dimensional function spaces. We propose a novel reframing of their use. By introducing an auxiliary base-space, any finite-dimensional function can be viewed as an operator acting by composition on functions of the base-space. Through a range of benchmarks on analytic functions of increasing complexity and dimensionality, we demonstrate that NOs can match or outperform standard multilayer perceptrons and Kolmogorov--Arnold Networks in accuracy while requiring significantly fewer parameters and training time. As a real-world application, we apply a two-dimensional Tensorized Fourier Neural Operator (TFNO) to the nuclear chart, learning a correction to state-of-the-art nuclear mass models as a partially observed residual field. A TFNO ensemble reaches a held-out root-mean-square error of 198.2 keV, placing it among the best recent neural-network approaches while retaining high parameter efficiency and short training times. More broadly, these results introduce NOs as a scalable framework for finite-dimensional function interpolation, from analytic benchmarks to structured scientific data.

ai.tldr: 提出将神经算子重新用作有限维函数插值器，通过辅助基空间将任意有限维函数视为算子，在解析函数和核质量模型修正上取得高精度与参数效率。
ai.motivation: 标准多层感知机和KAN网络在函数插值任务中参数多、训练慢，而神经算子原本设计用于无限维空间映射，本文探索其作为通用有限维函数插值器的潜力。
ai.method: 引入辅助基空间，将有限维函数视为通过复合作用于基空间函数的算子；使用张量化傅里叶神经算子（TFNO）对核图上的残差场进行学习。
ai.result: 在解析函数基准上，NO以更少参数和训练时间匹配或超越MLP/KAN；在核质量模型修正中，TFNO集成达到198.2 keV的均方根误差，参数效率高。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、可扩展的函数插值新框架，尤其适用于结构化科学数据（如核物理）中的残差建模。

idea_spark.idea: 将TFNO与物理信息约束结合，或用于多尺度科学数据（如分子动力学势能面）的插值。
idea_spark.risk: 辅助基空间的选择可能影响泛化性能，且对高维复杂函数的适用性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从核质量模型修正的成功中，启发将神经算子用于其他部分观测的物理场重建（如地震波场、电磁场）。

en.tldr: Proposes reframing neural operators as efficient finite-dimensional function interpolators via an auxiliary base-space, achieving high accuracy and parameter efficiency on analytic benchmarks and nuclear mass model correction.
en.motivation: Standard MLPs and KANs require many parameters and long training for function interpolation; neural operators, originally for infinite-dimensional maps, are explored as a general-purpose finite-dimensional interpolator.
en.method: Introduces an auxiliary base-space to view any finite-dimensional function as an operator acting by composition; uses a Tensorized Fourier Neural Operator (TFNO) to learn a residual field on the nuclear chart.
en.result: On analytic functions, NOs match or outperform MLP/KAN with fewer parameters and less training time; on nuclear mass correction, a TFNO ensemble achieves 198.2 keV RMSE with high parameter efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and scalable framework for function interpolation, especially suited for residual modeling on structured scientific data like nuclear physics.

idea_spark.idea: Combine TFNO with physics-informed constraints or apply it to multi-scale scientific data interpolation (e.g., molecular dynamics potential surfaces).
idea_spark.risk: Choice of auxiliary base-space may affect generalization; applicability to high-dimensional complex functions needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Success in nuclear mass correction inspires using neural operators for reconstructing other partially observed physical fields (e.g., seismic wavefields, electromagnetic fields).

papers.title: One Operator for Many Densities: Amortized Approximation of Conditioning by Neural Operators
papers.summary: Probabilistic conditioning is concerned with the identification of a distribution of a random variable $X$ given a random variable $Y$. It is a cornerstone of scientific and engineering applications where modeling uncertainty is key. This problem has traditionally been addressed in machine learning by directly learning the conditional distribution of a fixed joint distribution. This paper introduces a novel perspective: we propose to solve the conditioning problem by identifying a single operator that maps any joint density to its conditional, thus amortizing over joint-conditional pairs. We establish that the conditioning operator can be approximated to arbitrary accuracy by neural operators. Our proof relies on new results establishing continuity of the conditioning operator over suitable classes of densities. Finally, we learn the conditioning map for a class of Gaussian mixtures using neural operators, illustrating the promise of our framework. This work provides the theoretical underpinnings for general-purpose, amortized methods for probabilistic conditioning, such as foundation models for Bayesian inference.

ai.tldr: 本文提出将概率条件化问题视为一个算子学习问题，即学习一个从联合密度到条件密度的映射，并通过神经算子实现任意精度的近似。
ai.motivation: 传统方法针对固定联合分布学习条件分布，缺乏通用性。本文旨在开发一种可泛化的、摊销式的条件化方法，为贝叶斯推理的基础模型奠定理论基础。
ai.method: 将条件化操作定义为从联合密度空间到条件密度空间的算子，证明该算子在适当密度类上的连续性，并利用神经算子进行近似。
ai.result: 理论上证明了神经算子可以任意精度逼近条件化算子，并在高斯混合模型上进行了实验验证。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种新的视角来理解概率条件化，并展示了神经算子在该问题上的潜力，有助于开发更通用的贝叶斯推理工具。

idea_spark.idea: 可以尝试将条件化算子与扩散模型或流匹配结合，实现更高效的条件生成。
idea_spark.risk: 理论保证依赖于特定的密度类假设，实际应用中密度分布可能超出该范围，影响近似精度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受神经算子在其他PDE问题中成功应用的启发，将其引入概率建模领域。

en.tldr: This paper proposes to view probabilistic conditioning as an operator learning problem, learning a mapping from joint densities to conditional densities, and approximating it to arbitrary accuracy via neural operators.
en.motivation: Traditional methods learn conditional distributions for a fixed joint distribution, lacking generality. This work aims to develop a generalizable, amortized conditioning method, laying theoretical foundations for foundation models for Bayesian inference.
en.method: Define the conditioning operation as an operator from the space of joint densities to conditional densities, prove its continuity over suitable density classes, and approximate it using neural operators.
en.result: Theoretically prove that neural operators can approximate the conditioning operator to arbitrary accuracy, and validate on Gaussian mixture models.
en.help_to_user: Provides a novel perspective on probabilistic conditioning and demonstrates the potential of neural operators, aiding the development of more general Bayesian inference tools.

idea_spark.idea: Consider combining the conditioning operator with diffusion models or flow matching for more efficient conditional generation.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical guarantees rely on specific density class assumptions; real-world densities may fall outside this range, affecting approximation accuracy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the successful application of neural operators in other PDE problems, introducing them into probabilistic modeling.

papers.title: Physics-Based Flow Matching for Full-Field Prediction of Silicon Photonic Devices
papers.summary: Designing photonic integrated circuits requires accurate electromagnetic field simulations, which remain computationally expensive even for simple device geometries. We present PIC-Flow, a generative neural surrogate that predicts electromagnetic field distributions for photonic devices given their geometry and operating wavelength as an alternative to costly finite-difference time-domain (FDTD) simulations. Our approach combines three key ideas: (i) conditional flow matching as the generative framework, learning a velocity field that transports Gaussian noise to physically valid field solutions; (ii) a real-valued U-Net operating on split real and imaginary field channels; and (iii) physics-constrained training through a Helmholtz residual loss enforcing $\nabla^2 E_z + k_0^2 \varepsilon E_z = 0$. We introduce an interface-aware masking scheme for the Helmholtz residual that excludes dielectric boundary pixels where finite-difference stencil errors dominate, yielding a physically meaningful compliance metric. The data set consists of 22,500 ground-truth FDTD simulations split evenly between multimode interferometers, Y-branches, and directional couplers at $λ=1.55\,μ$m in an 80/10/10 split between training, validation, and test sets. We evaluate ablations on the network against the held out test devices and also show that the model generalizes to held out device classes such as S-bends, tapers, and cascaded Y-branches. Rather than a drop-in replacement for FDTD, this work establishes a foundation that, with broader data coverage, more compute, and further training optimization, could scale toward broadband, device-agnostic field prediction with dramatically improved runtime for rapid design-space exploration of complex photonic devices and circuits.

ai.tldr: 提出PIC-Flow，一种基于条件流匹配的生成式神经替代模型，用于预测硅光子器件的电磁场分布，替代昂贵的FDTD仿真。
ai.motivation: 光子集成电路设计需要大量电磁场仿真，传统FDTD方法计算成本高，即使简单几何结构也耗时。
ai.method: 结合条件流匹配、实值U-Net（分离实部和虚部通道）以及亥姆霍兹残差损失（带界面感知掩码）进行物理约束训练。
ai.result: 在22,500个FDTD仿真数据集上训练，对多模干涉仪、Y分支和定向耦合器测试集表现良好，并泛化到未见器件类（如S弯、锥形、级联Y分支）。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速探索光子器件设计空间，无需每次运行FDTD仿真。

idea_spark.idea: 将流匹配与物理残差结合，并引入界面掩码处理边界不连续性，为物理约束生成模型提供新思路。
idea_spark.risk: 当前仅支持单波长，泛化到宽带需更多数据和计算资源；界面掩码可能忽略部分物理信息。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将生成模型与偏微分方程残差结合，用于其他计算密集的物理仿真替代。

en.tldr: PIC-Flow is a generative neural surrogate using conditional flow matching to predict electromagnetic field distributions for silicon photonic devices, offering an alternative to costly FDTD simulations.
en.motivation: Designing photonic integrated circuits requires accurate but computationally expensive electromagnetic field simulations, even for simple geometries.
en.method: Combines conditional flow matching, a real-valued U-Net operating on split real/imaginary field channels, and a Helmholtz residual loss with an interface-aware masking scheme for physics-constrained training.
en.result: Trained on 22,500 FDTD simulations, the model performs well on test devices (multimode interferometers, Y-branches, directional couplers) and generalizes to unseen classes like S-bends, tapers, and cascaded Y-branches.
en.help_to_user: Enables rapid design-space exploration of photonic devices without running FDTD simulations each time.

idea_spark.idea: Combining flow matching with a physics residual and an interface mask to handle boundary discontinuities offers a novel way to incorporate physical constraints in generative models.
idea_spark.risk: Currently limited to single-wavelength operation; scaling to broadband requires more data and compute; the interface mask may discard some physical information.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining generative models with PDE residuals for surrogate modeling in other computationally intensive physics simulations.

papers.title: LLM-Guided Open Hypothesis Learning from Autonomous Scanning Probe Microscopy Experiments
papers.summary: Autonomous experimentation has transformed microscopy and materials discovery by enabling closed-loop optimization including imaging and spectroscopy tuning, strucutre property relationship discovery, and exploration of combinatorial libraries. However, most current workflows remain limited to selecting measurements within fixed objective or hypothesis spaces, rather than generating new physical models from experimental data. Here, we introduce an open hypothesis-learning framework that combines symbolic regression with large-language-model-based physical evaluation and implement it for autonomous scanning probe microscopy. Symbolic regression generates candidate analytical relationships directly from sparse measurements, while the language-model evaluator ranks these candidates according to physical plausibility, scaling behavior, and consistency with known mechanisms. We demonstrate the approach on autonomous piezoresponse force microscopy measurements of ferroelectric domain switching in a PZT thin film. Starting from five seed measurements, the workflow evolves from physically incomplete candidate expressions toward interpretable voltage-time growth laws consistent with kinetic domain-wall motion. This work extends autonomous microscopy from closed-loop optimization toward open hypothesis discovery, where candidate physical laws emerge from the experiment itself rather than being specified in advance. More broadly, the framework establishes a route for integrating symbolic regression, physical reasoning, and adaptive experimentation into hierarchical autonomous scientific workflows.

ai.tldr: 提出一种结合符号回归与大语言模型的开源假设学习框架，用于自主扫描探针显微镜实验，从稀疏测量中生成并评估物理定律。
ai.motivation: 现有自主实验局限于固定目标或假设空间，无法从数据中生成新的物理模型。
ai.method: 符号回归从稀疏测量中生成候选解析关系，大语言模型根据物理合理性、标度行为及已知机制进行排序。
ai.result: 在PZT薄膜铁电畴切换实验中，从5个种子测量出发，演化出符合动力学畴壁运动的电压-时间生长定律。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何将符号回归与LLM结合，实现从数据中自动发现可解释物理定律的自主实验框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将符号回归与LLM物理推理结合，使自主实验从闭环优化升级为开放假设发现。
idea_spark.risk: LLM评估可能引入偏见或遗漏非标准但正确的物理关系；符号回归对噪声敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将物理推理与数据驱动方法深度融合，构建层次化自主科学工作流。

idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将物理推理与数据驱动方法深度融合，构建层次化自主科学工作流。

en.tldr: Proposes an open hypothesis-learning framework combining symbolic regression with large language models for autonomous scanning probe microscopy, generating and evaluating physical laws from sparse measurements.
en.motivation: Current autonomous experiments are limited to fixed objective or hypothesis spaces and cannot generate new physical models from data.
en.method: Symbolic regression generates candidate analytical relationships from sparse measurements; a language model ranks them by physical plausibility, scaling behavior, and consistency with known mechanisms.
en.result: In ferroelectric domain switching experiments on PZT thin films, starting from five seed measurements, the workflow evolves voltage-time growth laws consistent with kinetic domain-wall motion.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to combine symbolic regression with LLMs to create an autonomous experimental framework that discovers interpretable physical laws from data.

idea_spark.idea: Combining symbolic regression with LLM-based physical reasoning upgrades autonomous experiments from closed-loop optimization to open hypothesis discovery.
idea_spark.risk: LLM evaluation may introduce bias or miss non-standard but correct physical relationships; symbolic regression is sensitive to noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires deep integration of physical reasoning with data-driven methods to build hierarchical autonomous scientific workflows.

papers.title: Physical Simulators as Do-Operators: Causal Discovery under Latent Confounders for AI-for-Science
papers.summary: Existing interventional causal discovery methods -- IGSP, DCDI, ENCO -- assume causal sufficiency (no latent confounders) and rely on virtual interventions in synthetic simulators. In AI-for-Science settings such as molecular design and materials science, latent confounders are ubiquitous and real interventions (e.g., physics-based simulations) require hours to days per data point. We propose CFM-SD (Causal Flow Matching with Simulation Data), which uses first-principles physical simulators as do-operators in Pearl's interventional calculus to simultaneously handle latent confounders and real interventional data. Theoretically, $d$-variable causal structure is identifiable with $O(d)$ single-variable interventions -- the minimum under physical realizability constraints. In Intrinsic Evaluation on synthetic data ($γ=0.2$--$0.8$), CFM-SD achieves average F1$=0.800$ vs. F1$=0.127$--$0.562$ for all baselines. In Extrinsic Evaluation on real scientific data, CFM-SD achieves 57--58\% bias reduction in molecular toxicity prediction and battery electrolyte optimization, demonstrating practical value beyond synthetic benchmarks.

ai.tldr: 提出CFM-SD方法，利用物理模拟器作为do算子，在存在潜在混杂因素的情况下进行因果发现，实现最小干预次数下的结构可识别性。
ai.motivation: 现有因果发现方法假设因果充分性，无法处理AI-for-Science中普遍存在的潜在混杂因素，且真实干预成本高昂。
ai.method: 将第一性原理物理模拟器作为Pearl干预演算中的do算子，结合因果流匹配同时处理潜在混杂和真实干预数据。
ai.result: 在合成数据上F1=0.800，优于基线（0.127-0.562）；在真实科学数据上，分子毒性预测和电池电解质优化偏差降低57-58%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员在物理模拟数据中识别因果关系，尤其适用于分子设计和材料科学等存在潜在混杂因素的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将物理模拟器视为可干预的do算子，结合流匹配实现高效因果发现。
idea_spark.risk: 模拟器精度不足可能导致因果推断偏差；理论保证依赖于干预的物理可实现性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发利用现有科学模拟器作为干预工具，降低因果发现对真实实验的依赖。

en.tldr: Proposes CFM-SD, using physical simulators as do-operators for causal discovery under latent confounders, achieving identifiability with minimal interventions.
en.motivation: Existing interventional causal discovery methods assume causal sufficiency, failing in AI-for-Science settings where latent confounders are common and real interventions are costly.
en.method: Employs first-principles physical simulators as do-operators in Pearl's interventional calculus, combined with causal flow matching to handle both latent confounders and real interventional data.
en.result: Achieves F1=0.800 on synthetic data vs. baselines (0.127-0.562); reduces bias by 57-58% in molecular toxicity prediction and battery electrolyte optimization on real data.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers discover causal relationships from physical simulation data, especially in molecular design and materials science with latent confounders.

idea_spark.idea: Treat physical simulators as do-operators for intervention, combined with flow matching for efficient causal discovery.
idea_spark.risk: Inaccurate simulators may bias causal inference; theoretical guarantees depend on physical realizability of interventions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires leveraging existing scientific simulators as intervention tools to reduce reliance on real experiments for causal discovery.

papers.title: Geometric Kolmogorov--Arnold Network (GeoKAN)
papers.summary: We introduce Geometric Kolmogorov--Arnold Networks (GeoKANs), a family of geometry-aware KAN-type models in which approximation is carried out in learned, geometry-adapted coordinates rather than in fixed Euclidean input coordinates. GeoKAN achieves this by learning a diagonal Riemannian metric that warps the input before basis expansion and feature mixing. The learned metric provides a geometric inductive bias through local length scaling and volume distortion, and in physics-informed settings it also affects the differential structure seen by the model. Within this framework, we develop three main variants, namely GeoKAN-NNMetric, GeoKAN-$γ$, and LM-KAN. For LM-KAN, we further consider three basis-specific versions, LM-KAN-RBF, LM-KAN-Wav, and LM-KAN-Fourier. These variants allow us to study geometry-aware KAN models both as general function approximators and as surrogates in physics-informed learning. By stretching regions with rapid variation and compressing smoother regions, GeoKAN reallocates representational resolution in a task-dependent manner, allowing the model to place capacity where it is most needed. As a result, GeoKAN is well suited to sharp, stiff, localized, and strongly non-uniform regimes arising in scientific machine learning and differential-equation problems.

ai.tldr: 提出几何KAN系列模型，通过学习对角黎曼度量扭曲输入坐标，使网络在几何自适应坐标下逼近函数，适用于尖锐、刚硬、局部化及强非均匀的科学计算问题。
ai.motivation: 标准KAN在固定欧氏坐标下逼近，难以高效处理具有尖锐变化或局部化结构的科学问题。
ai.method: 学习一个对角黎曼度量，对输入进行几何扭曲，再执行基展开与特征混合；开发了GeoKAN-NNMetric、GeoKAN-γ和LM-KAN（含RBF、Wav、Fourier三种基）三个变体。
ai.result: 模型能根据任务自动拉伸快速变化区域、压缩平滑区域，重新分配表示分辨率，从而在科学机器学习和微分方程问题中表现更优。
ai.help_to_user: 若您处理具有尖锐梯度、局部化特征或强非均匀性的物理/工程问题，GeoKAN可提供几何先验以提升逼近效率。

idea_spark.idea: 结合可逆神经网络学习保体积的坐标变换，使度量学习更稳定且可解释。
idea_spark.risk: 学习额外度量可能增加过拟合风险，尤其在数据稀疏时；需设计正则化策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受黎曼几何中度量张量控制局部长度与体积的启发，将几何先验直接嵌入网络结构。

en.tldr: Introduces Geometric Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (GeoKANs) that learn a diagonal Riemannian metric to warp input coordinates, enabling function approximation in geometry-adapted coordinates, particularly effective for sharp, stiff, localized, and strongly non-uniform regimes in scientific machine learning.
en.motivation: Standard KAN operates in fixed Euclidean coordinates, which is inefficient for problems with sharp variations or localized structures common in scientific computing.
en.method: Learns a diagonal Riemannian metric to geometrically warp the input before basis expansion and feature mixing; develops three main variants: GeoKAN-NNMetric, GeoKAN-γ, and LM-KAN (with RBF, Wav, Fourier bases).
en.result: The model automatically stretches regions of rapid variation and compresses smoother regions, reallocating representational resolution in a task-dependent manner, leading to improved performance on scientific machine learning and differential equation problems.
en.help_to_user: If you are dealing with physical/engineering problems featuring sharp gradients, localized features, or strong non-uniformity, GeoKAN provides a geometric inductive bias to enhance approximation efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Combine with invertible neural networks to learn volume-preserving coordinate transformations, making metric learning more stable and interpretable.
idea_spark.risk: Learning an additional metric may increase overfitting risk, especially with sparse data; regularization strategies are needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Riemannian geometry where the metric tensor controls local length and volume, embedding geometric priors directly into the network structure.

papers.title: Physics-Informed Reduced-Order Operator Learning for Hyperelasticity in Continuum Micromechanics
papers.summary: Physics-informed operator learning is an attractive candidate for surrogate modeling of microstructures, especially in multiscale finite-element simulations. Its practical use, however, is often limited by the high cost of loss evaluation. We address this bottleneck by combining the Equilibrium Neural Operator (EquiNO) with the QR-based discrete empirical interpolation method (Q-DEIM). EquiNO learns only the modal coefficients of reduced displacement-fluctuation and first Piola-Kirchhoff stress representations built from periodic and divergence-free bases, thereby enforcing periodicity and mechanical equilibrium by construction. Q-DEIM then identifies a small set of spatial points through a column-pivoted QR factorization of the stress basis and restricts constitutive evaluations during training to these points alone. This makes full-batch second-order optimization practical for three-dimensional representative volume elements (RVEs). Homogenized first Piola-Kirchhoff stresses are recovered directly from the offline-averaged reduced stress modes, without the need to reconstruct the full stress field at inference time. We validate the framework on two three-dimensional finite-strain hyperelastic RVEs. Q-DEIM reduces the per-step training cost by roughly three orders of magnitude relative to full-field loss evaluation, while reduced homogenization achieves speed-up factors of order $10^3$ to $10^4$ over direct full-field computations. Despite relying on only a small number of offline snapshot loading paths for basis construction, the method accurately interpolates and extrapolates both microscopic stress fields and homogenized stresses, with prediction quality improving systematically as more snapshots are added.

ai.tldr: 提出结合平衡神经算子与QR离散经验插值法的物理信息算子学习框架，大幅降低超弹性微结构代理模型的训练成本，实现高效均质化。
ai.motivation: 物理信息算子学习在微结构代理建模中具有潜力，但损失评估的高计算成本限制了其实际应用。
ai.method: 采用EquiNO学习约化位移波动和应力模态系数，通过周期和无散基强制满足周期性和力学平衡；利用Q-DEIM通过列主元QR分解选取少量空间点，仅在这些点上进行本构评估，实现全批量二阶优化。
ai.result: 在三维有限应变超弹性RVE上，Q-DEIM将每步训练成本降低约三个数量级，约化均质化加速因子达10^3至10^4倍，且能准确插值和外推微观应力场与均质化应力。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、可扩展的物理信息算子学习方法，适用于多尺度有限元模拟中的微结构代理建模。

idea_spark.idea: 将Q-DEIM与更复杂的物理约束（如不可压缩性或塑性）结合，或用于在线自适应采样以处理非线性演化。
idea_spark.risk: Q-DEIM基的构建依赖离线快照，若训练分布外变化剧烈，可能降低插值精度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用矩阵分解和稀疏采样加速物理信息学习，启发在其他计算密集型科学计算任务中采用类似策略。

idea_spark.inspiration: 利用矩阵分解和稀疏采样加速物理信息学习，启发在其他计算密集型科学计算任务中采用类似策略。

en.tldr: A physics-informed operator learning framework combining Equilibrium Neural Operator with QR-based discrete empirical interpolation method drastically reduces training cost for hyperelastic microstructure surrogate modeling and enables efficient homogenization.
en.motivation: Physics-informed operator learning is promising for surrogate modeling of microstructures, but high loss evaluation cost limits practical use.
en.method: EquiNO learns reduced modal coefficients of displacement fluctuations and stresses using periodic and divergence-free bases to enforce periodicity and equilibrium; Q-DEIM selects a small set of spatial points via column-pivoted QR factorization of the stress basis, restricting constitutive evaluations to those points for full-batch second-order optimization.
en.result: On 3D finite-strain hyperelastic RVEs, Q-DEIM reduces per-step training cost by about three orders of magnitude, and reduced homogenization achieves speed-ups of 10^3 to 10^4 over full-field computations, with accurate interpolation and extrapolation of both microscopic stress fields and homogenized stresses.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and scalable physics-informed operator learning method for microstructure surrogate modeling in multiscale finite-element simulations.

idea_spark.idea: Combine Q-DEIM with more complex physical constraints (e.g., incompressibility or plasticity) or use online adaptive sampling to handle nonlinear evolution.
idea_spark.risk: Q-DEIM basis construction relies on offline snapshots; significant out-of-distribution changes may degrade interpolation accuracy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using matrix factorization and sparse sampling to accelerate physics-informed learning inspires similar strategies in other computationally intensive scientific computing tasks.

papers.title: Physics-based Digital Twins for Integrated Thermal Energy Systems Using Active Learning
papers.summary: Real-time supervisory control of thermal energy distribution systems requires digital twins that are accurate, interpretable, and uncertainty-aware, yet remain data and computationally efficient. High-fidelity simulations alone are costly, while purely data-driven surrogates often lack robustness. To address these challenges, this work proposes an active learning (AL) framework that couples system-level Modelica simulations with four simpler physics-informed and data-driven surrogate modeling approaches: deterministic Sparse Identification of Nonlinear Dynamics with Control (SINDyC), its probabilistic multivariate-Gaussian extension (MvG-SINDyC), feedforward neural network (FNN), and gated recurrent unit (GRU) network. Tailored to each surrogate, model-specific AL query strategies are employed, including Mahalanobis-distance sampling in coefficient space for MvG-SINDyC and error-based sampling in prediction space for SINDyC, FNN, and GRU, allowing the learning process to prioritize dynamically informative trajectories.   The proposed approach is demonstrated on the glycol heat exchanger (GHX) subsystem of the Thermal Energy Distribution System (TEDS) at Idaho National Laboratory. Across key GHX outputs--the bypass mass flow rate $\dot{m}_{\mathrm{GHX}}$ and heat transfer rate $Q_{\mathrm{GHX}}$-the AL framework achieves comparable predictive accuracy using as few as one-fifth of the simulation trajectories required by random sampling. Among the evaluated surrogates, the GRU achieves the highest predictive fidelity, while SINDyC remains the most computationally efficient and interpretable. The probabilistic MvG-SINDyC surrogate further enables uncertainty quantification and exhibits the largest computational gains under AL.

ai.tldr: 提出一种主动学习框架，将系统级Modelica仿真与多种代理模型结合，用于热能量分布系统的数字孪生，实现高效、可解释且具有不确定性感知的实时监控。
ai.motivation: 高保真仿真成本高，纯数据驱动代理缺乏鲁棒性，需要准确、可解释且不确定性感知的数字孪生。
ai.method: 采用主动学习框架，耦合Modelica仿真与SINDyC、MvG-SINDyC、FNN和GRU四种代理模型，并针对每种模型设计特定的查询策略。
ai.result: 在GHX子系统上，主动学习仅需随机采样五分之一的轨迹即可达到相当精度；GRU预测精度最高，SINDyC计算效率最高且可解释，MvG-SINDyC实现不确定性量化。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用主动学习构建高效、可解释的数字孪生，用于热能量系统的实时控制。

idea_spark.idea: 结合物理信息代理模型与主动学习，可大幅减少所需仿真数据，同时保持模型可解释性和不确定性量化能力。
idea_spark.risk: 代理模型的选择和查询策略的适配可能影响泛化性能，需针对不同系统调优。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将主动学习与物理信息模型结合，用于数据稀缺场景下的系统建模与控制。

en.tldr: Proposes an active learning framework coupling system-level Modelica simulations with multiple surrogate models for digital twins of thermal energy distribution systems, achieving accurate, interpretable, and uncertainty-aware real-time control.
en.motivation: High-fidelity simulations are costly, and purely data-driven surrogates lack robustness; digital twins need to be accurate, interpretable, and uncertainty-aware.
en.method: Uses an active learning framework coupling Modelica simulations with four surrogate models (SINDyC, MvG-SINDyC, FNN, GRU) and model-specific query strategies.
en.result: On the GHX subsystem, active learning achieves comparable accuracy with only one-fifth of the simulation trajectories needed by random sampling; GRU yields highest predictive fidelity, SINDyC is most efficient and interpretable, and MvG-SINDyC enables uncertainty quantification.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to build efficient, interpretable digital twins for real-time control of thermal energy systems using active learning.

idea_spark.idea: Combining physics-informed surrogate models with active learning can significantly reduce required simulation data while maintaining interpretability and uncertainty quantification.
idea_spark.risk: Choice of surrogate model and query strategy may affect generalization performance and requires tuning for different systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining active learning with physics-informed models for system modeling and control in data-scarce scenarios.

papers.title: QuadNorm: Resolution-Robust Normalization for Neural Operators
papers.summary: Normalization layers in neural operators usually compute statistics by uniformly averaging discrete grid values, making the normalization itself discretization-dependent and thereby a source of transfer error across different resolutions or meshes. To enable discretization robustness, we introduce a quadrature normalization family that replaces existing uniform averaging in normalization layers with numerical quadrature: QuadNorm and BlendQuadNorm. On endpoint-inclusive uniform grids, the proposed quadrature moments are $O(h^2)$-consistent across discretizations, meaning that their cross-resolution mismatch decays quadratically with grid spacing. A transfer-error bound then predicts how normalization-induced mismatch scales with both the resolution gap and network depth. The experiments show the same gap- and depth-scaling trends predicted by the transfer-error bound. On Darcy, QuadNorm delivers the best cross-resolution performance at every tested target resolution from $64^2$ to $256^2$; on real-data benchmarks, Transolver with QuadNorm achieves nearly resolution-invariant transfer. The largest gains appear on nonperiodic PDEs and nonspectral architectures, where native-resolution improvements also emerge. We also validate BlendQuadNorm, which stays close to LayerNorm behavior and serves as a conservative default for periodic FNO settings. These results identify normalization as a previously overlooked source of resolution dependence in neural operators.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于数值积分的归一化方法QuadNorm，使神经算子在跨分辨率/网格时保持稳定，减少传递误差。
ai.motivation: 现有归一化层在神经算子中均匀平均离散网格值，导致归一化本身依赖离散化，成为跨分辨率传递误差的来源。
ai.method: 用数值积分替换归一化层中的均匀平均，设计QuadNorm和BlendQuadNorm，保证在端点包含均匀网格上O(h²)一致性。
ai.result: 在Darcy流问题中，QuadNorm在所有目标分辨率下取得最佳跨分辨率性能；在真实数据基准上，Transolver+QuadNorm实现几乎分辨率不变的传递。
ai.help_to_user: 若您处理不同分辨率或网格的PDE问题，QuadNorm可显著降低归一化引起的传递误差，提升模型泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 设计自适应积分权重，根据局部网格密度动态调整归一化统计量，进一步减少非均匀网格上的误差。
idea_spark.risk: 数值积分可能增加计算开销，且对非周期边界条件需谨慎处理积分权重。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从数值分析中的积分误差分析出发，将离散化误差理论引入深度学习归一化设计。

en.tldr: Proposes a quadrature-based normalization family (QuadNorm and BlendQuadNorm) that makes neural operators robust to resolution changes by replacing uniform averaging with numerical quadrature.
en.motivation: Existing normalization layers in neural operators uniformly average discrete grid values, making normalization itself discretization-dependent and a source of transfer error across resolutions.
en.method: Replaces uniform averaging in normalization layers with numerical quadrature, achieving O(h²) consistency on endpoint-inclusive uniform grids.
en.result: QuadNorm achieves best cross-resolution performance on Darcy flow at all tested resolutions; on real-data benchmarks, Transolver with QuadNorm achieves nearly resolution-invariant transfer.
en.help_to_user: If you work with PDEs across different resolutions or meshes, QuadNorm can significantly reduce normalization-induced transfer errors and improve model generalization.

idea_spark.idea: Design adaptive quadrature weights that adjust normalization statistics based on local grid density to further reduce errors on non-uniform grids.
idea_spark.risk: Quadrature may increase computational cost, and careful handling of integration weights is needed for non-periodic boundaries.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrow error analysis from numerical integration to inform normalization design in deep learning.

papers.title: Dirichlet problem for Lane-Emden type equations with several sublinear terms
papers.summary: We prove the existence, uniqueness, and sharp bilateral pointwise estimates for positive bounded solutions to the Lane--Emden type problem \[ \begin{cases}   L u = \sum\limits_{i=1}^{m}σ_{i} u^{q_{i}}+σ_0,   \quad u\geq0 & \text{in } Ω,   \liminf \limits_{x \rightarrow y} u(x) = f(y), & y \in \partial^\inftyΩ, \end{cases} \] where $0 < q_{i} < 1$. Here $Lu = - \text{div}(A \nabla u)$ is a uniformly elliptic operator with bounded coefficients, $σ_{i}$ is a nonnegative locally finite Borel measure on an $A$-regular domain $Ω\subset \mathbb{R}^n$ which possesses a positive Green function associated with $L$, and $f$ is a nonnegative continuous function on the boundary $\partial^\inftyΩ$. An analogous result for positive continuous solutions to the problem is also illustrated.   Our method can be adapted to address related sublinear problems with zero boundary conditions involving the fractional Laplace operator $(-Δ)^α$ for $0< α< n/2$, in place of $L$, in $\mathbb{R}^n$ as well.

ai.tldr: 研究了具有多个次线性项的Lane-Emden型方程的Dirichlet问题，证明了正有界解的存在性、唯一性和尖锐双边逐点估计。
ai.motivation: 推广Lane-Emden型方程到多个次线性项的情形，并建立解的存在性、唯一性和精确估计。
ai.method: 利用Green函数和测度理论，结合椭圆算子的正则性，通过构造上下解和比较原理得到结果。
ai.result: 获得了正有界解的存在性、唯一性以及尖锐的双边逐点估计，并推广到分数阶Laplace算子情形。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理含多个次线性项的椭圆型方程提供了理论工具和估计方法。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试将Green函数方法与非线性项的多尺度分解结合，处理更一般的非线性结构。
idea_spark.risk: 当非线性项包含超线性或奇异项时，现有估计可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从多个次线性项的叠加中，启发对复杂非线性系统的解结构进行分解研究。

en.tldr: We prove existence, uniqueness, and sharp bilateral pointwise estimates for positive bounded solutions to the Dirichlet problem for Lane-Emden type equations with several sublinear terms.
en.motivation: To extend the Lane-Emden type equation to multiple sublinear terms and establish existence, uniqueness, and precise estimates.
en.method: Using Green functions and measure theory, combined with regularity of elliptic operators, via construction of sub- and super-solutions and comparison principles.
en.result: Existence, uniqueness, and sharp bilateral pointwise estimates for positive bounded solutions are obtained, with extension to fractional Laplace operators.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical tools and estimation methods for elliptic equations with multiple sublinear terms.

idea_spark.idea: Combine Green function methods with multiscale decomposition of nonlinear terms to handle more general nonlinear structures.
idea_spark.risk: Estimates may fail when nonlinear terms include superlinear or singular components.
idea_spark.inspiration: The superposition of multiple sublinear terms inspires a decomposition approach to study solution structures of complex nonlinear systems.

papers.title: Essential Role of Extrinsic Noise in Models of E. coli Division Control
papers.summary: Our understanding of cell division control in bacteria still relies largely on interpreting correlations between phenomenological variables, with limited connection to the underlying molecular mechanisms.   Here, we analytically solve a stochastic threshold-accumulation model in which a size-dependent divisor protein triggers division upon reaching a noisy, autocorrelated threshold, quantifying within a unified framework the combined effects of intrinsic and extrinsic noise and key mechanistic parameters such as protein reset and threshold memory. We show that incorporating these elements yields behavior far richer than the commonly assumed adder, spanning a continuum of division strategies from timer to sizer while modulating size fluctuations in a nontrivial fashion. Comparison with single-cell E. coli data shows that extrinsic noise and additional mechanistic ingredients are required to account for the observed size fluctuations. The adder emerges when threshold correlations balance protein reset, generalizing the hypothesis that full reset is necessary to maintain adder control.   Our results establish a unified analytical framework linking stochastic molecular processes to emergent division laws, to be used in more complex bacterial cell-cycle models.

ai.tldr: 本研究通过解析随机阈值累积模型，揭示了外在噪声在大肠杆菌分裂控制中的关键作用，并统一解释了从计时器到尺寸器的连续分裂策略。
ai.motivation: 现有细菌分裂控制研究多依赖现象学变量间的相关性，缺乏与分子机制的连接。
ai.method: 解析求解一个随机阈值累积模型，该模型包含尺寸依赖的分裂蛋白、噪声自相关阈值以及蛋白重置和阈值记忆等机制参数。
ai.result: 模型预测了比常见加法器更丰富的分裂行为，包括连续的分裂策略谱系，且与单细胞大肠杆菌数据对比表明外在噪声和额外机制是解释尺寸波动所必需的。
ai.help_to_user: 该模型为连接随机分子过程与涌现分裂法则提供了统一分析框架，可用于更复杂的细菌细胞周期模型。

idea_spark.idea: 结合实验测量蛋白重置和阈值记忆参数，验证模型对分裂策略连续谱的预测。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设可能过于简化，实际分子机制可能包含更多反馈或空间异质性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 外在噪声与内在噪声的平衡可能是一种普适的细胞调控策略，值得在其他生物系统中探索。

en.tldr: This study analytically solves a stochastic threshold-accumulation model, revealing the essential role of extrinsic noise in E. coli division control and unifying a continuum of division strategies from timer to sizer.
en.motivation: Current understanding of bacterial division control relies on correlations between phenomenological variables with limited connection to molecular mechanisms.
en.method: Analytical solution of a stochastic threshold-accumulation model incorporating a size-dependent divisor protein, autocorrelated noisy threshold, and mechanistic parameters such as protein reset and threshold memory.

idea_spark.idea: Combine experimental measurements of protein reset and threshold memory to validate the predicted continuum of division strategies.
idea_spark.risk: Model assumptions may oversimplify actual molecular mechanisms, which could involve additional feedback or spatial heterogeneity.
idea_spark.inspiration: The balance between extrinsic and intrinsic noise may be a universal cellular regulatory strategy worth exploring in other systems.

papers.title: Predictive-Switching Control of Stochastic Gene Regulatory Networks: A Contractive PIDE Framework
papers.summary: This paper develops a predictive switching control algorithm for stochastic gene regulatory networks described by a Partial Integro-Differential Equation (PIDE) model, which enables direct shape control of the probability density function. Control inputs are selected from a finite candidate set to minimize a prescribed cost functional. A hybrid framework is proposed for scalability in higher-dimensional systems, using neural networks to approximate the control policy. A central theoretical contribution is a contraction-based analysis of the closed-loop PIDE dynamics. The paper establishes $L^ 1$-contractivity under the proposed control scheme, yielding formal stability guarantees and showing that the evolution of the probability density becomes progressively independent of the initial condition. Moreover, under strictly positive leakage terms, exponential convergence is obtained. The effectiveness and flexibility of the approach, together with the theoretical contractivity results, are illustrated through numerical simulations on three representative examples of increasing dimensionality.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于收缩性分析的预测切换控制算法，用于随机基因调控网络的概率密度函数形状控制。
ai.motivation: 现有方法难以直接控制随机基因调控网络的概率密度函数形状，且缺乏稳定性保证。
ai.method: 构建偏积分微分方程模型，从有限候选集中选择控制输入以最小化代价泛函，采用神经网络近似策略，并基于L1收缩性分析证明稳定性。
ai.result: 证明了闭环系统的L1收缩性和指数收敛性，通过三个维度递增的数值示例验证了方法的有效性和灵活性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可扩展的、具有稳定性保证的随机基因调控网络概率密度控制方法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用神经网络近似控制策略，结合收缩性理论，实现高维系统的可扩展控制。
idea_spark.risk: 神经网络近似可能引入误差，影响理论收缩性保证的严格性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将PIDE模型与切换控制结合，为复杂生物系统的概率密度控制开辟新途径。

idea_spark.risk: 神经网络近似可能引入误差，影响理论收缩性保证的严格性。

en.tldr: A predictive switching control algorithm with contraction-based analysis is proposed for shape control of the probability density function in stochastic gene regulatory networks.
en.motivation: Existing methods lack direct shape control of the probability density function and formal stability guarantees for stochastic gene regulatory networks.
en.method: A PIDE model is used; control inputs are selected from a finite set to minimize a cost functional, with neural network approximation for scalability. L1-contractivity and exponential convergence are proven.
en.result: The closed-loop system achieves L1-contractivity and exponential convergence, demonstrated on three numerical examples of increasing dimensionality.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable, stability-guaranteed method for probability density control in stochastic gene regulatory networks.

idea_spark.idea: Combining neural network approximation with contraction theory enables scalable control for high-dimensional systems.
idea_spark.risk: Neural network approximation may introduce errors that compromise the strictness of theoretical contraction guarantees.
idea_spark.inspiration: Integrating PIDE models with switching control opens new avenues for probability density control in complex biological systems.

papers.title: Flow Matching for Count Data
papers.summary: High-dimensional count data arise in applications such as single-cell RNA sequencing and neural spike trains, where mapping between distributions across successive batches or time points form critical components of data analysis. The recent success of diffusion- and flow-based deep generative models for images, video, and text motivates extending these ideas to count-valued settings, but many existing methods either treat each count as a categorical state or transform counts into a continuous space, neither of which is natural or efficient when the count range is large. We propose count-FM, a flow-matching framework for count data based on a continuous-time birth-death process with local unit jumps. Count-FM learns marginal transitions efficiently in count space through simulation-free training of conditional transition rates, allowing transport between arbitrary count-distributed source and target populations. In simulation, count-FM achieves better sample quality than representative baselines while using substantially fewer parameters. We further apply count-FM to scRNA-seq and neural spike-train data for unconditional generation, transport, and conditional generation. Across these tasks, count-FM yields improved sample quality, greater modeling efficiency, and interpretable transport paths.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种基于连续时间生灭过程的流匹配框架count-FM，用于高维计数数据的分布映射和生成。
ai.motivation: 现有方法将计数视为类别状态或变换到连续空间，在大计数范围内不自然且低效。
ai.method: 利用局部单位跳跃的连续时间生灭过程，通过无模拟训练条件转移率来学习计数空间中的边际转移。
ai.result: 在模拟和真实数据（scRNA-seq、神经脉冲序列）上，count-FM以更少参数获得更好的样本质量和可解释的传输路径。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种直接处理计数数据的生成和迁移方法，无需离散化或连续化预处理。

idea_spark.idea: 利用条件转移率的无模拟训练，实现计数空间中的高效分布传输。
idea_spark.risk: 对高维稀疏计数数据，训练稳定性和计算效率可能面临挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将连续时间马尔可夫过程与流匹配结合，为离散数据生成建模提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes count-FM, a flow-matching framework based on a continuous-time birth-death process, for mapping and generating high-dimensional count data.
en.motivation: Existing methods treat counts as categorical states or transform them into continuous space, which is unnatural and inefficient for large count ranges.
en.method: Uses a continuous-time birth-death process with local unit jumps and learns conditional transition rates via simulation-free training to achieve marginal transport in count space.
en.result: On simulated and real data (scRNA-seq, neural spike trains), count-FM achieves better sample quality with fewer parameters and interpretable transport paths.
en.help_to_user: Provides a direct method for generation and transport of count data without discretization or continuous preprocessing.

idea_spark.idea: Leverages simulation-free training of conditional transition rates for efficient distribution transport in count space.
idea_spark.risk: Training stability and computational efficiency may be challenging for high-dimensional sparse count data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Integrating continuous-time Markov processes with flow matching offers a new paradigm for generative modeling of discrete data.

papers.title: On the Role of Strain and Vorticity in Numerical Integration Error for Flow Matching
papers.summary: Flow matching generates data by integrating a learned velocity field, where the number of integration steps (NFE) directly determines inference cost. We analyze which properties of the velocity field govern integration error by decomposing the velocity Jacobian into its symmetric part S (strain rate) and antisymmetric part Omega (vorticity). We prove that strain and vorticity play different roles: strain controls exponential error amplification through the logarithmic norm, while vorticity contributes only linearly to the local truncation error. We further show that the optimal transport velocity field is irrotational and has zero material derivative, implying second-order Euler accuracy; for exact displacement interpolation, the associated Lagrangian particle dynamics are integrated exactly by Euler. Motivated by this analysis, we study weighted Jacobian regularization with strain weight alpha and vorticity weight beta. Experiments on 2D synthetic data confirm the main theoretical predictions, showing up to 2.7x lower integration error at NFE=5. Preliminary CIFAR-10 experiments show consistent trends, with a lightweight fine-tuning procedure improving FID by 14 percent at NFE=10 while preserving high-NFE quality.

ai.tldr: 本文分析了流匹配中速度场的应变和涡度对数值积分误差的不同影响，并提出了加权雅可比正则化方法。
ai.motivation: 流匹配生成数据时积分步数直接影响推理成本，需要理解速度场哪些属性控制积分误差以降低计算开销。
ai.method: 将速度雅可比分解为对称应变率S和反对称涡度Ω，证明应变通过对数范数控制指数误差放大，涡度仅线性贡献局部截断误差；据此设计加权雅可比正则化（应变权重α、涡度权重β）。
ai.result: 二维合成数据实验验证理论预测，NFE=5时积分误差降低2.7倍；CIFAR-10上轻量微调使NFE=10时FID改善14%，且不损失高NFE质量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解流匹配中积分误差的来源，并提供一种轻量正则化策略以在较少步数下提升生成质量。

zh.tldr: 本文分析了流匹配中速度场的应变和涡度对数值积分误差的不同影响，并提出了加权雅可比正则化方法。
zh.motivation: 流匹配生成数据时积分步数直接影响推理成本，需要理解速度场哪些属性控制积分误差以降低计算开销。
zh.method: 将速度雅可比分解为对称应变率S和反对称涡度Ω，证明应变通过对数范数控制指数误差放大，涡度仅线性贡献局部截断误差；据此设计加权雅可比正则化（应变权重α、涡度权重β）。
zh.result: 二维合成数据实验验证理论预测，NFE=5时积分误差降低2.7倍；CIFAR-10上轻量微调使NFE=10时FID改善14%，且不损失高NFE质量。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解流匹配中积分误差的来源，并提供一种轻量正则化策略以在较少步数下提升生成质量。

en.tldr: This paper analyzes the distinct roles of strain and vorticity in the velocity field on numerical integration error for flow matching, and proposes weighted Jacobian regularization.
en.motivation: In flow matching, the number of integration steps directly determines inference cost, so understanding which properties of the velocity field control integration error is essential to reduce computational overhead.
en.method: Decompose the velocity Jacobian into symmetric strain rate S and antisymmetric vorticity Ω; prove that strain governs exponential error amplification via the logarithmic norm while vorticity contributes only linearly to local truncation error; then design weighted Jacobian regularization with strain weight α and vorticity weight β.
en.result: Experiments on 2D synthetic data confirm theoretical predictions, achieving up to 2.7× lower integration error at NFE=5; preliminary CIFAR-10 results show consistent trends, with a lightweight fine-tuning procedure improving FID by 14% at NFE=10 while preserving high-NFE quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the sources of integration error in flow matching and provides a lightweight regularization strategy to improve generation quality with fewer steps.

idea_spark.idea: Combine strain/vorticity decomposition with adaptive step-size integrators to dynamically adjust regularization strength for further error reduction.
idea_spark.risk: Regularization may introduce additional hyperparameter tuning cost, and its effect might be less pronounced on high-dimensional complex data than on synthetic data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Drawing from the physical meanings of strain and vorticity in fluid dynamics inspires a more refined geometric analysis of generative model dynamics.

papers.title: Debiased Counterfactual Generation via Flow Matching from Observations
papers.summary: Estimating counterfactual distributions under interventions is central to treatment risk assessment and counterfactual generation tasks. Existing approaches model the counterfactual distribution as a standalone generative target, without exploiting its relationship to the observational data. In this work, we show that under standard assumptions, observational and counterfactual outcome distributions are tightly linked: they have identical support and tail behavior, remain statistically close under weak confounding, and share any features of high-dimensional outcomes which are invariant to confounders. These properties motivate learning counterfactual distributions not from scratch, but via a deconfounding flow from the observational distribution. We formulate this problem via flow-matching and derive a semiparametrically efficient estimator based on a novel efficient influence function correction. We subsequently extend our estimator to target minimal-energy flows in high-dimensions, which we show can be especially simple targets between observational and counterfactual distributions. In experiments, deconfounding flows outperform existing debiased counterfactual distribution estimators, while also mitigating known failure modes of flow-based methods.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于流匹配的反事实分布生成方法，利用观测分布与反事实分布之间的内在联系，通过去混淆流实现高效估计。
ai.motivation: 现有方法将反事实分布视为独立生成目标，忽略了其与观测数据的紧密关系，导致效率低下。
ai.method: 在标准假设下证明观测与反事实分布具有相同支撑和尾部行为，并基于流匹配构建去混淆流，通过高效影响函数校正实现半参数有效估计。
ai.result: 实验表明去混淆流优于现有去偏反事实分布估计器，同时缓解了流方法的已知失效模式。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种更高效、更稳健的反事实生成方法，适用于治疗风险评估等因果推断任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用观测与目标分布的结构相似性，通过流匹配实现低成本的分布转换。
idea_spark.risk: 对强混淆或高维复杂分布，流匹配的稳定性仍需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受因果推断中反事实与观测分布关系的启发，将生成建模与因果去偏结合。

en.tldr: Proposes a debiased counterfactual generation method via flow matching, leveraging the tight link between observational and counterfactual distributions for efficient estimation.
en.motivation: Existing methods treat counterfactual distributions as standalone targets, ignoring their relationship with observational data, leading to inefficiency.
en.method: Under standard assumptions, shows observational and counterfactual distributions share support and tail behavior; formulates deconfounding flow via flow matching with efficient influence function correction for semiparametric efficiency.
en.result: Experiments show deconfounding flows outperform existing debiased estimators and mitigate known failure modes of flow-based methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more efficient and robust approach for counterfactual generation, useful for treatment risk assessment and causal inference.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage structural similarity between observational and target distributions for low-cost distribution transformation via flow matching.
idea_spark.risk: Stability of flow matching under strong confounding or high-dimensional complex distributions remains to be verified.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the causal inference insight linking counterfactual and observational distributions, combining generative modeling with causal debiasing.

papers.title: A Unified Measure-Theoretic View of Diffusion, Score-Based, and Flow Matching Generative Models
papers.summary: We survey continuous-time generative modeling methods based on transporting a simple reference distribution to a data distribution via stochastic or deterministic dynamics. We present a unified framework in which diffusion models, score-based generative models, and flow matching are instances of learning a time-dependent vector field that induces a family of marginals $(ρ_t)_{t \in [0,1]}$ governed by continuity and Fokker-Planck equations. Such a unified theory is timely because these methods are converging methodologically, yet fragmented notation and competing derivations continue to obscure their shared structure and the practical tradeoffs governing sampling, stability, and computation. Within this framework, we (i) derive reverse-time sampling for diffusion and score-based models as controlled stochastic dynamics, (ii) show that the probability flow ODE yields identical marginals and connects diffusion to likelihood-based normalizing flows, and (iii) interpret flow matching as direct regression of the velocity field under a chosen interpolation, clarifying when it coincides with or differs from score-based training. We compare objectives, sampling schemes, and discretization errors under unified notation, discuss connections to Schrodinger bridges and entropic optimal transport, and summarize theoretical guarantees and open problems on approximation, stability, and scalability.

ai.tldr: 本文统一了扩散模型、基于分数的生成模型和流匹配的连续时间生成建模框架，揭示了它们共享的测度论结构。
ai.motivation: 这些方法在方法论上趋于收敛，但符号和推导的碎片化掩盖了其共同结构和实际权衡。
ai.method: 通过连续性方程和Fokker-Planck方程，将扩散、分数和流匹配统一为学习时变向量场，并推导反向时间采样、概率流ODE和速度场回归。
ai.result: 统一符号下比较了目标函数、采样方案和离散化误差，并讨论了与Schrödinger桥和熵最优传输的联系。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解这些生成模型的内在联系、理论保证和开放问题，便于选择和应用。

idea_spark.idea: 利用概率流ODE将扩散模型转化为似然模型，可能改进密度估计和采样效率。
idea_spark.risk: 统一框架可能忽略各方法特有的数值稳定性细节，导致实际实现中的偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从Schrödinger桥和最优传输中汲取灵感，设计新的插值路径或正则化策略。

en.motivation: These methods are converging methodologically, but fragmented notation and derivations obscure their common structure and practical tradeoffs.
en.method: By learning a time-dependent vector field governed by continuity and Fokker-Planck equations, the framework derives reverse-time sampling, probability flow ODE, and velocity field regression.

papers.title: OmicsLM: A Multimodal Large Language Model for Multi-Sample Omics Reasoning
papers.summary: Interpreting transcriptomic data is one of the most common analytical tasks in modern biology. Yet most current models either consume expression profiles without producing natural-language biological explanations, or reason in language without direct access to quantitative omics measurements. We introduce OmicsLM, a multimodal LLM that connects quantitative omics profiles with natural-language biological tasks. OmicsLM represents each transcriptomic profile as a compact continuous representation within the LLM context. This interface preserves quantitative expression signal while allowing natural-language instructions, explicit gene mentions, and multiple interleaved biological samples to be processed together in one model context. We train OmicsLM on more than 5.5 million instruction-following examples spanning over 70 task types, combining continuous transcriptomic inputs, experimental data rendered through diverse language templates, and free-text biological knowledge and question-answering data. This mixture covers cell type annotation, perturbation prediction, clinical prediction, pathway reasoning, and open-ended biological question answering. Existing benchmarks evaluate either profile-level prediction or text-only biological QA, leaving language-guided, multi-sample reasoning over real expression profiles unmeasured. To close this gap, we introduce GEO-OmicsQA, a benchmark for multi-sample biological question answering built from real Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) studies. We demonstrate that OmicsLM can use expression profiles directly and perform comparably to specialized omics models on profile-level tasks, while outperforming both omics-specialized models and general LLMs on language-guided biological reasoning over expression data.

ai.tldr: 提出OmicsLM，一种多模态大语言模型，能够直接处理定量转录组数据并执行多样本生物学推理。
ai.motivation: 现有模型要么处理表达谱但不生成自然语言解释，要么仅基于文本推理而无法访问定量组学数据。
ai.method: 将每个转录组谱表示为LLM上下文中的紧凑连续向量，结合自然语言指令和基因提及，在超过550万条指令示例上训练，涵盖70多种任务类型。
ai.result: 在谱级任务上与专用组学模型相当，在语言引导的生物学推理上优于专用模型和通用LLM。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户直接对转录组数据进行自然语言查询和多样本比较分析。

idea_spark.inspiration: 利用LLM的上下文窗口同时处理多个样本，实现对比分析和扰动预测。

papers.title: Trajectory as the Teacher: Few-Step Discrete Flow Matching via Energy-Navigated Distillation
papers.summary: Discrete flow matching generates text by iteratively transforming noise tokens into coherent language, but may require hundreds of forward passes. Distillation uses the multi-step trajectory to train a student to reproduce the process in a few steps. When the student underperforms, the usual explanation is insufficient capacity. We argue the opposite: the trajectory is the bottleneck, not the student. Each training trajectory is built through a chain of blind stochastic jumps with no evaluation of sequence quality; a single bad decision at an early midpoint propagates through subsequent steps, yet the student must imitate the result. Trajectory-Shaped Discrete Flow Matching (TS-DFM) replaces these blind jumps with guided navigation: a lightweight energy compass evaluates candidate continuations at each midpoint, selecting the most coherent. All shaping is training-only; inference cost is unchanged. On 170M-parameter language modeling, the shaped student at 8 steps achieves 32% lower perplexity than the 1,024-step teacher while being 128x faster, with gains consistent across source distributions and three evaluators of increasing scale. TS-DFM achieves the best perplexity of any discrete-generation baseline we compare against, including methods trained on 6x more data or using 5x larger models.

ai.tldr: 提出TS-DFM方法，通过能量导航蒸馏改进离散流匹配，仅用8步生成达到比1024步教师模型更低的困惑度，速度提升128倍。
ai.motivation: 离散流匹配需要数百步前向传播，蒸馏时学生模型性能瓶颈并非容量不足，而是训练轨迹中早期随机跳跃的坏决策被传播。
ai.method: 在训练时用轻量能量罗盘评估每个中间点的候选延续，选择最连贯的轨迹，推理时不变。
ai.result: 170M参数模型上，8步学生困惑度比1024步教师低32%，速度提升128倍，优于多种基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过改进训练轨迹而非增加模型容量来提升蒸馏效率，适用于文本生成加速。

idea_spark.idea: 将能量函数作为可学习的轨迹选择器，替代固定随机跳跃，可能进一步结合强化学习优化。
idea_spark.risk: 能量罗盘的训练可能引入额外计算开销，且对复杂长序列的泛化性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受控轨迹生成与能量模型结合，启发设计更高效的生成式蒸馏框架。

en.tldr: TS-DFM uses energy-navigated distillation to improve discrete flow matching, achieving lower perplexity with 8 steps than a 1024-step teacher, at 128x speedup.
en.motivation: Discrete flow matching requires hundreds of forward passes; distillation bottleneck is the blind stochastic trajectory, not student capacity.
en.method: A lightweight energy compass evaluates candidate continuations at each midpoint during training to select the most coherent trajectory; inference unchanged.
en.result: On 170M-parameter LM, 8-step student achieves 32% lower perplexity than 1024-step teacher, 128x faster, outperforming baselines.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand that improving training trajectories, not model capacity, can boost distillation efficiency for fast text generation.

idea_spark.idea: Use learnable energy functions as trajectory selectors instead of fixed random jumps; could combine with reinforcement learning.
idea_spark.risk: Training the energy compass may add overhead; generalization to long complex sequences needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Controlled trajectory generation with energy models inspires more efficient generative distillation frameworks.

papers.title: Tessellations of Semi-Discrete Flow Matching
papers.summary: We study Flow Matching in a semi-discrete setting where a Gaussian source is transported toward a discrete target supported on finitely many points. This semi-discrete regime is the theoretical setting behind the use of Flow Matching for generative modeling, where the target distribution is represented by a finite dataset. In this semi-discrete regime, the exact Flow Matching velocity field is available in closed form, which makes it possible to analyze the geometry induced by the terminal flow map independently of optimization and approximation effects. We investigate the terminal assignment regions, namely the preimages of the target atoms under the terminal flow. We show that these regions are open, simply connected and, under an additional assumption, homeomorphic to the unit ball. At the same time, a planar four-point example shows that these cells can differ sharply from Laguerre cells arising in semi-discrete optimal transport: they may be non-convex, have curved boundaries, and exhibit different boundedness and adjacency patterns. These results clarify the geometry intrinsically induced by the exact semi-discrete Flow Matching objective before neural approximation enters the picture.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了半离散流匹配的几何结构，揭示了终端分配区域的性质及其与最优传输中Laguerre细胞的差异。
ai.motivation: 流匹配在生成建模中广泛应用，但其在半离散目标下的几何特性尚未被充分理解。
ai.method: 利用半离散流匹配速度场的闭式解，分析终端流映射下的分配区域，并与半离散最优传输的Laguerre细胞进行对比。
ai.result: 终端分配区域是开集、单连通，且在附加假设下与单位球同胚；但平面四点示例显示这些区域可能非凸、边界弯曲，且邻接模式不同。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解流匹配在有限数据集上的内在几何，为改进生成模型提供理论指导。

idea_spark.idea: 利用终端分配区域的非凸性设计更灵活的生成路径，或结合Laguerre细胞优化训练目标。
idea_spark.risk: 理论结果可能依赖特定假设，实际神经网络近似会引入额外偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从最优传输的几何视角重新审视流匹配，启发新的正则化或架构设计。

zh.tldr: 本文研究了半离散流匹配的几何结构，揭示了终端分配区域的性质及其与最优传输中Laguerre细胞的差异。
zh.motivation: 流匹配在生成建模中广泛应用，但其在半离散目标下的几何特性尚未被充分理解。
zh.method: 利用半离散流匹配速度场的闭式解，分析终端流映射下的分配区域，并与半离散最优传输的Laguerre细胞进行对比。
zh.result: 终端分配区域是开集、单连通，且在附加假设下与单位球同胚；但平面四点示例显示这些区域可能非凸、边界弯曲，且邻接模式不同。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解流匹配在有限数据集上的内在几何，为改进生成模型提供理论指导。

idea_spark.idea: 利用终端分配区域的非凸性设计更灵活的生成路径，或结合Laguerre细胞优化训练目标。
idea_spark.risk: 理论结果可能依赖特定假设，实际神经网络近似会引入额外偏差。

en.tldr: This paper studies the geometry of semi-discrete flow matching, revealing properties of terminal assignment regions and their differences from Laguerre cells in optimal transport.
en.motivation: Flow matching is widely used in generative modeling, but its geometric properties under semi-discrete targets are not well understood.
en.method: Using the closed-form velocity field of semi-discrete flow matching, we analyze assignment regions under the terminal flow map and compare them with Laguerre cells from semi-discrete optimal transport.
en.result: Terminal assignment regions are open, simply connected, and homeomorphic to the unit ball under an additional assumption; however, a planar four-point example shows they can be non-convex, have curved boundaries, and exhibit different adjacency patterns.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical insight into the intrinsic geometry of flow matching on finite datasets, guiding improvements in generative models.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage the non-convexity of terminal assignment regions to design more flexible generation paths, or combine with Laguerre cells to optimize training objectives.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical results may rely on specific assumptions; neural network approximations introduce additional biases.
idea_spark.inspiration: Revisiting flow matching from the geometric perspective of optimal transport inspires new regularization or architecture designs.

papers.title: Global Dynamics and Synchronization of Hodgkin-Huxley-Wilson Neural Networks
papers.summary: Hodgkin-Huxley equations as a monumental breakthrough in biological and physiological theory of the 20th century had been distilled into many simplified models to study, typically FitzHugh-Nagumo equations and Hindmarsh-Rose equations, but the model itself not being fully investigated in terms of global and asymptotic dynamics due to its strong nonlinearity and higher dimensionality. In this paper a new model called Hodgkin-Huxley-Wilson neural networks is proposed and investigated. This model captured the essential features of the nonlinearity and the conductances of two dominant ionic current channels of sodium and potassium coupled with the membrane equation in the original Hodgkin-Huxley model. Through uniform and sharp \emph{a priori} estimates by hard analysis on the solutions of the model equations and the interneuron differencing equations, It is rigorously proved that global solution dynamics are robustly dissipative with a sharp ultimate bound and that complete synchronization of the Hodgkin-Huxley-Wilson neural networks at an exponential convergence rate occurs if the interneuron coupling strength satisfies an explicitly computable threshold condition. The main results are further extended to Caputo fractional memristive Hodgkin-Huxley-Wilson neural networks.

ai.tldr: 提出Hodgkin-Huxley-Wilson神经网络模型，严格证明其全局解耗散性和完全同步的指数收敛条件，并推广到分数阶忆阻情形。
ai.motivation: 原始Hodgkin-Huxley模型因强非线性和高维性，其全局与渐近动力学尚未被充分研究。
ai.method: 通过硬分析对模型方程和神经元间差分方程进行一致且尖锐的先验估计。
ai.result: 全局解具有尖锐的最终有界耗散性；当耦合强度超过显式可计算的阈值时，网络以指数速率完全同步。
ai.help_to_user: 为理解生物神经网络全局同步行为提供严格数学框架，可用于设计鲁棒同步控制策略。

idea_spark.idea: 将分数阶忆阻引入Hodgkin-Huxley类网络，可能增强记忆效应和同步鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 分数阶系统的数值模拟和硬件实现可能面临计算复杂度和物理可行性挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴Hodgkin-Huxley模型的离子通道非线性，可设计新型人工神经元电路。

zh.tldr: 提出Hodgkin-Huxley-Wilson神经网络模型，严格证明其全局解耗散性和完全同步的指数收敛条件，并推广到分数阶忆阻情形。

en.tldr: Proposes Hodgkin-Huxley-Wilson neural networks, rigorously proves global dissipative dynamics and exponential complete synchronization under a computable coupling threshold, and extends to Caputo fractional memristive networks.
en.motivation: The original Hodgkin-Huxley model's strong nonlinearity and high dimensionality have hindered full investigation of its global and asymptotic dynamics.
en.method: Uniform and sharp a priori estimates via hard analysis on model equations and interneuron differencing equations.
en.result: Global solutions are robustly dissipative with a sharp ultimate bound; complete synchronization at exponential rate occurs when coupling strength exceeds an explicitly computable threshold.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous mathematical framework for understanding global synchronization in biological neural networks, useful for designing robust synchronization control strategies.

idea_spark.idea: Introducing fractional-order memristors into Hodgkin-Huxley-type networks may enhance memory effects and synchronization robustness.
idea_spark.risk: Numerical simulation and hardware implementation of fractional-order systems may face computational complexity and physical feasibility challenges.
idea_spark.inspiration: The ion-channel nonlinearity in Hodgkin-Huxley models can inspire novel artificial neuron circuits.

papers.title: A Behavioral Framework for Data-Driven Modeling of Nonlinear Systems in Vector-Valued Reproducing Kernel Hilbert Spaces
papers.summary: We generalize Jan Willems' behavioral approach to a class of discrete-time nonlinear systems in a vector-valued reproducing kernel Hilbert space (RKHS). Apart from linear time-invariant systems, this class covers nonlinear systems modeled by Volterra series and their autoregressive variants, as well as systems admitting Hammerstein-type state-space realizations. We apply the proposed framework to the problem of data-driven modeling of such systems, i.e., when simulation or control objectives for an unknown system are carried out without an explicit system identification step. To that end, we link the behavioral approach to two data-driven modeling methods in a vector-valued RKHS: (1) minimum-norm interpolation and (2) subspace identification.

ai.tldr: 将Willems的行为方法推广到向量值再生核希尔伯特空间中的非线性系统，并应用于数据驱动建模。
ai.motivation: 传统系统辨识需要显式模型，而数据驱动方法可直接利用数据完成仿真或控制，但缺乏统一框架。
ai.method: 在向量值RKHS中定义行为集，并连接最小范数插值和子空间辨识两种数据驱动方法。
ai.result: 覆盖了Volterra级数、自回归变体及Hammerstein状态空间实现等非线性系统类。
ai.help_to_user: 为非线性系统的数据驱动建模提供理论统一视角，便于设计无模型控制或仿真算法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用RKHS的核技巧将非线性行为线性化，从而复用线性系统工具。
idea_spark.risk: 高维核空间可能导致计算复杂度过高，且核选择依赖先验知识。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将行为理论与核方法结合，启发新的数据驱动控制策略。

zh.motivation: 传统系统辨识需要显式模型，而数据驱动方法可直接利用数据完成仿真或控制，但缺乏统一框架。

idea_spark.risk: 高维核空间可能导致计算复杂度过高，且核选择依赖先验知识。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将行为理论与核方法结合，启发新的数据驱动控制策略。

en.tldr: Generalizes Willems' behavioral approach to nonlinear systems in vector-valued RKHS and applies it to data-driven modeling.
en.motivation: Traditional system identification requires explicit models, while data-driven methods directly use data for simulation or control but lack a unified framework.
en.method: Defines behaviors in vector-valued RKHS and connects two data-driven methods: minimum-norm interpolation and subspace identification.
en.result: Covers nonlinear system classes including Volterra series, autoregressive variants, and Hammerstein state-space realizations.
en.help_to_user: Provides a theoretical unified perspective for data-driven modeling of nonlinear systems, facilitating model-free control or simulation algorithms.

idea_spark.idea: Use the kernel trick in RKHS to linearize nonlinear behaviors, thus reusing linear system tools.
idea_spark.risk: High-dimensional kernel spaces may lead to excessive computational cost, and kernel selection relies on prior knowledge.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining behavioral theory with kernel methods inspires new data-driven control strategies.

papers.title: Tamed Feynman-Kac diffusion processes: Killing-branching intertwine
papers.summary: Relaxation to equilibrium of a drifted Brownian motion is quantified by a probability density function, whose main (multiplicative) entry is an inferred Feynman-Kac kernel of the Schrödinger semigroup operator. Although seemingly devoid of a natural probabilistic significance (except for its explicit path integral definition), the pertinent kernel relaxes to equilibrium as well. The implicit Feynman-Kac potential ${\cal{V}}(x)$, continuous, confining and bounded from below, may take negative values. If positive, ${\cal{V}}(x)$ can be interpreted as the killing rate of the decaying diffusion process. In case of relaxing F-K kernels the killing effects are tamed (often overcompensated). The taming inavoidably appears in conjunction with the existence of the negativity subdomains of ${\cal{V}}(x)$ in $R$. If locally ${\cal{V}}(x) < 0$, its sign inversion $- {\cal{V}}(x)$ can be interpreted as the branching (cloning, alternatively bifurcation) rate in the course of the other wise free random motion. The arising killed diffusion processes with branching, we interpret as the possible path-wise background of tamed (relaxing) Feynman-Kac diffusions. We present acomputer-assisted path-wise arguments, towards a consistency of the killing/branching taming scenario, for a number of nonlinear model systems in one space dimension. Special attention is paid to Feynman-Kac potential shapes, presumed to be in the double well form, where an analytic access to eigenvalues and eigenfunctions is scarce beyond the semiclassical regime.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了带漂移布朗运动通过Feynman-Kac核的松弛过程，提出了一种杀死-分支交织机制来驯服负势区域，并通过一维非线性模型验证了该路径积分框架的一致性。
ai.motivation: Feynman-Kac核的松弛行为缺乏直观的概率解释，尤其是当势函数取负值时，传统杀死解释失效，需要新的路径背景。
ai.method: 将负势区域解释为分支（克隆）率，正势区域为杀死率，构建杀死-分支交织的扩散过程，并通过计算机辅助路径论证检验一致性。
ai.result: 对于双阱势等一维非线性模型，所提出的杀死/分支驯服场景与Feynman-Kac核的松弛行为一致，且能处理半经典区域之外的解析困难。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解Feynman-Kac扩散中负势的物理意义，以及如何用路径积分方法处理非平衡松弛问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将势函数的局部负值区域视为分支源，从而将Feynman-Kac核的松弛转化为可模拟的粒子数涨落过程。
idea_spark.risk: 分支过程可能导致粒子数指数增长，数值模拟中需控制计算资源；理论一致性仅在一维模型验证，高维或复杂势下可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受量子力学中虚时间路径积分与概率解释的启发，将薛定谔半群的核与分支过程类比，开辟了非平衡统计物理的新路径视角。

zh.tldr: 本文研究了带漂移布朗运动通过Feynman-Kac核的松弛过程，提出了一种杀死-分支交织机制来驯服负势区域，并通过一维非线性模型验证了该路径积分框架的一致性。

en.tldr: This paper investigates the relaxation of drifted Brownian motion via the Feynman-Kac kernel, proposes a killing-branching intertwine mechanism to tame negative potential regions, and validates the consistency of this path-integral framework with one-dimensional nonlinear models.
en.motivation: The relaxation behavior of the Feynman-Kac kernel lacks an intuitive probabilistic interpretation, especially when the potential takes negative values, where the traditional killing interpretation fails and a new path background is needed.
en.method: Interpret negative potential regions as branching (cloning) rates and positive regions as killing rates, construct a killed diffusion process with branching, and test consistency via computer-assisted path arguments.
en.result: For one-dimensional nonlinear models such as double-well potentials, the proposed killing/branching taming scenario is consistent with the relaxation of the Feynman-Kac kernel and handles analytic difficulties beyond the semiclassical regime.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the physical meaning of negative potentials in Feynman-Kac diffusions and how to treat nonequilibrium relaxation problems using path integral methods.

idea_spark.idea: Treat local negative potential regions as branching sources, thereby converting the relaxation of the Feynman-Kac kernel into a simulatable particle number fluctuation process.
idea_spark.risk: Branching processes may lead to exponential growth in particle numbers, requiring computational resource control; theoretical consistency is only verified in one-dimensional models and may fail in higher dimensions or complex potentials.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the analogy between imaginary-time path integrals in quantum mechanics and probabilistic interpretations, the kernel of the Schrödinger semigroup is linked to branching processes, opening a new path perspective in nonequilibrium statistical physics.

papers.title: Prototype Guided Post-pretraining for Single-Cell Representation Learning
papers.summary: Single-cell representation learning (SCRL) from gene expression data offers a way to uncover the complex regulatory logic underlying cellular function. Inspired by large language models in natural language modeling, several single-cell pretrained models have recently been proposed that treat genes as tokens and cells as sentences. However, these models are fundamentally limited by the long-tailed nature of cell-type distributions and struggle to generalize under covariate shifts in gene expression data. While fine-tuning is often used to mitigate these issues, we observe that performance remains bounded. To address this challenge, we introduce CellRefine, a post-pretraining method that operates between the pretraining and fine-tuning stages of a single-cell foundation model. CellRefine uses a multi-faceted objective that incorporates marker-gene sets as structural priors to guide post-pretraining and refine the latent embedding manifold of cells. Across multiple computational biology tasks, empirical results show that CellRefine consistently improves downstream performance, yielding gains up to 15%.

ai.tldr: 提出CellRefine方法，在单细胞基础模型的预训练与微调之间进行后预训练，利用标记基因集作为结构先验优化细胞嵌入，提升下游任务性能。
ai.motivation: 现有单细胞预训练模型受限于细胞类型分布的长尾特性和基因表达数据的协变量偏移，微调后性能仍有瓶颈。
ai.method: CellRefine在预训练与微调之间引入后预训练阶段，使用多目标函数，将标记基因集作为结构先验指导嵌入空间优化。
ai.result: 在多个计算生物学任务上，CellRefine一致提升下游性能，最高提升15%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户更准确地从基因表达数据中学习单细胞表示，改善细胞类型识别和生物学发现。

idea_spark.idea: 利用领域知识（如标记基因集）作为结构先验，在预训练与微调之间增加一个引导性优化阶段。
idea_spark.risk: 标记基因集的质量和覆盖度可能影响效果，且后预训练增加计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受大语言模型启发，但针对单细胞数据特点设计后预训练，提示在基础模型中加入领域先验可突破微调上限。

zh.tldr: 提出CellRefine方法，在单细胞基础模型的预训练与微调之间进行后预训练，利用标记基因集作为结构先验优化细胞嵌入，提升下游任务性能。
