idea_spark.risk: 标记基因集的质量和覆盖度可能影响效果，且后预训练增加计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受大语言模型启发，但针对单细胞数据特点设计后预训练，提示在基础模型中加入领域先验可突破微调上限。

en.tldr: Proposes CellRefine, a post-pretraining method between pretraining and fine-tuning of single-cell foundation models, using marker-gene sets as structural priors to refine cell embeddings and improve downstream performance.
en.motivation: Existing single-cell pretrained models suffer from long-tailed cell-type distributions and covariate shifts in gene expression data, with fine-tuning yielding bounded performance.
en.method: CellRefine introduces a post-pretraining stage with a multi-faceted objective that incorporates marker-gene sets as structural priors to guide embedding refinement.
en.result: Consistent downstream performance improvements across multiple computational biology tasks, with gains up to 15%.
en.help_to_user: Helps users learn more accurate single-cell representations from gene expression data, improving cell-type identification and biological discovery.

idea_spark.idea: Use domain knowledge (e.g., marker-gene sets) as structural priors to add a guided optimization stage between pretraining and fine-tuning.
idea_spark.risk: Effectiveness depends on the quality and coverage of marker-gene sets, and post-pretraining adds computational overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by large language models but tailored for single-cell data, suggesting that incorporating domain priors in foundation models can break fine-tuning ceilings.

papers.title: Brownian-time Change of measure
papers.summary: We prove a fundamental change of measure theorem for the Brownian-time Brownian motion and its associated Brownian-time processes class introduced by Allouba and Zheng in 2001. This result, together with Allouba's prior work on (1) Brownian-time processes and their PDEs/SPDEs links and on (2) change of measure for SPDEs, is a critical building block in analyzing the behaviors of SDEs and SPDEs -- of different types and orders -- driven by Brownian-time noises and their relatives.

ai.tldr: 证明了布朗时间布朗运动及其相关过程的测度变换定理，为分析布朗时间噪声驱动的随机微分方程和随机偏微分方程提供了关键基础。
ai.motivation: 建立布朗时间过程及其相关随机方程的测度变换理论，以支持后续对SDE和SPDE行为的分析。
ai.method: 基于Allouba和Zheng在2001年引入的布朗时间布朗运动类，结合Allouba先前关于布朗时间过程与PDE/SPDE联系以及SPDE测度变换的工作，证明了一个基本的测度变换定理。
ai.result: 成功证明了布朗时间布朗运动及其关联过程的测度变换定理。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解布朗时间过程在随机分析中的核心性质，为研究此类噪声驱动的方程提供理论工具。

idea_spark.idea: 利用布朗时间测度变换，探索更复杂噪声（如分数布朗运动）驱动的SDE/SPDE的路径性质。
idea_spark.risk: 布朗时间过程非马尔可夫性可能导致分析复杂，测度变换的显式形式可能难以获得。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将时间变换与测度变换结合，为处理非标准随机积分提供新视角。

zh.tldr: 证明了布朗时间布朗运动及其相关过程的测度变换定理，为分析布朗时间噪声驱动的随机微分方程和随机偏微分方程提供了关键基础。
zh.motivation: 建立布朗时间过程及其相关随机方程的测度变换理论，以支持后续对SDE和SPDE行为的分析。
zh.method: 基于Allouba和Zheng在2001年引入的布朗时间布朗运动类，结合Allouba先前关于布朗时间过程与PDE/SPDE联系以及SPDE测度变换的工作，证明了一个基本的测度变换定理。
zh.result: 成功证明了布朗时间布朗运动及其关联过程的测度变换定理。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解布朗时间过程在随机分析中的核心性质，为研究此类噪声驱动的方程提供理论工具。

en.tldr: Proves a fundamental change of measure theorem for Brownian-time Brownian motion and its associated processes, providing a critical building block for analyzing SDEs and SPDEs driven by Brownian-time noises.
en.motivation: To establish a change of measure theory for Brownian-time processes and related stochastic equations, enabling analysis of SDE and SPDE behaviors.
en.method: Based on the Brownian-time Brownian motion class introduced by Allouba and Zheng (2001), combined with Allouba's prior work on Brownian-time processes and PDE/SPDE links and SPDE change of measure, a fundamental change of measure theorem is proved.
en.result: Successfully proves the change of measure theorem for Brownian-time Brownian motion and its associated processes.
en.help_to_user: Provides a theoretical tool for understanding core properties of Brownian-time processes in stochastic analysis and studying equations driven by such noises.

idea_spark.idea: Use Brownian-time change of measure to explore path properties of SDEs/SPDEs driven by more complex noises, such as fractional Brownian motion.
idea_spark.risk: Non-Markovian nature of Brownian-time processes may complicate analysis; explicit form of change of measure may be difficult to obtain.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining time change with change of measure offers a new perspective for handling non-standard stochastic integrals.

papers.title: Solving Convolution-type Integral Equations using Preconditioned Neural Operators
papers.summary: Convolution-type integral equations arise from various fields, \textit{e.g.}, finite impulse response filters in signal processing and deblurring problems in image processing. When solving these equations, conventional numerical methods, like the multigrid method, can only efficiently solve the low-frequency components in the error, but not the high-frequency components. In this paper, we apply neural operators to address this issue. By adopting a preconditioning approach, we propose a novel training strategy that trains neural operators to solve the high-frequency components efficiently. Then, we combine the neural operators with some classical iterative solvers, like the weighted Jacobi method, to obtain an efficient hybrid iterative algorithm for the integral equations. We analyze the generalization error of our training strategy and the convergence of the hybrid iterative algorithm. We test our algorithms on large-scale and ill-conditioned linear systems discretized from one- and two-dimensional convolution-type integral equations. Our proposed algorithm significantly outperforms the multigrid method and the preconditioned conjugate gradient method in both iteration numbers and computational time.

ai.tldr: 提出一种预条件神经算子方法，高效求解卷积型积分方程中的高频误差分量，并与经典迭代法结合形成混合算法。
ai.motivation: 传统数值方法（如多重网格法）只能有效求解低频误差，而高频误差难以处理。
ai.method: 采用预条件策略训练神经算子专门求解高频分量，再与加权Jacobi法等经典迭代器结合为混合迭代算法。
ai.result: 在一维和二维大规模病态线性系统上，所提算法在迭代次数和计算时间上均显著优于多重网格法和预条件共轭梯度法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效求解卷积型积分方程的数值方案，尤其适用于信号处理和图像去模糊等应用。

idea_spark.idea: 将神经算子作为高频误差校正器，嵌入经典迭代法循环中，实现混合求解。
idea_spark.risk: 神经算子的训练可能对问题规模或核函数形式敏感，泛化性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受多重网格法中平滑器思想的启发，用数据驱动方式替代传统高频平滑算子。

en.tldr: Proposes a preconditioned neural operator method to efficiently solve high-frequency error components in convolution-type integral equations, combined with classical iterative solvers into a hybrid algorithm.
en.motivation: Conventional numerical methods (e.g., multigrid) efficiently handle only low-frequency errors, leaving high-frequency components unresolved.
en.method: Trains neural operators with a preconditioning strategy to solve high-frequency components, then integrates them with classical iterative solvers like weighted Jacobi into a hybrid iterative algorithm.
en.result: On large-scale ill-conditioned 1D and 2D linear systems, the proposed algorithm significantly outperforms multigrid and preconditioned conjugate gradient methods in both iteration count and computational time.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient numerical solution for convolution-type integral equations, beneficial for signal processing and image deblurring applications.

idea_spark.idea: Use neural operators as high-frequency error correctors embedded in classical iterative cycles for hybrid solving.
idea_spark.risk: Training neural operators may be sensitive to problem scale or kernel form; generalization requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the smoother role in multigrid methods, replacing traditional high-frequency smoothers with data-driven neural operators.

papers.title: American Options Pricing under Heston Model via Curriculum Learning in Coupled PINNs
papers.summary: In American options, the early exercise feature allows the option to be exercised at any time prior to expiration. However, this flexibility introduces a challenge: the pricing model must value the option while simultaneously determining an unknown, time-varying exercise boundary. The Heston model is one of the most popular ways to model real market behavior because it allows volatility to change over time. However, unlike European options, there is no closed-form solution for American options under the Heston model, so we have to use numerical methods. In this paper, we propose a novel approach to solving the stochastic Heston partial differential equation for American options, using coupled physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) to predict both the option price and the free boundary, while employing curriculum learning and adaptive resampling to stabilize model training. Our work builds on recent deep learning methods but introduces a more effective training strategy to address the limitations of these approaches. The numerical results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed learning framework, providing a robust and efficient alternative to pricing American options, enabling rapid inference and accurate estimation under stochastic volatility.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于课程学习和自适应重采样的耦合物理信息神经网络，用于在Heston模型下定价美式期权并预测自由边界。
ai.motivation: 美式期权因可提前行权而需同时定价和确定未知时变边界，Heston模型虽能刻画随机波动但无解析解，现有数值方法效率低或训练不稳定。
ai.method: 采用耦合物理信息神经网络同时预测期权价格和自由边界，引入课程学习逐步增加训练难度，结合自适应重采样稳定训练过程。
ai.result: 数值实验表明该框架能高效、准确地定价美式期权，在随机波动下实现快速推理和精确估计。
ai.help_to_user: 为金融衍生品定价提供一种鲁棒且高效的深度学习替代方案，尤其适用于需要快速计算和随机波动建模的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将课程学习与物理信息神经网络结合，通过分阶段训练和动态采样提升复杂PDE求解的稳定性与精度。
idea_spark.risk: 课程学习阶段划分和重采样频率需手动调参，可能影响泛化性；对高维或强非线性问题计算成本可能增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴机器学习中的课程学习思想，解决传统PINNs在非光滑解或自由边界问题中的训练困难。

en.tldr: Proposes a coupled physics-informed neural network with curriculum learning and adaptive resampling to price American options under the Heston model and predict the free boundary.
en.motivation: American options require simultaneous pricing and determination of an unknown time-varying exercise boundary; the Heston model captures stochastic volatility but lacks closed-form solutions, and existing numerical methods are inefficient or suffer from training instability.
en.method: Uses coupled PINNs to predict both option price and free boundary, introduces curriculum learning to gradually increase training difficulty, and employs adaptive resampling to stabilize training.
en.result: Numerical experiments demonstrate that the framework prices American options efficiently and accurately, enabling rapid inference and precise estimation under stochastic volatility.
en.help_to_user: Provides a robust and efficient deep learning alternative for financial derivative pricing, especially in scenarios requiring fast computation and stochastic volatility modeling.

idea_spark.idea: Combines curriculum learning with PINNs through staged training and dynamic sampling to improve stability and accuracy in solving complex PDEs.
idea_spark.risk: Manual tuning of curriculum stages and resampling frequency may affect generalization; computational cost may increase for high-dimensional or strongly nonlinear problems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws on curriculum learning from machine learning to address training difficulties in traditional PINNs for non-smooth solutions or free boundary problems.

papers.title: NSPOD: acceleratingthe convergence ofKrylov-based iterative linearsolvers via approximated PODs
papers.summary: The convergence of Krylov-based linear iterative solvers applied to parametric partial differential equations (PDEs) is often highly sensitive to the domain, its discretization, the location/values of the applied Dirichlet/Neumann boundary conditions, body forces and material properties, among others. We have previously introduced hybridization of classical linear iterative solvers with neural operators for specific geometries, but they tend to not perform well on geometries not previously seen during training. We partially addressed this challenge by introducing the deep operator network Geo-DeepONet and hybridizing it with Krylov-based iterative linear solvers, which, despite learning effectively across arbitrary unstructured meshes without requiring retraining, led to only modest reductions in iterations compared to state-of-the-art preconditioners. In this study we introduce Neural Subspace Proper Orthogonal Decomposition (NSPOD), a multigrid-like deep operator network-based preconditioner which can dramatically reduce the number of iterations needed for convergence in Krylov-based linear iterative solvers, even when compared to state-of-the-art methods such as algebraic multigrid preconditioners. We demonstrate its efficiency via numerical experiments on a linearized version of solid mechanics PDEs applied to unstructured domains obtained from complex CAD geometries. We expect that the findings in this study lead to more efficient hybrid preconditioners that can match, or possibly even surpass, the convergence properties of the current gold standard preconditioning methods for solid mechanics PDEs.

ai.tldr: 提出NSPOD，一种基于深度算子网络的多重网格类预条件器，可大幅减少Krylov迭代线性求解器在参数化偏微分方程上的收敛迭代次数。
ai.motivation: Krylov迭代求解器对参数化PDE的几何、边界条件等敏感，现有混合方法在未见几何上表现不佳或迭代减少有限。
ai.method: 引入神经子空间本征正交分解（NSPOD），一种类似多重网格的深度算子网络预条件器，与Krylov迭代求解器混合。
ai.result: 在固体力学线性化PDE的数值实验中，NSPOD相比代数多重网格等先进预条件器显著减少收敛所需迭代次数。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用神经网络加速传统迭代求解器在复杂几何上的收敛。

idea_spark.idea: 将POD与深度算子网络结合，构建可跨网格泛化的预条件器。
idea_spark.risk: 训练成本高，且对未见几何的泛化能力仍需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从多重网格方法中汲取灵感，用神经网络学习子空间校正算子。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从多重网格方法中汲取灵感，用神经网络学习子空间校正算子。

en.tldr: NSPOD is a multigrid-like deep operator network preconditioner that dramatically reduces iteration counts for Krylov-based iterative solvers on parametric PDEs.
en.motivation: Krylov solvers are sensitive to geometry and boundary conditions; existing hybrid methods generalize poorly or yield modest speedups.
en.method: Introduces Neural Subspace Proper Orthogonal Decomposition (NSPOD), a multigrid-like deep operator network preconditioner hybridized with Krylov solvers.
en.result: Numerical experiments on linearized solid mechanics PDEs show NSPOD significantly reduces iterations compared to state-of-the-art preconditioners like algebraic multigrid.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how neural networks can accelerate traditional iterative solvers on complex geometries.

idea_spark.idea: Combine POD with deep operator networks to build a preconditioner that generalizes across meshes.
idea_spark.risk: Training cost is high, and generalization to unseen geometries remains to be validated.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from multigrid methods, using neural networks to learn subspace correction operators.

papers.title: PPI-Net connects molecular protein interactions to functional processes in disease
papers.summary: Understanding how molecular alterations propagate across biological systems to drive disease remains a central challenge. Although high-throughput profiling enables comprehensive characterization of tumor states, most models neglect structured biological relationships or lack interpretability across scales. Here we present PPI-Net, a hierarchical graph neural network that integrates protein-protein interaction (PPI) networks with pathway-level representations to model disease from molecular interactions to functional processes. Patient-specific molecular profiles are embedded within a shared interaction network from STRING and propagated through a multi-layer Reactome hierarchy using graph attention, enabling aggregation of gene-level signals into higher-order biological programs. Across RNA-seq data from ten cancer types from The Cancer Genome Atlas, PPI-Net achieves robust predictive performance, with balanced accuracy exceeding 90% in multiple cohorts. Comparative analysis on RNA-Seq data from breast cancer demonstrated that PPI-Net's integration of the Reactome hierarchy improved balanced accuracy by 6.7% relative to a PPI-only model, while hierarchical multi-level supervision improved balanced accuracy by 12.3% relative to using only a single top-level prediction head. Applying a multi-omics approach using RNA-seq and methylation data improves model interpretation, recovering canonical oncogenic modules, including TP53-AKT signaling and stress response pathways, while revealing convergence onto coherent programs such as ion signaling and cellular responses to stimuli. These results demonstrate that integrating interaction networks with pathway hierarchies enables accurate prediction while providing mechanistic insight into cancer biology.

ai.tldr: 提出PPI-Net，一种分层图神经网络，整合蛋白质相互作用网络与通路层级，从分子层面到功能过程建模疾病，在多种癌症RNA-seq数据中实现高精度预测并提供机制洞察。
ai.motivation: 现有模型忽略生物结构关系或缺乏跨尺度可解释性，难以理解分子改变如何通过生物系统传播驱动疾病。
ai.method: 将患者分子谱嵌入STRING共享相互作用网络，通过图注意力机制在多层Reactome层级中传播，聚合基因级信号为高阶生物程序。
ai.result: 在TCGA十种癌症RNA-seq数据中平衡准确率超90%；乳腺癌数据中整合Reactome层级提升6.7%，层级多级监督提升12.3%；多组学分析恢复TP53-AKT等经典致癌模块。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员从分子相互作用和通路层级理解癌症机制，提供可解释的预测模型。

idea_spark.idea: 结合单细胞数据与PPI-Net，解析细胞类型特异的通路扰动。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量PPI和通路数据库，可能受数据不完整或偏差影响。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用图注意力机制在生物层级中传播信息，启发设计更通用的多尺度生物网络模型。

en.tldr: PPI-Net is a hierarchical graph neural network integrating protein-protein interaction networks with pathway hierarchies to model disease from molecular to functional levels, achieving high accuracy and mechanistic insights across multiple cancer types.
en.motivation: Existing models neglect structured biological relationships or lack cross-scale interpretability, hindering understanding of how molecular alterations propagate through biological systems to drive disease.
en.method: Patient molecular profiles are embedded in the STRING interaction network and propagated through a multi-layer Reactome hierarchy using graph attention, aggregating gene-level signals into higher-order biological programs.
en.result: Balanced accuracy exceeds 90% across ten TCGA cancer types; integrating Reactome hierarchy improves accuracy by 6.7% and hierarchical multi-level supervision by 12.3% in breast cancer; multi-omics analysis recovers canonical modules like TP53-AKT signaling.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate single-cell data with PPI-Net to dissect cell-type-specific pathway perturbations.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality PPI and pathway databases, which may be incomplete or biased.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using graph attention to propagate information across biological hierarchies inspires general multi-scale biological network models.

papers.title: Scaling Categorical Flow Maps
papers.summary: Continuous diffusion and flow matching models could represent a powerful alternative to autoregressive approaches for language modelling (LM), as they unlock a host of advantages currently reserved for continuous modalities, including accelerated sampling and tilting. Recently, several works have demonstrated the possibility of generating discrete data continuously by a simple flow matching process between a Gaussian and the one-hot encoded data distribution. They have further shown the feasibility of accelerated sampling via Categorical Flow Maps (CFMs), resulting in competitive sample quality in the few-step regime. However, this method had only been evaluated at relatively modest scales ($<1$B), leaving the question of its scalability completely open. In this article, we train a $1.7$B-parameter base flow model on $2.1$T tokens and self-distill it into a CFM that generates diverse, high-quality text in as few as $4$ inference steps while maintaining near-data-level token entropy. Furthermore, we introduce a likelihood bound for CFMs in the semi-discrete setting, and show that they can be used to score the model on standard LM benchmarks, achieving results in the same range as discrete diffusion methods. Finally, we uncover some of the challenges that arise from training these models at scale, and we provide prescriptive insights on loss weighting and time scheduling.

ai.tldr: 本文展示了类别流映射（CFM）在语言建模中的可扩展性，训练了17亿参数的基流模型，并通过自蒸馏实现仅需4步推理即可生成高质量文本，同时提出了半离散设置下的似然界。
ai.motivation: 连续扩散和流匹配模型在语言建模中具有加速采样和倾斜等优势，但此前仅在较小规模（<1B参数）上验证，其可扩展性未知。
ai.method: 在2.1T tokens上训练17亿参数的基流模型，通过自蒸馏得到CFM，并引入半离散似然界用于评分，同时研究了损失加权和时间调度策略。
ai.result: CFM在4步推理内生成多样、高质量文本，保持接近数据级别的token熵，在标准LM基准上达到与离散扩散方法相当的结果。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何将连续流匹配模型扩展到大规模语言建模，并提供加速采样和评分的方法。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将CFM与自回归模型混合，结合两者优势。
idea_spark.risk: 大规模训练时可能面临稳定性问题，需精细调整超参数。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从连续扩散模型在图像生成的成功中借鉴，将其应用于离散文本生成。

en.tldr: This paper demonstrates the scalability of categorical flow maps (CFMs) for language modelling, training a 1.7B-parameter base flow model and self-distilling it into a CFM that generates high-quality text in as few as 4 inference steps, while introducing a likelihood bound for the semi-discrete setting.
en.motivation: Continuous diffusion and flow matching models offer advantages like accelerated sampling and tilting for language modelling, but their scalability had only been tested at modest scales (<1B parameters).
en.method: Train a 1.7B-parameter base flow model on 2.1T tokens, self-distill into a CFM, introduce a semi-discrete likelihood bound for scoring, and study loss weighting and time scheduling.
en.result: The CFM generates diverse, high-quality text in 4 inference steps with near-data-level token entropy, achieving results comparable to discrete diffusion methods on standard LM benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to scale continuous flow matching models to large-scale language modelling, with methods for accelerated sampling and scoring.

idea_spark.idea: Explore hybrid CFM-autoregressive models to combine strengths of both approaches.
idea_spark.risk: Large-scale training may face stability issues requiring careful hyperparameter tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw inspiration from continuous diffusion models in image generation and adapt them to discrete text generation.

papers.title: Structured Coupling for Flow Matching
papers.summary: Standard flow matching scales well but typically relies on an unstructured source distribution, limiting its ability to learn interpretable latent structure. Latent-variable models, by contrast, capture structure but often sacrifice generative quality. We bridge this gap by proposing Structured Coupling for Flow Matching (SCFM), a cooperative framework that augments flow matching with structured latent representation learning. By introducing structured latent variables and exogenous noise into the source, SCFM jointly learns a structured prior (via latent variable modeling) and a continuous transport map (via flow matching). It uses a shared time-dependent recognition network for both latent variable model variational inference and intermediate-time flow velocity estimation. This yields a structurally informed yet unconditional, simulation-free flow model, where the latent variable model can also assist flow sampling. Empirically, SCFM facilitates unsupervised latent representation learning for clustering, disentanglement and downstream tasks, while remaining competitive with flow matching in sample quality, showing that meaningful structure can be learned without sacrificing generative fidelity.

ai.tldr: 提出结构化耦合流匹配（SCFM），将结构化潜变量建模与流匹配结合，在保持生成质量的同时学习可解释的潜结构。
ai.motivation: 标准流匹配依赖无结构源分布，难以学习可解释潜结构；潜变量模型能捕捉结构但牺牲生成质量。
ai.method: 在源分布中引入结构化潜变量和外生噪声，通过共享时间依赖识别网络联合学习结构化先验（潜变量模型）和连续传输映射（流匹配）。
ai.result: SCFM在无监督聚类、解耦和下游任务上表现良好，生成质量与流匹配相当，证明可学习有意义结构而不牺牲保真度。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需监督即可学习数据潜结构同时保持高生成质量的框架，适用于需要可解释表示和高质量生成的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将潜变量模型与流匹配的共享网络设计用于多任务学习，如同时进行数据压缩和生成。
idea_spark.risk: 共享网络可能引入优化冲突，需平衡潜变量推断与流速度估计的损失。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受变分自编码器与流匹配互补优势的启发，探索协同训练范式。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受变分自编码器与流匹配互补优势的启发，探索协同训练范式。

en.tldr: Proposes Structured Coupling for Flow Matching (SCFM), combining structured latent variable modeling with flow matching to learn interpretable latent structure while maintaining generative quality.
en.motivation: Standard flow matching relies on unstructured source distributions, limiting interpretable latent structure learning; latent variable models capture structure but sacrifice generative quality.
en.method: Introduces structured latent variables and exogenous noise into the source, jointly learning a structured prior (via latent variable modeling) and a continuous transport map (via flow matching) using a shared time-dependent recognition network.
en.result: SCFM performs well on unsupervised clustering, disentanglement, and downstream tasks, with generative quality competitive to flow matching, demonstrating that meaningful structure can be learned without sacrificing fidelity.
en.help_to_user: Provides a framework to learn latent structure without supervision while maintaining high generative quality, useful for applications needing interpretable representations and high-quality generation.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage the shared network design for multi-task learning, such as simultaneous data compression and generation.
idea_spark.risk: Shared network may introduce optimization conflicts; careful balancing of losses for latent inference and flow velocity estimation is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the complementary strengths of variational autoencoders and flow matching, exploring a cooperative training paradigm.

papers.title: Does Your Neural Network Extrapolate? Feature Engineering as Identifiability Bias for OOD Generalization
papers.summary: Successful deep neural networks discover salient features of data. We show when and why they fail to learn out-of-distribution (OOD)-relevant representations from an in-distribution (ID) training window. This requires decoupling feature learning from data-generating-process (DGP) identifiability. From a single training window, OOD extrapolation is non-identifiable: infinitely many DGPs are $\varepsilon$-observationally equivalent on the training data but diverge arbitrarily outside it, and no in-distribution criterion alone reliably breaks the tie. A structural commitment, the feature map, label map, and model class $(\varphi, ψ, \mathcal{M})$, dictates the assumed DGP and governs OOD generalization while leaving ID performance essentially unchanged. When architecture, pretraining, augmentation, input formats, or domain knowledge implicitly inject the missing commitment, the model succeeds. When it cannot infer OOD-relevant structure from ID evidence, it fails. Changing only the representation can make the same architecture, at the same in-distribution loss, differ by ${\sim}520\times$ out of distribution. When the commitment is correct and identifiable, OOD error vanishes. For example, Fourier coordinates turn periodic extrapolation into interpolation on $\mathbb{S}^1$. The same mechanism predicts outcomes in three natural-science settings (mass-action chemistry; Kepler's-third-law exoplanet prediction, $n=2{,}362$; and cross-species coding-DNA detection) and in a 264-run positional-encoding study across Transformer, Mamba, and S4D. Finally, a controlled study shows: correct features are necessary but not sufficient. The model class must express the target, and the transformed training data must cover the relevant representation space.

ai.tldr: 本文揭示了神经网络在分布外泛化中失败的根本原因：从单一训练窗口无法唯一确定数据生成过程，需要特征工程提供可识别性偏差。
ai.motivation: 现有方法难以解释为何相同架构、相同分布内损失下，分布外性能差异巨大。
ai.method: 将特征学习与数据生成过程可识别性解耦，提出特征映射、标签映射和模型类构成的结构承诺框架。
ai.result: 正确的特征工程可使分布外误差消失，例如傅里叶坐标将周期外推转化为内插；在多个自然科学场景和位置编码研究中验证。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解为何某些特征工程（如傅里叶特征、位置编码）对分布外泛化至关重要，并提供诊断和设计指南。

idea_spark.idea: 利用领域知识设计特征映射，使模型在训练数据上无法区分的多个数据生成过程在分布外区域被唯一确定。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖特定特征映射可能导致对训练分布过拟合，若特征假设错误则泛化失败。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受因果推断中工具变量思想的启发，将特征工程视为一种结构性约束，类似于因果图中的后门调整。

en.tldr: This paper reveals why neural networks fail at out-of-distribution generalization: from a single training window, the data-generating process is non-identifiable, and feature engineering provides the necessary identifiability bias.
en.motivation: Existing methods cannot explain why the same architecture with the same in-distribution loss can differ by ~520× out of distribution.
en.method: Decouple feature learning from DGP identifiability, proposing a structural commitment framework consisting of feature map, label map, and model class.
en.result: Correct features make OOD error vanish (e.g., Fourier coordinates turn periodic extrapolation into interpolation); validated on multiple natural-science settings and a 264-run positional encoding study.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand why certain feature engineering (e.g., Fourier features, positional encodings) is critical for OOD generalization, and provides diagnostic and design guidelines.

idea_spark.idea: Use domain knowledge to design feature maps that uniquely determine the DGP outside the training distribution, even when multiple DGPs are indistinguishable on training data.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on specific feature maps may cause overfitting to the training distribution; if the feature assumption is wrong, generalization fails.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by instrumental variables in causal inference, treating feature engineering as a structural constraint analogous to backdoor adjustment in causal graphs.

papers.title: Flatness and Gradient Alignment Are Both Necessary: Spectral-Aware Gradient-Aligned Exploration for Multi-Distribution Learning
papers.summary: Sharpness-aware and gradient-alignment methods have been shown to improve generalization, however each family of methods targets a single geometric property of the loss landscape, while ignoring the other. In this paper, we show that this omission is structurally unavoidable and that both flatness and gradient alignment should be considered in multi-distribution learning settings. Specifically, we derive an excess-risk decomposition that yields two additive leading-order terms: (i) an alignment term, controlled by the trace of $\bar{H}^{-1}Σ_g$ and (ii) a curvature term, controlled by $\bar{H}$, where $\bar{H}$ is the average Hessian and $Σ_g$ is the covariance of the gradient across distributions. Notably, $\bar{H}$ appears inverted in one and non-inverted in the other. We further show, via a counterexample, that neither quantity bounds the other in general, so no algorithm targeting only one term can guarantee low excess risk. Motivated by this decomposition, we propose SAGE (Spectral-Aware Gradient-Aligned Exploration) that targets both terms. The curvature component replaces SAM's gradient-scaled perturbation with the polar factor of each layer's gradient matrix, computed via Newton-Schulz iteration, so that the ascent step probes all directions with similar magnitude. On the other hand, the alignment component injects isotropic noise at the descent step, the magnitude of which scales with cross-distribution gradient disagreement. Experiments on five domain-generalization and two multi-task learning benchmarks show that the proposed method establishes a new state-of-the-art on DomainBed and acts as a general-purpose improvement to base MTL solvers, remaining competitive with, or even surpassing, state-of-the-art methods.

ai.tldr: 本文证明在多分布学习中，损失景观的平坦性和梯度对齐性两者缺一不可，并提出了同时优化两者的SAGE方法。
ai.motivation: 现有方法分别关注平坦性或梯度对齐性，但忽略了另一性质，导致在多分布学习场景下泛化性能受限。
ai.method: 推导出超额风险分解，包含对齐项和曲率项；提出SAGE方法，用梯度矩阵的极因子替代SAM的扰动进行上升步，并在下降步注入与跨分布梯度不一致性成比例的噪声。
ai.result: 在五个域泛化和两个多任务学习基准上达到新最优，或与现有方法相当甚至更优。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解在多分布学习中同时考虑平坦性和梯度对齐性的必要性，并提供一种实用的优化算法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Hessian谱信息自适应调整扰动方向，可能比固定扰动更高效。
idea_spark.risk: 计算极因子和Hessian近似可能增加训练开销，需权衡效率与收益。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从理论分解出发设计算法，确保每个优化项都有明确的泛化意义。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Hessian谱信息自适应调整扰动方向，可能比固定扰动更高效。
idea_spark.risk: 计算极因子和Hessian近似可能增加训练开销，需权衡效率与收益。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从理论分解出发设计算法，确保每个优化项都有明确的泛化意义。

en.tldr: This paper proves that both flatness and gradient alignment are necessary in multi-distribution learning, and proposes SAGE that optimizes both.
en.motivation: Existing methods target either flatness or gradient alignment, ignoring the other, which limits generalization in multi-distribution settings.
en.method: Derives an excess-risk decomposition with an alignment term and a curvature term; proposes SAGE that replaces SAM's perturbation with the polar factor of gradient matrices in the ascent step and injects isotropic noise scaled by cross-distribution gradient disagreement in the descent step.
en.result: Achieves new state-of-the-art on five domain generalization and two multi-task learning benchmarks, or remains competitive with existing methods.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the necessity of considering both flatness and gradient alignment in multi-distribution learning, and provides a practical optimization algorithm.

idea_spark.idea: Using Hessian spectral information to adaptively adjust perturbation directions may be more efficient than fixed perturbations.
idea_spark.risk: Computing polar factors and Hessian approximations may increase training overhead; trade-offs between efficiency and benefit need to be considered.
idea_spark.inspiration: Designing algorithms from theoretical decomposition ensures each optimization term has clear generalization significance.

papers.title: A Note on the Construction of Trial States for the Dilute Bose Gas
papers.summary: We review how the local particle number cutoff introduced in [11] is used to build trial states for the dilute Bose gas that capture the substantial correlation structure of the ground state in the thermodynamic limit. In particular, we provide a simplified derivation of the Lee-Huang-Yang correction as an upper bound for the ground state energy.

ai.tldr: 本文回顾了如何利用局部粒子数截断构造稀薄玻色气体的试探态，并简化推导了Lee-Huang-Yang修正作为基态能量的上界。
ai.motivation: 为稀薄玻色气体构造能捕捉热力学极限下基态相关结构的试探态，并给出基态能量的严格上界。
ai.method: 采用局部粒子数截断方法构造试探态，并简化Lee-Huang-Yang修正的上界推导。
ai.result: 成功构造了试探态，并证明了Lee-Huang-Yang修正作为基态能量的上界。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解稀薄玻色气体中试探态的构造方法及基态能量上界的推导。

idea_spark.idea: 将局部粒子数截断与变分法结合，可能为其他玻色系统提供更紧的能量上界。
idea_spark.risk: 该方法可能依赖于特定系统的对称性，推广时需谨慎验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Lee-Huang-Yang修正的启发，可探索其他量子修正项的上界构造。

zh.tldr: 本文回顾了如何利用局部粒子数截断构造稀薄玻色气体的试探态，并简化推导了Lee-Huang-Yang修正作为基态能量的上界。

zh.result: 成功构造了试探态，并证明了Lee-Huang-Yang修正作为基态能量的上界。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解稀薄玻色气体中试探态的构造方法及基态能量上界的推导。

idea_spark.idea: 将局部粒子数截断与变分法结合，可能为其他玻色系统提供更紧的能量上界。
idea_spark.risk: 该方法可能依赖于特定系统的对称性，推广时需谨慎验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Lee-Huang-Yang修正的启发，可探索其他量子修正项的上界构造。

en.tldr: This note reviews how the local particle number cutoff is used to construct trial states for the dilute Bose gas, and provides a simplified derivation of the Lee-Huang-Yang correction as an upper bound for the ground state energy.
en.motivation: To construct trial states capturing the correlation structure of the ground state in the thermodynamic limit for the dilute Bose gas, and to provide a rigorous upper bound for the ground state energy.
en.method: Using the local particle number cutoff to build trial states and simplifying the derivation of the Lee-Huang-Yang correction as an upper bound.
en.result: Trial states are successfully constructed, and the Lee-Huang-Yang correction is proven as an upper bound for the ground state energy.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the construction of trial states for the dilute Bose gas and the derivation of an upper bound for the ground state energy.

idea_spark.idea: Combining the local particle number cutoff with variational methods may yield tighter energy upper bounds for other Bose systems.
idea_spark.risk: The method may rely on specific symmetries of the system, requiring careful validation when generalized.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the Lee-Huang-Yang correction, one can explore upper bound constructions for other quantum correction terms.

papers.title: CellScientist: Dual-Space Hierarchical Orchestration for Closed-Loop Refinement of Virtual Cell Models
papers.summary: Virtual Cell Modeling (VCM) requires models that not only predict perturbation responses, but also support targeted revision when predictions fail. Current LLM-assisted modeling workflows face a refinement-routing problem: prediction discrepancies are observed through executable implementations, but the relevant revision may involve the modeling assumption, representation design, implementation, or task constraint. Without structured feedback propagation across these levels, iterative refinement may repair code while failing to revise the assumption responsible for the discrepancy. We propose CellScientist, a dual-space hierarchical framework that couples a high-level hypothesis space with a low-level executable implementation space. CellScientist represents modeling decisions as structured states, realizes them as admissible programs under task and interface constraints, and routes execution discrepancies back to targeted hypothesis or implementation updates. This enables a closed Hypothesis -> Implementation -> Hypothesis loop where failures become structured signals for model refinement rather than debugging events. Across morphology and transcriptomic benchmarks, with additional single-cell perturbation evaluations, the final executable models selected by CellScientist improve over reference baselines under fixed split and evaluation protocols, while the workflow produces auditable refinement traces.

ai.tldr: 提出CellScientist框架，通过双空间层次编排实现虚拟细胞模型的闭环精炼，将执行失败转化为结构化信号进行假设或实现更新。
ai.motivation: 当前LLM辅助建模工作流面临精炼-路由问题：预测偏差通过可执行实现观察到，但相关修订可能涉及建模假设、表示设计、实现或任务约束，缺乏跨层次的结构化反馈传播。
ai.method: 构建双空间层次框架，耦合高层假设空间与低层可执行实现空间，将建模决策表示为结构化状态，在任务和接口约束下实现为可接受程序，并将执行偏差路由回目标假设或实现更新。
ai.result: 在形态学和转录组学基准测试以及单细胞扰动评估中，CellScientist选择的最终可执行模型在固定分割和评估协议下优于参考基线，并产生可审计的精炼轨迹。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员系统性地精炼虚拟细胞模型，将失败转化为结构化信号而非调试事件，提升模型预测扰动响应的能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将建模假设显式表示为可路由的结构化状态，使失败信号能精准定位到假设或实现层面，避免仅修复代码而忽略根本假设。
idea_spark.risk: 假设空间和实现空间的划分可能过于简化实际建模中的复杂依赖关系；结构化状态表示可能增加框架的初始设计成本。

en.tldr: Proposes CellScientist, a dual-space hierarchical framework for closed-loop refinement of virtual cell models, turning execution failures into structured signals for hypothesis or implementation updates.
en.motivation: Current LLM-assisted modeling workflows face a refinement-routing problem: prediction discrepancies are observed through executable implementations, but relevant revisions may involve modeling assumptions, representation design, implementation, or task constraints, lacking structured feedback propagation across levels.
en.method: Constructs a dual-space hierarchical framework coupling a high-level hypothesis space with a low-level executable implementation space, representing modeling decisions as structured states, realizing them as admissible programs under task and interface constraints, and routing execution discrepancies back to targeted hypothesis or implementation updates.
en.result: On morphology and transcriptomic benchmarks with single-cell perturbation evaluations, final executable models selected by CellScientist improve over reference baselines under fixed split and evaluation protocols, producing auditable refinement traces.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers systematically refine virtual cell models by turning failures into structured signals rather than debugging events, improving the model's ability to predict perturbation responses.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly represent modeling assumptions as routable structured states, enabling failure signals to pinpoint hypothesis or implementation levels, avoiding mere code fixes while ignoring underlying assumptions.
idea_spark.risk: The separation of hypothesis and implementation spaces may oversimplify complex dependencies in real modeling; structured state representation may increase initial design cost.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by layered architectures and error routing mechanisms in software engineering, modeling is treated as a hypothesis-implementation dual-space iteration rather than a single code debugging loop.

papers.title: Stochastic Differential Dynamic Programming for Trajectory Optimization under Partial Observability
papers.summary: Designing spacecraft trajectories remains challenging in the presence of stochastic effects such as maneuver execution errors and observation uncertainties. Although covariance control and belief-space planning provide useful tools for designing robust control policies and information-aware trajectories under uncertainty, practical methods remain limited for partially observable trajectory optimization problems in which trajectory design, orbit determination, and correction maneuver planning are tightly coupled. This paper presents a stochastic differential dynamic programming algorithm for such coupled problems. The proposed method optimizes the nominal control sequence and feedback gains subject to belief dynamics and general mission constraints, explicitly accounting for the dependence of covariance propagation on the nominal trajectory without relying on the separation principle. Numerical examples demonstrate that the proposed algorithm produces navigation-aware and uncertainty-robust solutions across a range of dynamical systems, observation models, and uncertainty levels. In particular, the circular restricted three-body problem shows that the proposed method can exploit the coupling between trajectory design and orbit determination to obtain navigation-aware solutions with substantially lower fuel consumption than those from deterministic local optimization starting from the same initial guess.

ai.tldr: 提出一种随机微分动态规划算法，用于部分可观测条件下的航天器轨迹优化，能够同时考虑轨迹设计、轨道确定和修正机动规划之间的耦合。
ai.motivation: 现有方法在处理轨迹设计、轨道确定和修正机动规划的耦合问题时存在局限，难以在随机效应下获得鲁棒且导航感知的轨迹。
ai.method: 提出随机微分动态规划算法，在信念动力学和一般任务约束下优化标称控制序列和反馈增益，显式考虑协方差传播对标称轨迹的依赖，不依赖分离原理。
ai.result: 数值实验表明，该算法在多种动力学系统、观测模型和不确定性水平下均能产生导航感知且不确定性鲁棒的轨迹；在圆形限制性三体问题中，相比确定性局部优化，燃料消耗显著降低。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在部分可观测和随机环境下设计更鲁棒、更高效的航天器轨迹。

idea_spark.idea: 将信念动力学与任务约束显式耦合，通过优化反馈增益来主动管理不确定性，而非被动应对。
idea_spark.risk: 算法计算复杂度可能较高，且对模型精度敏感；实际应用中需验证实时性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将轨迹设计与状态估计联合优化，利用耦合关系降低燃料消耗，可推广至多智能体协同规划。

en.tldr: Proposes a stochastic differential dynamic programming algorithm for spacecraft trajectory optimization under partial observability, coupling trajectory design, orbit determination, and correction maneuver planning.
en.motivation: Existing methods struggle with the tight coupling between trajectory design, orbit determination, and correction maneuver planning under stochastic effects, limiting robustness and navigation awareness.
en.method: A stochastic differential dynamic programming algorithm optimizes nominal control sequences and feedback gains subject to belief dynamics and general mission constraints, explicitly accounting for covariance propagation dependence on the nominal trajectory without relying on the separation principle.
en.result: Numerical examples show the algorithm produces navigation-aware and uncertainty-robust solutions across various dynamical systems, observation models, and uncertainty levels; in the circular restricted three-body problem, it achieves substantially lower fuel consumption than deterministic local optimization.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to design more robust and fuel-efficient spacecraft trajectories under partial observability and stochastic effects.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly couple belief dynamics with mission constraints and optimize feedback gains to actively manage uncertainty rather than react to it.
idea_spark.risk: Computational complexity may be high and sensitive to model accuracy; real-time applicability needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires joint optimization of trajectory design and state estimation, leveraging coupling to reduce fuel consumption, extendable to multi-agent cooperative planning.

papers.title: Kurtosis-Guided Denoising Score Matching for Tabular Anomaly Detection
papers.summary: Denoising score matching (DSM) provides a way to learn data distributions by training a neural network to recover the score function, defined as the gradient of the log density, from noise-corrupted samples. Once trained, the score magnitude at a test point reflects how consistent that point is with the learned distribution, making it a natural anomaly signal. The key practical challenge is selecting the perturbation scale: too little noise yields unstable score estimates in sparse regions, while too much erases local structure and weakens anomaly sensitivity. This is compounded by the difficulty of hyperparameter tuning when anomalies are unknown and no validation set is available. We introduce kurtosis-based noise scaling (K-DSM), a per-feature scheme that sets noise levels from the shape of each marginal distribution, improving coverage of low-density regions and precision in high-density regions without extra model complexity. Contrary to prior claims that multi-scale or noise-conditioned training is necessary, we find that a carefully trained single-scale model is already a strong anomaly detector. On standard tabular anomaly detection benchmarks, K-DSM achieves state-of-the-art performance in the semi-supervised setting. When combined with a lightweight EMA-teacher filtering rule that removes low-density training points before each gradient step, it also achieves strong performance in the fully unsupervised (contaminated) setting, suggesting that simple, data-adaptive noise scaling enables robust anomaly detection while reducing reliance on hyperparameter tuning.

ai.tldr: 提出基于峰度的噪声缩放方法，使单尺度去噪分数匹配模型在表格异常检测中达到最优性能，无需多尺度训练或复杂调参。
ai.motivation: 去噪分数匹配用于异常检测时，噪声尺度选择是关键难题：噪声过小导致稀疏区域分数估计不稳定，噪声过大则抹去局部结构、降低异常敏感性。且异常未知、无验证集时超参数调优困难。
ai.method: 提出K-DSM：对每个特征根据其边缘分布的峰度自适应设置噪声水平，改善低密度区域覆盖和高密度区域精度；并结合轻量EMA教师过滤规则，在训练前移除低密度点，实现无监督（污染）场景下的鲁棒检测。
ai.result: 在标准表格异常检测基准上，半监督设置下达到最优；结合EMA过滤后，完全无监督设置下也取得强性能，表明简单数据自适应噪声缩放可减少超参数依赖。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需复杂调参的表格异常检测方法，适用于无验证集或异常未知的实际场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将峰度自适应噪声缩放与EMA教师过滤结合，实现单尺度模型的鲁棒无监督异常检测。
idea_spark.risk: 峰度假设数据分布大致对称，对严重偏态或多峰分布可能效果不佳；EMA过滤可能误删正常低密度点。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用数据分布的形状统计量（峰度）指导噪声尺度，避免繁琐的网格搜索，启发其他超参数自适应方法。

idea_spark.risk: 峰度假设数据分布大致对称，对严重偏态或多峰分布可能效果不佳；EMA过滤可能误删正常低密度点。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用数据分布的形状统计量（峰度）指导噪声尺度，避免繁琐的网格搜索，启发其他超参数自适应方法。

en.tldr: Proposes kurtosis-based noise scaling that enables a single-scale denoising score matching model to achieve state-of-the-art tabular anomaly detection without multi-scale training or heavy hyperparameter tuning.
en.motivation: In denoising score matching for anomaly detection, selecting the perturbation scale is critical: too little noise yields unstable score estimates in sparse regions, too much erases local structure and weakens sensitivity. Hyperparameter tuning is difficult when anomalies are unknown and no validation set is available.
en.method: Introduces K-DSM: per-feature noise levels set by the kurtosis of each marginal distribution to improve coverage of low-density regions and precision in high-density regions. Combined with a lightweight EMA-teacher filtering rule that removes low-density training points before each gradient step for robust unsupervised (contaminated) detection.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard tabular anomaly detection benchmarks in the semi-supervised setting; strong performance in the fully unsupervised setting when combined with EMA filtering, showing that simple data-adaptive noise scaling reduces hyperparameter reliance.
en.help_to_user: Provides a tabular anomaly detection method that requires minimal hyperparameter tuning and works well in practical scenarios with no validation set or unknown anomalies.

idea_spark.idea: Combine kurtosis-adaptive noise scaling with EMA teacher filtering to achieve robust unsupervised anomaly detection with a single-scale model.
idea_spark.risk: Kurtosis assumes roughly symmetric distributions; may underperform on heavily skewed or multimodal data. EMA filtering might accidentally remove normal low-density points.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using distribution shape statistics (kurtosis) to guide noise scales avoids exhaustive grid search, inspiring other hyperparameter-adaptive methods.

papers.title: Scalable Distributed Stochastic Optimization via Bidirectional Compression: Beyond Pessimistic Limits
papers.summary: In centralized, distributed, and federated learning with stochastic gradients and $n$ workers, it was recently shown that it is infeasible to find an $\varepsilon$-stationary point faster than $\tildeΩ\left(\min\left\{\frac{d κL Δ}{\varepsilon} + \frac{h L Δ}{\varepsilon} + \frac{h σ^2 L Δ}{n \varepsilon^2},\; \frac{h σ^2 L Δ}{\varepsilon^2} + \frac{h L Δ}{\varepsilon}\right\}\right)$ seconds in both homogeneous and heterogeneous settings under standard assumptions: $L$-smoothness, $σ^2$-bounded unbiased stochastic gradients, and lower boundedness of the function, i.e., $f(x) \geq f^*$ for all $x \in \mathbb{R}^d$, where $Δ= f(x^0) - f^*$, $h$ is the computation time, $κ$ is the communication speed between the workers and the server, and $d$ is the dimension of the iterates and gradients. This result is pessimistic since it does not allow a complexity in which both $\frac{d κL Δ}{\varepsilon}$ and $\frac{h σ^2 L Δ}{\varepsilon^2}$ improve with $n$, even when using random sparsification techniques; moreover, this lower bound can be matched by either non-distributed SGD or vanilla Synchronous SGD, which reduces the impact of recent progress in the design of compression-based methods. In this work, we challenge this limitation and propose new compressed methods, Inkheart SGD and M4, and show that under an additional structural assumption, which is necessary due to the lower bound and which does not restrict the class of considered problems, we achieve new state-of-the-art time complexities that break this pessimistic barrier and allow scaling with the number of workers $n$.

ai.tldr: 提出两种新的压缩方法Inkheart SGD和M4，在额外结构假设下打破了分布式随机优化中已知的悲观下界，实现了随工人数n扩展的最优时间复杂度。
ai.motivation: 现有下界表明，在标准假设下无法通过压缩技术同时改善与维度和噪声相关的复杂度项，导致非分布式SGD或同步SGD即可匹配该下界，限制了压缩方法的实际收益。
ai.method: 引入额外结构假设（不限制问题类别），设计Inkheart SGD和M4两种双向压缩算法，在中心化、分布式和联邦学习场景中实现新的时间复杂度。
ai.result: 新方法的时间复杂度突破了悲观下界，允许两个关键项随工人数n同时改善，达到了新的最优复杂度。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过压缩技术突破分布式优化中的理论瓶颈，并指导实际系统设计中选择更高效的通信策略。

idea_spark.idea: 利用问题结构（如梯度稀疏性）设计自适应压缩率，使压缩误差随迭代动态调整，从而绕过已知下界。
idea_spark.risk: 结构假设可能在实际数据中不成立，导致理论保证失效；压缩引入的额外方差可能影响收敛稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受信息论下界与算法匹配的启发，通过引入必要且合理的结构假设，重新审视压缩方法的潜力。

idea_spark.idea: 利用问题结构（如梯度稀疏性）设计自适应压缩率，使压缩误差随迭代动态调整，从而绕过已知下界。
idea_spark.risk: 结构假设可能在实际数据中不成立，导致理论保证失效；压缩引入的额外方差可能影响收敛稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受信息论下界与算法匹配的启发，通过引入必要且合理的结构假设，重新审视压缩方法的潜力。

en.tldr: Proposes two new compressed methods, Inkheart SGD and M4, which break the known pessimistic lower bound in distributed stochastic optimization under an additional structural assumption, achieving state-of-the-art time complexities that scale with the number of workers n.
en.motivation: Existing lower bounds show that under standard assumptions, compression techniques cannot simultaneously improve both the dimension-dependent and noise-dependent complexity terms, making non-distributed SGD or vanilla synchronous SGD sufficient to match the bound, thus limiting the practical impact of compression-based methods.
en.method: Introduces an additional structural assumption (without restricting the problem class) and designs two bidirectional compression algorithms, Inkheart SGD and M4, for centralized, distributed, and federated learning settings to achieve new time complexities.
en.result: The new time complexities break the pessimistic barrier, allowing both key terms to improve with the number of workers n, achieving new state-of-the-art complexities.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to break theoretical bottlenecks in distributed optimization via compression, guiding the design of more communication-efficient strategies in practical systems.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage problem structure (e.g., gradient sparsity) to design adaptive compression rates that dynamically adjust compression error across iterations, thereby circumventing known lower bounds.
idea_spark.risk: The structural assumption may not hold in real data, causing theoretical guarantees to fail; compression-induced additional variance may affect convergence stability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the interplay between information-theoretic lower bounds and algorithm design, re-examine the potential of compression methods by introducing necessary and reasonable structural assumptions.

papers.title: Lifespan estimate for one dimensional wave equation with semilinear terms of spatial derivative
papers.summary: This paper studies the upper and lower bounds of the lifespan for the classical solutions to the initial value problems of one dimensional wave equations with non-autonomous semilinear terms including the space-derivative of the unknown function.This is a non-trivial business comparing to the analogous results with time-derivative type semilinear terms, especially for the proof to obtain the sharp upper bound of the lifespan as we have to deal with space dependent weights among iteration procedures of the weighted functional of the solution. Also it is surprising that a part of them reaches to the same ordinary differential inequality for classical semilinear damped wave equations introduced by Li and Zhou (Discrete Contin. Dynam. Systems, 1995, 1(4): 503-520), and we show a simple proof for blow up result from this ordinary differential inequality by iteration argument and slicing method in more general situation.

ai.tldr: 研究一维波动方程含空间导数半线性项的解的生命跨度上下界，并证明部分情形下与经典阻尼波动方程相同的常微分不等式。
ai.motivation: 现有结果多针对时间导数型半线性项，空间导数型因迭代中空间依赖权重而更复杂，需建立生命跨度估计。
ai.method: 通过加权泛函迭代和切片方法，从常微分不等式推导爆破，并给出上下界证明。
ai.result: 获得了生命跨度的尖锐上界，并发现与Li-Zhou经典结果一致的常微分不等式。
ai.help_to_user: 为含空间导数半线性项的波动方程爆破分析提供通用框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将空间导数项转化为等效时间导数型不等式，简化爆破证明。
idea_spark.risk: 空间依赖权重可能破坏迭代收敛性，需额外正则性条件。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴阻尼波动方程的常微分不等式技巧，拓展至非自治情形。

en.tldr: Studies upper and lower lifespan bounds for classical solutions of 1D wave equations with semilinear terms involving spatial derivatives, and shows that some cases reduce to the same ODE inequality as for damped wave equations.
en.motivation: Existing results focus on time-derivative semilinear terms; spatial-derivative terms are more challenging due to space-dependent weights in iteration, requiring lifespan estimates.
en.method: Uses weighted functional iteration and slicing method to derive blow-up from an ODE inequality, and proves upper/lower bounds.
en.result: Obtains sharp upper lifespan bounds and identifies an ODE inequality identical to the classical Li-Zhou result for damped wave equations.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general framework for blow-up analysis of wave equations with spatial-derivative semilinear terms.

idea_spark.idea: Transform spatial-derivative terms into equivalent time-derivative-type inequalities to simplify blow-up proofs.
idea_spark.risk: Space-dependent weights may break iteration convergence, requiring extra regularity conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrow ODE inequality techniques from damped wave equations and adapt them to non-autonomous settings.

papers.title: Noise-Driven Free Boundaries In The Compressible Navier-Stokes Equations
papers.summary: A stochastic free-boundary problem for the three-dimensional barotropic compressible Navier--Stokes equations is studied. The main feature of the model is that the free boundary is transported by a Stratonovich stochastic flow, so that the noise enters the kinematic boundary condition and hence the evolution of the moving domain. An additional Itô forcing in the momentum equation is also allowed. The problem is transformed by a stochastic Lagrangian map generated by the velocity and the transport vector fields. In these coordinates the density is represented through the Jacobian of the flow, and the remaining system is solved by combining stochastic maximal regularity, deterministic %\rL^p%-%\rL^q$ estimates, and a localized contraction argument. Local pathwise well-posedness is obtained up to an a.s. positive stopping time, with strictly positive density and pathwise uniqueness.

ai.tldr: 研究三维可压缩Navier-Stokes方程中由噪声驱动的自由边界问题，通过随机拉格朗日映射和随机极大正则性理论，证明了局部路径适定性。
ai.motivation: 探索随机噪声如何影响可压缩流体自由边界的演化，为随机流体力学提供理论基础。
ai.method: 将自由边界问题转化为随机拉格朗日坐标系，结合随机极大正则性、确定性Lp-Lq估计和局部压缩论证。
ai.result: 在正密度和路径唯一性条件下，得到局部路径适定性，停止时间几乎必然为正。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解随机噪声在可压缩流体自由边界问题中的数学处理方法和适定性结果。

idea_spark.idea: 考虑将噪声引入边界条件而非方程本身，可能简化随机流体模型的数值模拟。
idea_spark.risk: 随机自由边界问题的全局适定性可能难以保证，需要更精细的随机分析工具。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受随机Navier-Stokes方程和确定性自由边界问题研究的启发，结合两者优势。

en.tldr: A stochastic free-boundary problem for the 3D barotropic compressible Navier-Stokes equations is studied, where the free boundary is driven by a Stratonovich stochastic flow. Local pathwise well-posedness is proved via stochastic Lagrangian map and stochastic maximal regularity.
en.motivation: To understand how stochastic noise influences the evolution of free boundaries in compressible fluids, providing a theoretical foundation for stochastic fluid dynamics.
en.method: Transform the problem using a stochastic Lagrangian map generated by velocity and transport fields, then combine stochastic maximal regularity, deterministic Lp-Lq estimates, and a localized contraction argument.
en.result: Local pathwise well-posedness up to an almost surely positive stopping time, with strictly positive density and pathwise uniqueness.
en.help_to_user: Provides mathematical tools and well-posedness results for stochastic free-boundary problems in compressible fluid dynamics.

idea_spark.idea: Consider introducing noise only in the boundary condition rather than the equation, which may simplify numerical simulations of stochastic fluid models.
idea_spark.risk: Global well-posedness of stochastic free-boundary problems may be challenging and require more refined stochastic analysis tools.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by studies of stochastic Navier-Stokes equations and deterministic free-boundary problems, combining their strengths.

papers.title: Have Graph -- Will Lift? The Case for Higher-Order Benchmarks
papers.summary: After a somewhat rocky start, geometry and topology have established a foothold in machine learning. Message passing, either on graphs or higher-order complexes, is one of the main drivers of geometric deep learning, and paradigms that were once considered to be firmly in the realm of the abstract-like sheaves-have been "tamed" to serve as novel inductive biases for model architectures in topological deep learning. The veritable diversity of models, however, is in stark contrast to the scarcity of suitable benchmark datasets. As a result, researchers often resort to lifting existing graph datasets to include higher-order information. In this opinion paper, I want to encourage the community to also source new datasets, which may be used to prop up the foundations of our research field.

ai.tldr: 本文指出拓扑深度学习模型多样但缺乏合适的基准数据集，呼吁社区在提升现有图数据集的同时，积极收集新数据集以支撑该领域发展。
ai.motivation: 拓扑深度学习模型日益丰富，但缺乏足够的高阶基准数据集，导致研究者只能依赖对现有图数据集的提升，限制了领域基础。
ai.method: 作为观点文章，作者通过分析现状，提出应同时收集新数据集而非仅依赖图数据提升。
ai.result: 强调新数据集对巩固拓扑深度学习研究基础的重要性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解拓扑深度学习领域的数据集瓶颈，并启发其关注高阶基准的构建。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一个包含真实高阶交互（如超图、单纯复形）的基准数据集，用于评估拓扑深度学习模型。
idea_spark.risk: 新数据集可能难以覆盖所有高阶结构类型，且收集成本较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从生物网络（如蛋白质相互作用）或物理系统（如多体问题）中提取天然高阶数据。

en.tldr: This opinion paper highlights the scarcity of suitable benchmark datasets for topological deep learning and urges the community to source new datasets rather than only lifting existing graph datasets.
en.motivation: The diversity of topological deep learning models contrasts sharply with the lack of appropriate higher-order benchmarks, forcing researchers to rely on lifting graph datasets.
en.method: As an opinion piece, the author analyzes the current situation and advocates for collecting new datasets to support the field.
en.result: Emphasizes the importance of new datasets for strengthening the foundations of topological deep learning research.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the dataset bottleneck in topological deep learning and encourages attention to higher-order benchmark construction.

idea_spark.idea: Design a benchmark dataset containing real higher-order interactions (e.g., hypergraphs, simplicial complexes) for evaluating topological deep learning models.
idea_spark.risk: New datasets may not cover all higher-order structure types and could be costly to collect.
idea_spark.inspiration: Extract naturally higher-order data from biological networks (e.g., protein interactions) or physical systems (e.g., many-body problems).

papers.title: TRACE: Transport Alignment Conformal Prediction via Diffusion and Flow Matching Models
papers.summary: Constructing valid and informative conformal prediction regions for multi-dimensional outputs remains a fundamental challenge. While conformal prediction provides finite-sample, distribution-free coverage guarantees, its practical performance critically depends on the choice of nonconformity score. Existing approaches often rely on restrictive geometric assumptions or require explicit likelihood evaluation and invertible transformations, limiting their applicability in complex generative settings.   In this work, we introduce TRACE (TRansport Alignment Conformal Estimation), a conformal prediction framework that defines nonconformity through transport alignment in diffusion and flow matching models. Rather than evaluating likelihoods, we measure how well a candidate output aligns with the learned generative dynamics by averaging denoising or velocity-matching errors along stochastic transport trajectories. The resulting transport-based scores are scalar-valued and can be calibrated using split conformal prediction, yielding valid marginal coverage under exchangeability. We further analyze the statistical properties of the proposed scores and their sensitivity to computational budget. Experiments on synthetic and real datasets demonstrate valid coverage and show that the resulting regions adapt naturally to multimodal and non-convex conditional distributions.

ai.tldr: 提出TRACE框架，利用扩散和流匹配模型中的传输对齐定义非一致性分数，实现多输出共形预测。
ai.motivation: 现有共形预测方法依赖严格几何假设或可逆变换，难以适用于复杂生成模型。
ai.method: 通过平均去噪或速度匹配误差沿随机传输轨迹衡量候选输出与生成动力学的对齐程度，得到标量非一致性分数，并用分割共形预测校准。
ai.result: 在合成和真实数据集上实现有效覆盖，区域自适应于多模态和非凸条件分布。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需显式似然评估的共形预测方法，适用于扩散和流匹配模型。

idea_spark.idea: 将传输对齐分数与变分推断结合，提升高维输出预测效率。
idea_spark.risk: 计算预算敏感，可能在高分辨率数据上成本较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用生成动力学轨迹作为非一致性度量，替代传统似然或距离度量。

en.tldr: Proposes TRACE, a conformal prediction framework using transport alignment in diffusion and flow matching models to define nonconformity scores for multi-output prediction.
en.motivation: Existing conformal prediction methods rely on restrictive geometric assumptions or invertible transformations, limiting applicability in complex generative settings.
en.method: Measures alignment of candidate outputs with learned generative dynamics by averaging denoising or velocity-matching errors along stochastic transport trajectories, yielding scalar scores calibrated via split conformal prediction.
en.result: Valid coverage on synthetic and real datasets, with regions adapting naturally to multimodal and non-convex conditional distributions.
en.help_to_user: Provides a conformal prediction method without explicit likelihood evaluation, suitable for diffusion and flow matching models.

idea_spark.idea: Combine transport alignment scores with variational inference for efficient high-dimensional output prediction.
idea_spark.risk: Sensitive to computational budget, potentially costly for high-resolution data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Use generative dynamics trajectories as nonconformity measures, replacing traditional likelihood or distance metrics.

papers.title: BeeVe: Unsupervised Acoustic State Discovery in Honey Bee Buzzing
papers.summary: Discovering structure in biological signals without supervision is a fundamental problem in computational intelligence, yet existing bioacoustic methods assume vocal production models or predefined semantic units, leaving non-vocal species poorly served. This work introduces BeeVe, an unsupervised framework for acoustic state discovery in collective honey bee buzzing. BeeVe uses the self-supervised Patchout Spectrogram Transformer (PaSST) as a frozen feature extractor, then trains a Vector-Quantized Variational Autoencoder (VQ-VAE) without labels on those embeddings, learning a finite discrete codebook of acoustic tokens directly from unlabelled hive audio. No labels, pretext tasks, or contrastive objectives are used at any stage. Post-hoc evaluation against known queen status reveals that the learned tokens separate queenright and queenless conditions with Jensen-Shannon Divergence values between 0.609 and 0.688, and that the queenless condition further decomposes into three internally coherent sub-states stable across experiments with different codebook sizes and random seeds. Token transition analysis confirms non-random sequential structure (p << 0.001) across all experiments. Generalisation to unseen recordings preserves both token overlap (Jaccard = 0.947) and global manifold topology. These results demonstrate that unsupervised discrete codebook learning can recover repeatable acoustic structure from a non-vocal biological signal without annotation, opening a path toward non-invasive acoustic hive health monitoring.

ai.tldr: 提出BeeVe框架，无需标注即可从蜂群嗡嗡声中自动发现离散声学状态，并用于蜂巢健康监测。
ai.motivation: 现有生物声学方法依赖发声模型或预定义语义单元，无法处理非发声物种（如蜜蜂）的集体嗡嗡声。
ai.method: 使用自监督Patchout Spectrogram Transformer（PaSST）作为冻结特征提取器，再训练无标签的向量量化变分自编码器（VQ-VAE），直接从未标注的蜂巢音频中学习离散声学令牌。
ai.result: 学习到的令牌能区分有王与无王蜂群（JSD 0.609–0.688），无王状态进一步分解为三个稳定子状态；令牌转移序列具有显著非随机结构（p<<0.001）；对未见录音泛化良好（Jaccard=0.947）。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需标注即可从非发声生物信号中提取可重复声学结构的无监督方法，可用于蜂巢健康非侵入式监测。

idea_spark.idea: 将VQ-VAE与自监督Transformer结合，从连续音频中自动发现离散状态，无需任何标签或对比学习。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖PaSST预训练模型，可能对域外音频（如不同蜂种或环境噪声）泛化能力有限；离散令牌的语义解释性仍需人工验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受语音处理中无监督离散表示学习启发，将类似思路应用于非发声生物信号，开辟了无标注生物声学分析新路径。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖PaSST预训练模型，可能对域外音频（如不同蜂种或环境噪声）泛化能力有限；离散令牌的语义解释性仍需人工验证。

en.tldr: BeeVe is an unsupervised framework that discovers discrete acoustic states from honey bee buzzing without labels, enabling hive health monitoring.
en.motivation: Existing bioacoustic methods rely on vocal production models or predefined semantic units, failing for non-vocal species like honey bees.
en.method: BeeVe uses a frozen self-supervised Patchout Spectrogram Transformer (PaSST) as feature extractor, then trains a Vector-Quantized Variational Autoencoder (VQ-VAE) on those embeddings to learn a discrete codebook of acoustic tokens from unlabeled hive audio.
en.result: Learned tokens separate queenright and queenless conditions (JSD 0.609–0.688), queenless state decomposes into three stable sub-states; token transitions show non-random structure (p<<0.001); generalization to unseen recordings is high (Jaccard=0.947).
en.help_to_user: Provides a label-free method to extract repeatable acoustic structure from non-vocal biological signals, applicable to non-invasive hive health monitoring.

idea_spark.idea: Combine VQ-VAE with self-supervised Transformer to discover discrete states from continuous audio without any labels or contrastive objectives.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on PaSST pretrained model may limit generalization to out-of-domain audio; semantic interpretability of discrete tokens requires manual validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by unsupervised discrete representation learning in speech processing, applied to non-vocal biological signals, opening a new path for annotation-free bioacoustics.

papers.title: Primitive Sequences for Probability Measures on Compact Intervals
papers.summary: We introduce a sequence representation of a random variable $X$ supported on a compact interval $[a,b]$, which we call a primitive sequence. We construct this sequence by repeatedly antidifferentiating the associated cumulative distribution function of $X$ and evaluating the antiderivatives at the endpoint $b$. We show that the primitive sequence of $X$ can be identified as a factorially rescaled moment sequence of the reflected random variable $b-X$. Through this identification, we show that the primitive sequence transparently captures qualitative features of the distribution of $X$. We then connect primitive sequences directly to classical moment theory and exploit this connection to characterize admissible primitive sequences and to show that under natural topologies, the map from probability measures to primitive sequences is a homeomorphism. We end by examining the set of probability measures whose first $m$ primitive sequence terms are fixed, and thereby obtaining sharp upper and lower bounds on two functionals of those measures.

ai.tldr: 提出了一种紧区间上概率测度的原始序列表示，并建立了与经典矩理论的联系。
ai.motivation: 为紧区间上的随机变量提供一种新的序列表示，以透明地捕捉分布特征。
ai.method: 通过反复对累积分布函数进行反微分并在端点b处求值构造原始序列，并利用反射随机变量的矩序列进行识别。
ai.result: 证明了原始序列与反射随机变量的阶乘缩放矩序列等价，刻画了可容许原始序列，并证明了概率测度到原始序列的映射是同胚。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解紧区间上概率分布的一种新表示方法及其与矩理论的关系。

idea_spark.idea: 利用原始序列的固定前m项对概率测度进行约束，从而获得两个泛函的尖锐上下界。
idea_spark.risk: 原始序列的构造依赖于反微分，可能对分布的光滑性有隐含要求。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将分布表示与经典矩问题结合，为分布约束优化提供新工具。

en.tldr: Introduces a primitive sequence representation for probability measures on compact intervals and connects it to classical moment theory.
en.motivation: To provide a new sequence representation for random variables on compact intervals that transparently captures distributional features.
en.method: Constructs the primitive sequence by repeatedly antidifferentiating the cumulative distribution function and evaluating at endpoint b, then identifies it with a factorially rescaled moment sequence of the reflected variable.
en.result: Shows the primitive sequence equals the factorial-rescaled moment sequence of b-X, characterizes admissible sequences, and proves the map from measures to sequences is a homeomorphism.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand a novel representation of probability distributions on compact intervals and its connection to moment theory.

idea_spark.idea: Fixing the first m primitive sequence terms yields sharp bounds on two functionals of the probability measure.
idea_spark.risk: The construction relies on antidifferentiation, which may impose smoothness assumptions on the distribution.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining distribution representation with classical moment problems offers new tools for constrained distribution optimization.

papers.title: Medical Imaging Classification with Cold-Atom Reservoir Computing using Auto-Encoders and Surrogate-Driven Training
papers.summary: We introduce a hybrid quantum-classical pipeline, based on neutral-atom reservoir computing, for medical image classification, focusing on the binary classification task of polyp detection. To deal effectively with the high dimensionality, we integrate a guided auto-encoder. This pipeline learns compact and discriminative representations of image data that are also well-suited for quantum reservoir computing. A key challenge in such systems is the non-differentiable nature of quantum measurements, which creates a 'gradient barrier' for standard training. We overcome this barrier by incorporating a differentiable surrogate model that emulates the quantum layer, enabling end-to-end backpropagation through the entire system. This guided training process is jointly optimized for classification accuracy and for faithful image recovery from the auto-encoder. The learned latent representations are encoded as pulse detuning parameters within a Rydberg Hamiltonian, and quantum embeddings are subsequently obtained through expectation values. These embeddings are then passed to a linear classifier. Our simulations show that this method outperforms some traditional approaches that use PCA or unguided autoencoders. We also conduct ablation studies to assess the impact of various quantum and training parameters, demonstrating the robustness and flexibility of our proposed pipeline for real-world medical imaging applications, even in the current NISQ era.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于冷原子储层计算的混合量子-经典流水线，用于医学图像分类，通过引导自编码器和可微替代模型克服量子测量的梯度障碍。
ai.motivation: 医学图像分类中高维数据难以直接用于量子储层计算，且量子测量不可微导致训练困难。
ai.method: 使用引导自编码器学习紧凑判别表示，通过可微替代模型模拟量子层实现端到端反向传播，将潜在表示编码为Rydberg哈密顿量的脉冲失谐参数。
ai.result: 在息肉检测任务上优于PCA或非引导自编码器方法，消融实验验证了鲁棒性和灵活性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种在NISQ时代利用量子计算进行医学图像分类的实用方案。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将替代模型替换为物理感知的神经网络，以更精确模拟量子系统行为。
idea_spark.risk: 替代模型可能引入近似误差，导致量子层真实性能与模拟不一致。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受可微代理在强化学习中应用的启发，将不可微量子测量转化为可训练模块。

en.tldr: Proposes a hybrid quantum-classical pipeline based on cold-atom reservoir computing for medical image classification, using a guided auto-encoder and a differentiable surrogate model to overcome the gradient barrier of quantum measurements.
en.motivation: High-dimensional medical image data is challenging for quantum reservoir computing, and non-differentiable quantum measurements hinder standard training.
en.method: Integrates a guided auto-encoder to learn compact discriminative representations, employs a differentiable surrogate model to emulate the quantum layer for end-to-end backpropagation, and encodes latent representations as pulse detuning parameters in a Rydberg Hamiltonian.
en.result: Outperforms traditional approaches using PCA or unguided auto-encoders on polyp detection; ablation studies confirm robustness and flexibility.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical pipeline for leveraging quantum computing in medical imaging classification within the NISQ era.

idea_spark.idea: Explore replacing the surrogate model with a physics-aware neural network to more accurately simulate quantum system behavior.
idea_spark.risk: The surrogate model may introduce approximation errors, causing discrepancies between simulated and real quantum layer performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by differentiable proxies in reinforcement learning, transforming non-differentiable quantum measurements into trainable modules.

papers.title: Blow-up of solutions to semilinear parabolic equations driven by mixed local-nonlocal operators with large initial data
papers.summary: We investigate finite-time blow-up for nonnegative solutions to the Cauchy problem associated with semilinear parabolic equations driven by a mixed local--nonlocal operator. The reaction term is assumed to satisfy suitable structural hypotheses, the prototype being $f(u)=u^p$ with $p>1$. By adapting the Kaplan method to the present framework, we prove that solutions blow up in finite time whenever the initial datum is sufficiently large. In the prototype case $f(u)=u^p$, this conclusion holds for every $p>1$. As a particular case of our operator, we also include the fractional Laplacian; to the best of our knowledge, this type of result is new even in that special case.

ai.tldr: 研究由混合局部-非局部算子驱动的半线性抛物方程的非负解在有限时间内爆破的条件，证明当初始数据足够大时解必然爆破。
ai.motivation: 混合局部-非局部算子在物理和数学中广泛出现，但关于其驱动的抛物方程解的爆破行为尚缺乏系统研究。
ai.method: 将经典的Kaplan方法推广到混合算子框架，通过构造合适的测试函数和能量估计来证明有限时间爆破。
ai.result: 对于满足结构假设的反应项（如f(u)=u^p, p>1），只要初始数据足够大，解必然在有限时间内爆破；该结果对分数阶拉普拉斯算子也是新的。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究混合非局部算子的爆破问题提供了理论工具和判据。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试将Kaplan方法与非线性比较原理结合，处理更一般的反应项或非局部扩散项。
idea_spark.risk: 对于非局部算子，测试函数的构造和能量估计可能依赖于算子的具体形式，推广需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 混合算子的结构可能允许同时利用局部和非局部的分析技巧，为爆破问题提供新视角。

zh.tldr: 研究由混合局部-非局部算子驱动的半线性抛物方程的非负解在有限时间内爆破的条件，证明当初始数据足够大时解必然爆破。
zh.motivation: 混合局部-非局部算子在物理和数学中广泛出现，但关于其驱动的抛物方程解的爆破行为尚缺乏系统研究。

zh.help_to_user: 为研究混合非局部算子的爆破问题提供了理论工具和判据。

idea_spark.risk: 对于非局部算子，测试函数的构造和能量估计可能依赖于算子的具体形式，推广需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 混合算子的结构可能允许同时利用局部和非局部的分析技巧，为爆破问题提供新视角。

en.tldr: We study finite-time blow-up for nonnegative solutions of semilinear parabolic equations driven by a mixed local-nonlocal operator, proving that blow-up occurs for sufficiently large initial data.
en.motivation: Mixed local-nonlocal operators appear in various physical and mathematical contexts, yet the blow-up behavior of solutions to the associated parabolic equations is not well understood.
en.method: We adapt the classical Kaplan method to the mixed operator framework, constructing suitable test functions and energy estimates to prove finite-time blow-up.
en.result: Under structural assumptions on the reaction term (e.g., f(u)=u^p, p>1), solutions blow up in finite time whenever the initial datum is sufficiently large; the result is new even for the fractional Laplacian.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical tools and criteria for studying blow-up problems involving mixed nonlocal operators.

idea_spark.idea: Combine Kaplan's method with nonlinear comparison principles to handle more general reaction terms or nonlocal diffusion operators.
idea_spark.risk: For nonlocal operators, the construction of test functions and energy estimates may depend heavily on the specific operator, requiring careful generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: The mixed operator structure may allow simultaneous use of local and nonlocal analytical techniques, offering new perspectives on blow-up problems.

papers.title: foap4: Adaptive mesh refinement with OpenACC, MPI, and p4est
papers.summary: GPUs and other accelerators are increasingly used for scientific computing. In the future, we want to add GPU support to parallel adaptive mesh refinement (AMR) codes written in Fortran. To understand which changes are necessary to obtain good performance we have developed foap4, an AMR framework implemented in Fortran that uses OpenACC, MPI, and the p4est library. We discuss the design and implementation of the framework. Several benchmark problems are considered, in which Euler's equations of gas dynamics are solved using explicit time integration. These benchmarks are performed in both 2D and 3D, using static and adaptive meshes, for varying problem sizes on different hardware. Our results show that AMR simulations can be carried out efficiently on GPUs with OpenACC and MPI, even when using relatively small grid blocks of $8^3$ or $16^3$ cells.

ai.tldr: 开发了基于Fortran、OpenACC、MPI和p4est的自适应网格细化框架foap4，实现了GPU上的高效AMR模拟。
ai.motivation: 未来希望在Fortran编写的并行AMR代码中支持GPU，需了解实现高性能所需的变化。
ai.method: 使用Fortran、OpenACC、MPI和p4est库实现AMR框架foap4，求解欧拉方程，采用显式时间积分。
ai.result: 在2D和3D中，静态和自适应网格下，不同硬件和问题规模上均能高效运行，即使使用8^3或16^3的小网格块。
ai.help_to_user: 展示了如何用OpenACC和MPI在GPU上高效实现AMR，为Fortran代码的GPU移植提供参考。

idea_spark.idea: 结合p4est的树结构，设计更灵活的网格块大小自适应策略以优化GPU利用率。
idea_spark.risk: 小网格块可能限制GPU并行度，需平衡通信与计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从p4est的并行树管理中获得启发，可应用于其他自适应数据结构。

zh.tldr: 开发了基于Fortran、OpenACC、MPI和p4est的自适应网格细化框架foap4，实现了GPU上的高效AMR模拟。

zh.help_to_user: 展示了如何用OpenACC和MPI在GPU上高效实现AMR，为Fortran代码的GPU移植提供参考。
