en.tldr: Introduces TajPersLexon, a Tajik-Persian parallel lexical resource of 40,112 pairs, and benchmarks hybrid, neural, and retrieval methods on CPU, achieving 98-99% top-1 accuracy for neural/retrieval baselines and 96.4% accuracy for the hybrid model in OCR post-correction.
en.motivation: Tajik and Persian share high lexical similarity but use different scripts; large multilingual sentence transformers fail on exact lexical matching, motivating a specialized low-resource cross-script resource and method.
en.method: Constructed a parallel lexical resource of 40,112 word/short-phrase pairs and compared three method families: lightweight hybrid pipeline, neural seq2seq models, and retrieval methods, all on CPU with fixed random seeds.
en.result: Neural and retrieval baselines achieve 98-99% top-1 accuracy; the hybrid model achieves 96.4% accuracy on OCR post-correction, offering a favorable accuracy-efficiency trade-off.
en.help_to_user: Provides a benchmark dataset and reproducible experiments for Tajik-Persian cross-script NLP tasks, and recommends the interpretable hybrid model for practical applications.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the hybrid model with OCR post-correction to build an end-to-end cross-script document digitization system for historical texts or multilingual regions.
idea_spark.risk: The hybrid model relies on handcrafted rules/features, which may not generalize to languages with larger lexical divergence; the dataset size is limited.
idea_spark.inspiration: In low-resource settings, interpretable lightweight methods can outperform large pretrained models, inspiring a focus on efficiency-accuracy balance.

papers.title: LLMs Improving LLMs: Agentic Discovery for Test-Time Scaling
papers.summary: Test-time scaling (TTS) has become an effective approach for improving large language model performance by allocating additional computation during inference. However, existing TTS strategies are largely hand-crafted: researchers manually design reasoning patterns and tune heuristics by intuition, leaving much of the computation-allocation space unexplored. We propose an environment-driven framework, AutoTTS, that changes what researchers design: from individual TTS heuristics to environments where TTS strategies can be discovered automatically. The key to AutoTTS lies in environment construction: the discovery environment must make the control space tractable and provide cheap, frequent feedback for TTS search. As a concrete instantiation, we formulate width--depth TTS as controller synthesis over pre-collected reasoning trajectories and probe signals, where controllers decide when to branch, continue, probe, prune, or stop and can be evaluated cheaply without repeated LLM calls. We further introduce beta parameterization to make the search tractable and fine-grained execution trace feedback to improve discovery efficiency by helping the agent diagnose why a TTS program fails. Experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks show that the discovered strategies improve the overall accuracy--cost tradeoff over strong manually designed baselines. The discovered strategies generalize to held-out benchmarks and model scales, while the entire discovery costs only $39.9 and 160 minutes. Our data, and code will be open-source at https://github.com/zhengkid/AutoTTS.

ai.tldr: 提出AutoTTS框架，通过环境驱动自动发现测试时缩放策略，提升大语言模型推理效率。
ai.motivation: 现有测试时缩放策略依赖人工设计，探索不充分。
ai.method: 构建发现环境，将宽度-深度TTS转化为控制器合成问题，使用beta参数化和执行轨迹反馈优化搜索。
ai.result: 在数学推理基准上优于手工基线，泛化到未见基准和模型规模，发现成本仅39.9美元和160分钟。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户自动发现更优的测试时缩放策略，节省人工调参成本。

idea_spark.idea: 将TTS策略搜索建模为控制器合成，利用执行轨迹反馈加速搜索。
idea_spark.risk: 环境设计可能无法覆盖所有有效策略空间，反馈信号可能不充分。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从手工设计策略转向设计发现环境，启发自动化机器学习新范式。

en.tldr: Proposes AutoTTS, an environment-driven framework for automatically discovering test-time scaling strategies to improve LLM inference efficiency.
en.motivation: Existing test-time scaling strategies are hand-crafted, leaving much of the computation-allocation space unexplored.
en.method: Constructs a discovery environment that formulates width-depth TTS as controller synthesis, using beta parameterization and execution trace feedback to optimize search.
en.result: Outperforms strong manually designed baselines on math reasoning benchmarks, generalizes to held-out benchmarks and model scales, with discovery cost of only $39.9 and 160 minutes.
en.help_to_user: Helps users automatically discover better test-time scaling strategies, saving manual tuning effort.

idea_spark.idea: Model TTS strategy search as controller synthesis, leveraging execution trace feedback to accelerate search.
idea_spark.risk: Environment design may not cover all effective strategy spaces, and feedback signals may be insufficient.
idea_spark.inspiration: Shift from manually designing strategies to designing discovery environments, inspiring new paradigms in automated machine learning.

papers.title: SARA: Semantically Adaptive Relational Alignment for Video Diffusion Models
papers.summary: Recent video diffusion models (VDMs) synthesize visually convincing clips, yet still drop entities, mis-bind attributes, and weaken the interactions specified in the prompt. Representation-alignment objectives such as VideoREPA and MoAlign improve fine-grained text following by distilling spatio-temporal token relations from a frozen visual foundation model, but their pairwise supervision budget is allocated by visual or motion cues rather than by how relevant each pair is to the prompt. We present SARA, Semantically Adaptive Relational Alignment, which keeps token-relation distillation (TRD) on a frozen VFM target and adds a text-conditioned saliency that decides which token pairs carry supervision. A lightweight Stage 1 aligner is trained with per-entity SAM 3.1 mask supervision and an InfoNCE regulariser, and its continuous saliency is fused into TRD through a pair-routing operator that assigns each token pair a weight whenever either of its two endpoints is salient, thereby routing supervision toward subject-subject and subject-background pairs and away from background-background ones. In the Wan2.2 continual-training setting, SARA improves both text alignment and motion quality over SFT, VideoREPA, and MoAlign on a 13-dimension VLM rubric, on the public VBench benchmarks, and in a blind user study.

ai.tldr: 提出SARA方法，通过文本条件显著性引导的成对路由操作，在视频扩散模型中实现语义自适应的关系对齐，提升文本跟随和运动质量。
ai.motivation: 现有视频扩散模型在细粒度文本跟随上存在实体遗漏、属性错绑和交互弱化问题，而现有表示对齐方法（如VideoREPA、MoAlign）的成对监督预算由视觉或运动线索分配，未考虑与提示的相关性。
ai.method: 在冻结视觉基础模型的标记关系蒸馏基础上，加入文本条件显著性模块（通过SAM 3.1掩码监督和InfoNCE正则化训练），并通过成对路由操作将显著性融合到蒸馏中，使监督偏向主体-主体和主体-背景对，远离背景-背景对。
ai.result: 在Wan2.2持续训练设置下，SARA在13维VLM评估、VBench基准和盲用户研究中均优于SFT、VideoREPA和MoAlign，同时提升文本对齐和运动质量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过语义自适应的成对监督分配来改善视频扩散模型的文本-视频对齐能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将显著性模块与冻结基础模型结合，通过轻量级对齐器实现动态监督分配，避免全模型微调。
idea_spark.risk: 显著性模块依赖SAM掩码标注，可能引入标注偏差；路由操作增加计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类注意力机制出发，让模型自动关注与提示最相关的标记对，而非均匀或仅基于视觉线索分配监督。

zh.tldr: 提出SARA方法，通过文本条件显著性引导的成对路由操作，在视频扩散模型中实现语义自适应的关系对齐，提升文本跟随和运动质量。
zh.motivation: 现有视频扩散模型在细粒度文本跟随上存在实体遗漏、属性错绑和交互弱化问题，而现有表示对齐方法（如VideoREPA、MoAlign）的成对监督预算由视觉或运动线索分配，未考虑与提示的相关性。
zh.method: 在冻结视觉基础模型的标记关系蒸馏基础上，加入文本条件显著性模块（通过SAM 3.1掩码监督和InfoNCE正则化训练），并通过成对路由操作将显著性融合到蒸馏中，使监督偏向主体-主体和主体-背景对，远离背景-背景对。
zh.result: 在Wan2.2持续训练设置下，SARA在13维VLM评估、VBench基准和盲用户研究中均优于SFT、VideoREPA和MoAlign，同时提升文本对齐和运动质量。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过语义自适应的成对监督分配来改善视频扩散模型的文本-视频对齐能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将显著性模块与冻结基础模型结合，通过轻量级对齐器实现动态监督分配，避免全模型微调。
idea_spark.risk: 显著性模块依赖SAM掩码标注，可能引入标注偏差；路由操作增加计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类注意力机制出发，让模型自动关注与提示最相关的标记对，而非均匀或仅基于视觉线索分配监督。

en.tldr: SARA proposes semantically adaptive relational alignment for video diffusion models, using text-conditioned saliency and pair-routing to improve fine-grained text following and motion quality.
en.motivation: Existing video diffusion models suffer from entity dropping, attribute misbinding, and weakened interactions; current alignment methods allocate pairwise supervision based on visual or motion cues rather than prompt relevance.
en.method: On top of token-relation distillation from a frozen VFM, a text-conditioned saliency module (trained with SAM 3.1 mask supervision and InfoNCE) is fused via a pair-routing operator that weights token pairs based on saliency, routing supervision toward subject-subject and subject-background pairs and away from background-background ones.
en.result: In the Wan2.2 continual-training setting, SARA outperforms SFT, VideoREPA, and MoAlign on a 13-dimension VLM rubric, VBench benchmarks, and a blind user study, improving both text alignment and motion quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how semantically adaptive pairwise supervision allocation can enhance text-to-video alignment in diffusion models.

idea_spark.idea: Combine a lightweight saliency aligner with a frozen foundation model to dynamically route supervision, avoiding full model fine-tuning.
idea_spark.risk: Saliency module relies on SAM mask annotations, which may introduce labeling bias; routing adds computational overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human attention, the model automatically focuses on token pairs most relevant to the prompt rather than allocating supervision uniformly or based solely on visual cues.

papers.title: GraphReAct: Reasoning and Acting for Multi-step Graph Inference
papers.summary: Reasoning-acting frameworks enhance large language models (LLMs) by interleaving reasoning with actions for dynamic information acquisition. However, extending this paradigm to graph learning remains underexplored. Graph data is inherently structured, with information distributed across nodes and edges and encoded through both topology and latent representations. As a result, effective reasoning over graphs requires not only retrieving informative evidence from the graph, but also progressively refining the accumulated context during multi-step inference. In this work, we propose GraphReAct, a graph reasoning-acting framework that enables step-by-step inference over graph-structured data. Specifically, we design a graph-based action space with two complementary retrieval actions: topological retrieval, which captures local structural dependencies, and semantic retrieval, which accesses non-local but relevant evidence in the representation space. These actions dynamically expand the reasoning context. To further support multi-step reasoning, we introduce another type of action, context refinement, which distills and reorganizes accumulated information into a compact representation. By interleaving reasoning with both retrieval and refinement actions, our framework enables a progressive transition from context expansion to compression. Extensive experiments on six benchmark datasets demonstrate that GraphReAct consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, validating the effectiveness of reasoning-acting for graph learning.

ai.tldr: 提出GraphReAct框架，将推理-行动范式扩展到图数据，通过拓扑检索、语义检索和上下文精炼实现多步图推理。
ai.motivation: 现有推理-行动框架主要针对文本，未充分探索图结构数据，而图推理需要逐步获取和精炼信息。
ai.method: 设计基于图的行动空间，包含拓扑检索（捕获局部结构依赖）、语义检索（获取非局部相关证据）和上下文精炼（压缩累积信息），交替推理与行动。
ai.result: 在六个基准数据集上持续优于现有方法，验证了推理-行动在图学习中的有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何将LLM的推理-行动能力应用于图数据，提升多步推理的准确性和效率。

idea_spark.idea: 结合图注意力机制动态调整检索权重，或引入强化学习优化行动选择策略。
idea_spark.risk: 上下文精炼可能丢失关键信息，需平衡压缩与保留。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受ReAct框架启发，将图结构视为外部知识源，通过行动扩展推理上下文。

zh.tldr: 提出GraphReAct框架，将推理-行动范式扩展到图数据，通过拓扑检索、语义检索和上下文精炼实现多步图推理。
zh.motivation: 现有推理-行动框架主要针对文本，未充分探索图结构数据，而图推理需要逐步获取和精炼信息。
zh.method: 设计基于图的行动空间，包含拓扑检索（捕获局部结构依赖）、语义检索（获取非局部相关证据）和上下文精炼（压缩累积信息），交替推理与行动。
zh.result: 在六个基准数据集上持续优于现有方法，验证了推理-行动在图学习中的有效性。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何将LLM的推理-行动能力应用于图数据，提升多步推理的准确性和效率。

idea_spark.idea: 结合图注意力机制动态调整检索权重，或引入强化学习优化行动选择策略。
idea_spark.risk: 上下文精炼可能丢失关键信息，需平衡压缩与保留。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受ReAct框架启发，将图结构视为外部知识源，通过行动扩展推理上下文。

en.tldr: Proposes GraphReAct, extending the reasoning-acting paradigm to graph data, enabling multi-step graph inference via topological retrieval, semantic retrieval, and context refinement.
en.motivation: Existing reasoning-acting frameworks focus on text and underexplore graph-structured data, where inference requires stepwise information acquisition and refinement.
en.method: Designs a graph-based action space with topological retrieval (local structure), semantic retrieval (non-local evidence), and context refinement (compression), interleaving reasoning with actions.
en.result: Consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods on six benchmarks, validating reasoning-acting for graph learning.
en.help_to_user: Helps users apply LLM reasoning-acting capabilities to graph data, improving multi-step inference accuracy and efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate graph attention to dynamically adjust retrieval weights, or use reinforcement learning to optimize action selection.
idea_spark.risk: Context refinement may lose critical information; balance between compression and retention is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the ReAct framework, treating graph structure as an external knowledge source and expanding reasoning context through actions.

papers.title: Similar Pattern Annotation via Retrieval Knowledge for LLM-Based Test Code Fault Localization
papers.summary: Software failures remain a major challenge in modern software development, and identifying the code elements responsible for failures is a time-consuming debugging task. While extensive research has focused on fault localization in the system under test (SUT), failures can also originate from faulty system test scripts. This problem, known as Test Code Fault Localization (TCFL), has received significantly less attention despite its importance in continuous integration (CI) environments where large test suites are executed frequently. TCFL is particularly challenging because it typically operates under black-box conditions, relies on limited diagnostic signals such as error messages and partial logs, and involves large system-level test scripts that expand the fault localization search space. In this paper, we propose SPARK, a framework that integrates accumulated debugging knowledge from continuous integration (CI) environments into Large Language Model (LLM)-based TCFL. Given a newly observed failing test case, SPARK retrieves similar fault-labeled test cases from a debugging knowledge corpus and selectively annotates suspicious lines of the failing test based on their similarity to previously observed fault patterns. These annotations guide the LLM's reasoning while maintaining scalability and avoiding the prompt-length explosion common to naive retrieval-augmented approaches. We evaluate SPARK on three industrial datasets containing real-world faulty Python test cases from different software products. The results show that SPARK consistently improves fault localization effectiveness compared to the existing LLM-based TCFL baseline while maintaining comparable inference cost and token usage. In particular, the approach advances the state of the art by identifying more correct faulty locations in complex test cases containing multiple faults.

ai.tldr: 提出SPARK框架，通过检索相似故障模式并注释可疑代码行，提升LLM在测试代码故障定位中的效果。
ai.motivation: 测试代码故障定位（TCFL）在持续集成中重要但研究不足，现有方法依赖有限信号且搜索空间大。
ai.method: 构建调试知识库，对新失败测试检索相似故障用例，基于相似性选择性注释可疑行，引导LLM推理。
ai.result: 在三个工业数据集上，SPARK比现有LLM基线更有效定位故障，且推理成本与token用量相当。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助开发者在持续集成环境中快速定位测试脚本中的故障代码行。

idea_spark.idea: 利用历史故障模式作为先验知识，通过选择性注释避免提示过长，提升LLM推理效率。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量故障标注库，新项目或罕见故障类型可能效果下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将相似案例的显式注释作为LLM推理的“提示骨架”，平衡信息量与长度约束。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量故障标注库，新项目或罕见故障类型可能效果下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将相似案例的显式注释作为LLM推理的“提示骨架”，平衡信息量与长度约束。

en.tldr: Proposes SPARK, a framework that retrieves similar fault patterns and annotates suspicious lines to improve LLM-based test code fault localization.
en.motivation: Test Code Fault Localization (TCFL) is critical in CI but understudied; existing methods rely on limited signals and face large search spaces.
en.method: Builds a debugging knowledge corpus, retrieves similar fault-labeled test cases for a new failing test, and selectively annotates suspicious lines to guide LLM reasoning.
en.result: On three industrial datasets, SPARK consistently outperforms the LLM baseline in fault localization effectiveness with comparable cost and token usage.
en.help_to_user: Helps developers quickly locate faulty lines in test scripts within continuous integration environments.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage historical fault patterns as prior knowledge, using selective annotation to avoid prompt bloat and boost LLM reasoning efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Depends on high-quality fault-labeled corpora; may degrade for new projects or rare fault types.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explicit annotations from similar cases serve as a 'prompt skeleton' for LLM reasoning, balancing informativeness and length constraints.

papers.title: WeatherSyn: An Instruction Tuning MLLM For Weather Forecasting Report Generation
papers.summary: Accurate weather forecast reporting enables individuals and communities to better plan daily activities and agricultural operations. However, the current reporting process primarily relies on manual analysis of multi-source data, which leads to information overload and reduced efficiency. With the development of multimodal large language models (MLLMs), leveraging data-driven models to analyze and generate reports in the weather forecasting domain remains largely underexplored. In this work, we propose the Weather Forecasting Report (WFR) task and construct the first instruction-tuning dataset for this task, named~\DatasetNameL, which covers 31 cities in America and 8 weather aspects. Based on this corpus, we develop the first model, \ModelNameL, specialized in generating weather forecast reports. Evaluation across multiple metrics on our dataset shows that \ModelNameL~ consistently outperforms leading closed-source MLLMs, particularly on structurally complex weather aspects. We further analyze its performance across diverse geographic regions and weather aspects. \ModelNameL~ demonstrates strong transferability across different regions, highlighting its zero-shot generalization capability. \ModelNameL~offers valuable insight for developing MLLMs specialized in weather report generation. .

ai.tldr: 提出天气报告生成任务，构建首个指令微调数据集，开发专用多模态大模型，在多个指标上优于闭源模型，并展现跨区域零样本泛化能力。
ai.motivation: 当前天气报告依赖人工分析多源数据，效率低且易信息过载，而多模态大模型在该领域尚未被充分探索。
ai.method: 构建覆盖美国31城市、8个天气方面的指令微调数据集WeatherSyn，基于此训练专用多模态大模型WeatherSyn。
ai.result: 在数据集上多个指标一致优于领先闭源MLLM，尤其在结构复杂的天气方面表现突出，并展现出跨区域强迁移性和零样本泛化能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用指令微调多模态大模型自动生成高质量天气报告，提升预报效率。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将天气报告生成与实时传感器数据、卫星图像结合，实现动态更新和个性化定制。
idea_spark.risk: 模型可能对罕见天气事件泛化不足，且依赖高质量标注数据，数据偏差可能影响报告准确性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受天气报告结构化特点启发，可推广至其他领域（如医疗报告、金融摘要）的指令微调任务。

zh.tldr: 提出天气报告生成任务，构建首个指令微调数据集，开发专用多模态大模型，在多个指标上优于闭源模型，并展现跨区域零样本泛化能力。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用指令微调多模态大模型自动生成高质量天气报告，提升预报效率。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将天气报告生成与实时传感器数据、卫星图像结合，实现动态更新和个性化定制。
idea_spark.risk: 模型可能对罕见天气事件泛化不足，且依赖高质量标注数据，数据偏差可能影响报告准确性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受天气报告结构化特点启发，可推广至其他领域（如医疗报告、金融摘要）的指令微调任务。

en.tldr: Proposes the Weather Forecasting Report task, constructs the first instruction-tuning dataset, develops a specialized MLLM that outperforms closed-source models on multiple metrics, and demonstrates zero-shot generalization across regions.
en.motivation: Current weather reporting relies on manual analysis of multi-source data, causing inefficiency and information overload, while MLLMs remain underexplored in this domain.
en.method: Constructs the first instruction-tuning dataset WeatherSyn covering 31 US cities and 8 weather aspects, and trains a dedicated MLLM named WeatherSyn.
en.result: Consistently outperforms leading closed-source MLLMs on multiple metrics, especially on structurally complex weather aspects, and shows strong cross-region transferability and zero-shot generalization.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to leverage instruction-tuned MLLMs for automatic high-quality weather report generation, improving forecasting efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Explore integrating real-time sensor data and satellite imagery with weather report generation for dynamic updates and personalized reports.
idea_spark.risk: The model may generalize poorly to rare weather events and relies on high-quality labeled data; data bias could affect report accuracy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the structured nature of weather reports, the instruction-tuning paradigm can be extended to other fields like medical reports and financial summaries.

papers.title: A Syndrome-Space Approach to Proximity Gaps and Correlated Agreement for Random Linear Codes
papers.summary: Proximity gaps and correlated agreement have become central tools in the analysis of interactive oracle proofs of proximity (IOPPs) and code-based SNARKs. Informally, a proximity-gap statement says that for a structured set of words -- such as a line, an affine space, or a curve -- either all points are close to the code, or most are far from it. Such statements are essential in sampling-based proof systems, where a verifier queries only a few random locations on a structured object but must still obtain a global soundness guarantee. In Reed--Solomon-based proof systems, one would ideally like the proximity parameter to approach the information-theoretic limit $1-R$, since this is the largest possible radius for a rate-$R$ code and directly affects protocol efficiency. While recent work has substantially strengthened the picture for algebraic codes and linked proximity gaps to decoding-related structural properties, it remains unclear whether analogous results for random linear codes can be proved directly, rather than through decoding-theoretic surrogates.   In this work, we establish a direct approach to proximity gaps and correlated agreement for random linear codes in the random parity-check-matrix model, without relying on list decoding as the main engine of the proof. Our approach is based on a syndrome-space reformulation together with a witness-based reduction mechanism, and it yields strong results for affine lines, affine spaces, and polynomial curves. It is conceptually different from the existing decoding-driven route for random linear codes, and it also leads to sharper parameters, including the optimal-up-to-$\varepsilon$ large-alphabet radius bound $ρ<1-R-\varepsilon$ for $q=Θ(n)$, as well as near-capacity bounds over constant alphabets with improved alphabet-size requirements.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于综合征空间的直接方法，用于证明随机线性码的邻近间隙和相关一致性，无需依赖列表解码。
ai.motivation: 现有方法依赖解码理论，而直接证明随机线性码的邻近间隙和相关一致性仍不明确。
ai.method: 通过综合征空间重构和基于见证的归约机制，直接分析仿射线、仿射空间和多项式曲线。
ai.result: 获得了大字母表下最优至ε的半径界ρ<1-R-ε（q=Θ(n)），以及常数字母表下接近容量的界。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解随机线性码在交互式证明系统中的应用和性能极限。

idea_spark.idea: 利用综合征空间替代列表解码，简化随机线性码的邻近间隙分析。
idea_spark.risk: 方法可能依赖于特定模型（随机奇偶校验矩阵），通用性待验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从代数码的邻近间隙结果出发，探索随机码的直接证明路径。

zh.result: 获得了大字母表下最优至ε的半径界ρ<1-R-ε（q=Θ(n)），以及常数字母表下接近容量的界。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解随机线性码在交互式证明系统中的应用和性能极限。

idea_spark.risk: 方法可能依赖于特定模型（随机奇偶校验矩阵），通用性待验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从代数码的邻近间隙结果出发，探索随机码的直接证明路径。

en.tldr: This work presents a direct syndrome-space approach to prove proximity gaps and correlated agreement for random linear codes, bypassing list decoding. It yields optimal radius bounds for large alphabets and near-capacity bounds for constant alphabets.
en.motivation: Existing decoding-driven methods for random linear codes are indirect; a direct proof of proximity gaps and correlated agreement is needed.
en.method: A syndrome-space reformulation combined with a witness-based reduction mechanism is used to analyze affine lines, affine spaces, and polynomial curves.
en.result: Achieves the optimal-up-to-ε radius bound ρ<1-R-ε for q=Θ(n) and near-capacity bounds over constant alphabets with improved alphabet-size requirements.
en.help_to_user: Provides insight into the efficiency and soundness of random linear codes in interactive proof systems and SNARKs.

idea_spark.idea: Replace list decoding with syndrome-space analysis to simplify proximity gap proofs for random codes.
idea_spark.risk: The method relies on the random parity-check-matrix model; generalization to other models may be nontrivial.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by algebraic code proximity gap results, seeking a direct combinatorial proof for random linear codes.

papers.title: Differentiable Ray Tracing with Gaussians for Unified Radio Propagation Simulation and View Synthesis
papers.summary: Explicit neural representations such as 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) enable high-fidelity and real-time novel view synthesis, yet optimize for alpha-composited optical appearance rather than ray-intersectable geometry. In contrast, radio-frequency (RF) digital twins require deterministic multi-bounce paths, where the geometry dictates trajectories and their associated attenuation and delay. We introduce a framework enabling differentiable RF propagation simulation directly within visually reconstructed neural scenes, allowing point-to-point path computation between arbitrary 3D locations while preserving high-quality visual rendering. Unlike conventional RF simulation pipelines that rely on manually constructed meshes, we embed Gaussian primitives into a hardware-accelerated ray tracing structure as the underlying spatial representation. By extracting physically meaningful channel impulse responses from visual-only reconstructions, we provide cross-modal evidence that neural reconstructions can serve as unified spatial representations for both electromagnetic propagation simulation and photorealistic view synthesis.

ai.tldr: 提出一种可微射线追踪框架，利用3D高斯表示统一无线电传播模拟与视觉新视角合成。
ai.motivation: 现有3DGS优化视觉外观而非可射线相交的几何，而RF数字孪生需要确定性多径几何，两者表示不统一。
ai.method: 将高斯基元嵌入硬件加速的射线追踪结构中，作为底层空间表示，实现任意3D点间的路径计算。
ai.result: 从视觉重建中提取物理有意义的信道冲激响应，证明神经重建可作为电磁传播与视觉合成的统一空间表示。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何将视觉场景表示直接用于无线电传播模拟，无需手动构建网格。

idea_spark.idea: 利用可微射线追踪，联合优化视觉渲染与RF传播参数，实现跨模态场景理解。
idea_spark.risk: 高斯表示可能无法精确建模复杂几何细节，影响RF路径计算精度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将计算机图形学中的可微渲染技术应用于无线通信数字孪生，实现多模态统一表示。

zh.tldr: 提出一种可微射线追踪框架，利用3D高斯表示统一无线电传播模拟与视觉新视角合成。
zh.motivation: 现有3DGS优化视觉外观而非可射线相交的几何，而RF数字孪生需要确定性多径几何，两者表示不统一。
zh.method: 将高斯基元嵌入硬件加速的射线追踪结构中，作为底层空间表示，实现任意3D点间的路径计算。
zh.result: 从视觉重建中提取物理有意义的信道冲激响应，证明神经重建可作为电磁传播与视觉合成的统一空间表示。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何将视觉场景表示直接用于无线电传播模拟，无需手动构建网格。

idea_spark.idea: 利用可微射线追踪，联合优化视觉渲染与RF传播参数，实现跨模态场景理解。
idea_spark.risk: 高斯表示可能无法精确建模复杂几何细节，影响RF路径计算精度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将计算机图形学中的可微渲染技术应用于无线通信数字孪生，实现多模态统一表示。

en.tldr: A differentiable ray tracing framework using Gaussian primitives unifies radio propagation simulation and novel view synthesis.
en.motivation: 3DGS optimizes for visual appearance rather than ray-intersectable geometry, while RF digital twins require deterministic multi-bounce paths, leading to representation mismatch.
en.method: Embed Gaussian primitives into a hardware-accelerated ray tracing structure as the underlying spatial representation for point-to-point path computation.
en.result: Extract physically meaningful channel impulse responses from visual-only reconstructions, demonstrating neural reconstructions as unified spatial representations for both EM propagation and view synthesis.
en.help_to_user: Understand how to directly use visual scene representations for radio propagation simulation without manual mesh construction.

idea_spark.idea: Jointly optimize visual rendering and RF propagation parameters via differentiable ray tracing for cross-modal scene understanding.
idea_spark.risk: Gaussian representation may not capture fine geometric details, affecting RF path computation accuracy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Apply differentiable rendering from computer graphics to wireless communication digital twins for unified multimodal representation.

papers.title: How Well Do LLMs Perform on the Simplest Long-Chain Reasoning Tasks: An Empirical Study on the Equivalence Class Problem
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved great improvements in recent years. Nevertheless, it still remains unclear how good LLMs are for reasoning tasks, especially for long-chain ones. In this paper, we evaluate LLMs' performance on the simplest yet long-chain reasoning task, namely the Equivalence Class Problem (ECP), i.e., determining whether two variables are equal given a set of randomly generated equivalence relations. We consider both reasoning and non-reasoning representative LLMs over a large variety of problem instances, ranging over different numbers of variables, connectivity probabilities, prompts, and other factors. The experimental results show that non-reasoning LLMs fail ECP, while reasoning models are significantly better but still struggle to completely solve this problem. Interestingly, considering various connectivity probabilities with a fixed number of variables, we observe that, for non-reasoning models, the hardest problem instances coincide with the phase transition point of ln n/(n-1), suggesting the chaos of the problem; in contrast, for reasoning models, the hardest ones coincide with the biggest diameter, suggesting the reasoning difficulty of the problem.

ai.tldr: 本文实证研究了大型语言模型在等价类问题上的表现，发现非推理模型完全失败，推理模型显著更好但仍无法完全解决，且最难实例分别对应相变点和最大直径。
ai.motivation: 评估LLM在简单但长链推理任务上的能力，填补对推理模型性能理解的空白。
ai.method: 在随机生成的等价关系实例上，测试推理与非推理LLM，变化变量数、连接概率、提示等因素。
ai.result: 非推理模型失败，推理模型表现更好但未完全解决；非推理模型最难实例对应相变点，推理模型最难实例对应最大直径。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM在长链推理中的局限性，以及不同模型类型对问题难度的敏感性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用相变点和直径特征设计自适应难度推理任务。
idea_spark.risk: 任务过于简单可能无法区分高级推理模型。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合图论中的相变现象来设计更有效的推理评估方法。

idea_spark.inspiration: 结合图论中的相变现象来设计更有效的推理评估方法。

en.tldr: This paper empirically studies LLMs on the Equivalence Class Problem, finding that non-reasoning models fail completely, reasoning models perform significantly better but still struggle, with hardest instances aligning with phase transition and largest diameter respectively.
en.motivation: To evaluate LLM performance on simple but long-chain reasoning tasks, filling a gap in understanding reasoning model capabilities.
en.method: Test reasoning and non-reasoning LLMs on randomly generated equivalence relation instances, varying number of variables, connectivity probability, prompts, and other factors.
en.result: Non-reasoning models fail; reasoning models perform better but not perfectly. Hardest instances for non-reasoning models correspond to phase transition point, while for reasoning models they correspond to largest diameter.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand LLM limitations in long-chain reasoning and how different model types are sensitive to problem difficulty.

idea_spark.idea: Design adaptive difficulty reasoning tasks using phase transition and diameter features.
idea_spark.risk: The task may be too simple to distinguish advanced reasoning models.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage phase transition phenomena from graph theory to design more effective reasoning evaluation methods.

papers.title: Retrieve, Integrate, and Synthesize: Spatial-Semantic Grounded Latent Visual Reasoning
papers.summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have made remarkable progress on vision-language reasoning, yet most methods still compress visual evidence into discrete textual thoughts, creating an information bottleneck for fine-grained perception. Recent latent visual reasoning methods attempt to reason in continuous hidden states, but we find that they suffer from insufficient manifold compatibility: latent trajectories drift away from pretrained reasoning circuits, collapse into instance-agnostic patterns, and are often bypassed during answer generation. To address these issues, we propose RIS (Retrieve, Integrate, and Synthesize), a spatial-semantic grounded framework that develops latent reasoning as a compatible extension of pretrained MLLM computation. We first construct a step-wise grounded reasoning dataset with bounding boxes and region-specific semantic descriptions. Built on this supervision, RIS anchors latent tokens to both spatial and semantic evidence, enforces their causal role through a progressive attention bottleneck, and introduces short language transition tokens to bridge synthesized latent states back to vocabulary-aligned decoding. Experiments on V*, HRBench4K, HRBench8K, MMVP, and BLINK show consistent improvements over closed/open-source and latent reasoning baselines. Further analyses demonstrate that RIS learns diverse, interpretable, and progressively integrated latent trajectories, offering a practical path toward faithful internal visual reasoning in MLLMs.

ai.tldr: 提出RIS框架，通过空间-语义锚定和渐进注意力瓶颈，解决多模态大语言模型在潜在视觉推理中的流形不兼容问题。
ai.motivation: 现有潜在推理方法存在流形不兼容，导致潜在轨迹偏离预训练推理回路、坍缩为实例无关模式，且常被答案生成阶段绕过。
ai.method: 构建带边界框和区域语义描述的逐步接地推理数据集；RIS将潜在令牌锚定到空间和语义证据，通过渐进注意力瓶颈强制其因果作用，并引入短语言过渡令牌桥接潜在状态与词汇对齐解码。
ai.result: 在V*、HRBench4K、HRBench8K、MMVP、BLINK上一致优于闭源/开源及潜在推理基线，学习到多样、可解释且逐步整合的潜在轨迹。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过空间-语义接地和注意力机制改进多模态模型的内部视觉推理能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将潜在推理与显式空间语义监督结合，设计因果注意力约束，使潜在状态可解释且与预训练模型兼容。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量边界框和语义描述数据集，可能限制在无标注场景的泛化；短语言过渡令牌可能引入额外计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类逐步推理启发，将推理过程分解为检索、整合、合成三个阶段，并利用空间语义锚定保持与预训练知识的兼容性。

en.tldr: Proposes RIS, a spatial-semantic grounded framework that anchors latent tokens to spatial and semantic evidence with a progressive attention bottleneck, addressing manifold incompatibility in latent visual reasoning of MLLMs.
en.motivation: Existing latent reasoning methods suffer from manifold incompatibility: latent trajectories drift from pretrained circuits, collapse into instance-agnostic patterns, and are often bypassed during answer generation.
en.method: Constructs a step-wise grounded reasoning dataset with bounding boxes and region-specific semantic descriptions; RIS anchors latent tokens to spatial and semantic evidence, enforces causal role via progressive attention bottleneck, and introduces short language transition tokens to bridge latent states to vocabulary-aligned decoding.
en.result: Consistent improvements over closed/open-source and latent reasoning baselines on V*, HRBench4K, HRBench8K, MMVP, BLINK; learns diverse, interpretable, and progressively integrated latent trajectories.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how spatial-semantic grounding and attention mechanisms can improve internal visual reasoning in multimodal models.

idea_spark.idea: Combine latent reasoning with explicit spatial-semantic supervision, design causal attention constraints to make latent states interpretable and compatible with pretrained models.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality bounding box and semantic description datasets, which may limit generalization to unlabeled scenarios; short language transition tokens may introduce extra computational overhead.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human step-by-step reasoning, decomposes reasoning into retrieve, integrate, and synthesize stages, using spatial-semantic anchoring to maintain compatibility with pretrained knowledge.

papers.title: Variable Aerodynamic Damping via Co-Contraction: A Dynamic Isomorphism with Variable Stiffness Actuators
papers.summary: We prove that aerodynamic co-contraction in a redundant dual-rotor actuator can tune a passive, trim-defined aero-mechanical damping while keeping the commanded net force constant. In particular, we define an incremental damping coefficient as the local sensitivity of net thrust to air-relative velocity at a trim and prove that it increases monotonically along constant-force fibers under a mild aerodynamic hardening condition. We then validate the required damping and hardening properties from a first-principles Blade Element Theory derivation, which yields a minimal thrust model affine in inflow and explicitly reveals the speed--inflow coupling driving the effect. The resulting mechanism is formalized as a Variable Aerodynamic Damping Actuator (VADA) and shown to be dynamically isomorphic to stiffness modulation in antagonistic variable-stiffness actuation (VSA), similar to the co-contraction of tendons by muscle co-activation. The same fiber-density principle also enhances the active aerodynamic promptness measure of redundant multirotors. Finally, an impedance-form representation clarifies the roles of common-mode and differential-mode actuation in the control of passive impedance and the equilibrium velocity of the VADA system.

ai.tldr: 证明冗余双旋翼执行器中的气动共收缩可调节被动气动机械阻尼，同时保持净推力恒定，并建立与变刚度执行器的动态同构关系。
ai.motivation: 传统变刚度执行器通过机械共收缩调节刚度，但缺乏气动阻尼的类似机制。本文旨在探索气动共收缩能否实现等效的阻尼调节功能。
ai.method: 基于叶素理论推导最小推力模型，定义增量阻尼系数并证明其在恒定推力纤维上单调递增；通过气动硬化条件验证该机制，并建立与变刚度执行器的动态同构。
ai.result: 提出了可变气动阻尼执行器（VADA），证明其与拮抗变刚度执行器在动态上同构；共收缩原理还可增强冗余多旋翼的主动气动响应速度。
ai.help_to_user: 为多旋翼无人机设计提供一种无需机械变刚度机构即可调节阻尼的新思路，有助于提升飞行稳定性和控制灵活性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用共收缩机制设计自适应阻尼器，用于抑制飞行器在湍流或阵风中的振动。
idea_spark.risk: 气动硬化条件可能不适用于所有飞行工况，需验证极端条件下的有效性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从生物肌肉共收缩调节关节刚度获得启发，将类似原理应用于气动系统。

zh.result: 提出了可变气动阻尼执行器（VADA），证明其与拮抗变刚度执行器在动态上同构；共收缩原理还可增强冗余多旋翼的主动气动响应速度。

idea_spark.idea: 利用共收缩机制设计自适应阻尼器，用于抑制飞行器在湍流或阵风中的振动。
idea_spark.risk: 气动硬化条件可能不适用于所有飞行工况，需验证极端条件下的有效性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从生物肌肉共收缩调节关节刚度获得启发，将类似原理应用于气动系统。

en.tldr: Proves that aerodynamic co-contraction in a redundant dual-rotor actuator can tune passive aero-mechanical damping while keeping net thrust constant, and establishes a dynamic isomorphism with variable stiffness actuators.
en.motivation: Traditional variable stiffness actuators use mechanical co-contraction to adjust stiffness, but lack an analogous mechanism for aerodynamic damping. This paper explores whether aerodynamic co-contraction can achieve equivalent damping modulation.
en.method: Derives a minimal thrust model using Blade Element Theory, defines an incremental damping coefficient and proves its monotonic increase along constant-thrust fibers under a mild aerodynamic hardening condition; validates the mechanism and establishes dynamic isomorphism with variable stiffness actuators.
en.result: Proposes the Variable Aerodynamic Damping Actuator (VADA), demonstrates its dynamic isomorphism with antagonistic variable stiffness actuation; the co-contraction principle also enhances active aerodynamic promptness of redundant multirotors.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new approach to modulate damping in multirotor UAVs without mechanical variable stiffness mechanisms, improving flight stability and control flexibility.

idea_spark.idea: Design adaptive dampers using co-contraction to suppress vibrations in aircraft during turbulence or gusts.
idea_spark.risk: The aerodynamic hardening condition may not hold in all flight regimes; validity under extreme conditions needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by biological muscle co-contraction to modulate joint stiffness, applying a similar principle to aerodynamic systems.

papers.title: Sat3R: Satellite DSM Reconstruction via RPC-Aware Depth Fine-tuning
papers.summary: Accurate Digital Surface Model (DSM) reconstruction from satellite imagery is critical for applications such as disaster response, urban planning, and large-scale geographic mapping. Existing approaches face a fundamental trade-off: optimization-based methods achieve strong accuracy but require hours of per-scene computation, while generalizable geometry foundation models offer near-instant inference but fail to generalize to satellite imagery due to the domain gap introduced by the Rational Polynomial Camera (RPC) model and mismatched depth scale distributions. We present Sat3R, a feed-forward framework that bridges this gap via RPC-aware metric depth fine-tuning of Depth Anything V2 using the Scale-Invariant Logarithmic (SiLog) loss. By constructing physically consistent pseudo depth supervision from RPC geometry, Sat3R adapts a monocular depth foundation model to the satellite domain without per-scene optimization. Experiments on the DFC2019 benchmark demonstrate that Sat3R reduces MAE by 38% over zero-shot feed-forward baselines and achieves competitive accuracy against optimization-based methods, while delivering over 300x speedup. Sat3R demonstrates that feed-forward models, when properly adapted to the satellite domain, can match optimization-based accuracy at a fraction of the computational cost, paving the way for practical large-scale satellite DSM reconstruction.

ai.tldr: 提出Sat3R，通过RPC感知的度量深度微调，将单目深度基础模型适配到卫星领域，实现快速且高精度的DSM重建。
ai.motivation: 现有卫星DSM重建方法在精度与速度之间存在权衡：优化方法精度高但耗时，通用几何基础模型推理快但无法泛化到卫星图像。
ai.method: 基于Depth Anything V2，利用RPC几何构建物理一致的伪深度监督，采用尺度不变对数损失进行微调，无需逐场景优化。
ai.result: 在DFC2019基准上，MAE降低38%，精度与优化方法相当，速度提升300倍以上。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速从卫星图像重建高精度DSM，适用于灾害响应、城市规划等大规模地理制图任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用RPC模型提供的几何先验作为伪标签，无需真实深度即可微调深度模型。
idea_spark.risk: 伪深度监督的质量依赖RPC几何的准确性，在复杂地形或低质量图像中可能引入误差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合领域先验（如相机模型）微调通用基础模型，可有效弥合域差距，实现快速且准确的推理。

zh.tldr: 提出Sat3R，通过RPC感知的度量深度微调，将单目深度基础模型适配到卫星领域，实现快速且高精度的DSM重建。
zh.motivation: 现有卫星DSM重建方法在精度与速度之间存在权衡：优化方法精度高但耗时，通用几何基础模型推理快但无法泛化到卫星图像。
zh.method: 基于Depth Anything V2，利用RPC几何构建物理一致的伪深度监督，采用尺度不变对数损失进行微调，无需逐场景优化。
zh.result: 在DFC2019基准上，MAE降低38%，精度与优化方法相当，速度提升300倍以上。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速从卫星图像重建高精度DSM，适用于灾害响应、城市规划等大规模地理制图任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用RPC模型提供的几何先验作为伪标签，无需真实深度即可微调深度模型。
idea_spark.risk: 伪深度监督的质量依赖RPC几何的准确性，在复杂地形或低质量图像中可能引入误差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合领域先验（如相机模型）微调通用基础模型，可有效弥合域差距，实现快速且准确的推理。

en.tldr: Sat3D adapts a monocular depth foundation model to satellite domain via RPC-aware metric depth fine-tuning, achieving fast and accurate DSM reconstruction.
en.motivation: Existing satellite DSM reconstruction methods face a trade-off between accuracy and speed: optimization-based methods are accurate but slow, while generalizable geometry foundation models are fast but fail on satellite imagery due to domain gap.
en.method: Fine-tunes Depth Anything V2 using RPC geometry to construct physically consistent pseudo depth supervision with Scale-Invariant Logarithmic loss, without per-scene optimization.
en.result: On DFC2019 benchmark, reduces MAE by 38% over zero-shot baselines, matches optimization-based accuracy, and achieves over 300x speedup.
en.help_to_user: Enables fast, high-quality DSM reconstruction from satellite imagery for applications like disaster response and urban planning.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage geometric priors from RPC model as pseudo-labels to fine-tune depth models without ground-truth depth.
idea_spark.risk: Quality of pseudo-depth supervision depends on RPC geometry accuracy, which may degrade in complex terrain or low-quality images.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining domain-specific priors (e.g., camera models) with fine-tuning of general foundation models can effectively bridge domain gaps for fast and accurate inference.

papers.title: Global Existence of Classical Solutions to Brenner-Navier-Stokes-Fourier System for Large Data
papers.summary: We study the 1D Brenner-Navier-Stokes-Fourier (BNSF) system, proposed as a refinement of the classical Navier--Stokes--Fourier model through the introduction of the volume velocity, distinct from the mass velocity describing convective transport. When formulated in the Lagrangian mass coordinates with the volume velocity, the discrepancy between the two velocities induces a dissipative structure in the mass conservation law.   We prove the global existence of classical solutions for arbitrarily large initial data. More precisely, for initial data in $H^k(\mathbb{R})$ with $k\ge3$, with the specific volume and absolute temperature initially bounded away from zero, we construct global-in-time solutions that remain in the same regularity class. Our result accommodates arbitrarily large initial data. A major difficulty is to establish lower and upper bounds for the specific volume \(v\). The additional dissipation yields an $L_t^2 L_x^2$ bound for $v_x$, which is further improved to an $L_t^\infty L_x^\infty$ bound of $v$ and $1/v$ via the parabolic De Giorgi method. We also apply the maximum principle to obtain a positive lower bound for the absolute temperature.

ai.motivation: 经典Navier-Stokes-Fourier模型在描述对流输运时存在局限，Brenner等人引入体积速度概念进行改进，但该系统的整体适定性尚未解决。

ai.result: 对于初始数据在H^k(R) (k≥3)中且比容和温度初始有界远离零的情形，构造了整体经典解，且允许初始数据任意大。
ai.help_to_user: 为理解Brenner修正流体模型在强非线性下的整体可解性提供了严格分析框架。

idea_spark.risk: 该方法高度依赖一维结构，推广到高维时可能面临本质困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过引入非标准速度场来增强系统耗散性，为设计数值格式或简化模型提供了新思路。

en.tldr: This paper proves the global existence of classical solutions to the 1D Brenner-Navier-Stokes-Fourier system for arbitrarily large initial data.
en.motivation: The classical Navier-Stokes-Fourier model has limitations in describing convective transport; Brenner et al. introduced the volume velocity to refine it, but the global well-posedness of the resulting system remained open.
en.method: The system is formulated in Lagrangian mass coordinates with the volume velocity. The parabolic De Giorgi method is used to obtain uniform bounds for the specific volume and its reciprocal, and the maximum principle yields a positive lower bound for the absolute temperature.
en.result: For initial data in H^k(R) (k≥3) with specific volume and temperature initially bounded away from zero, global classical solutions are constructed, allowing arbitrarily large initial data.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous analytical framework for understanding the global solvability of the Brenner-modified fluid model under strong nonlinearities.

idea_spark.idea: Treating the discrepancy between volume velocity and mass velocity as an extra dissipation mechanism introduces a regularizing effect in the mass conservation law.
idea_spark.risk: The method heavily relies on the one-dimensional setting; extension to higher dimensions may face essential difficulties.
idea_spark.inspiration: Introducing non-standard velocity fields to enhance system dissipation offers new ideas for designing numerical schemes or simplified models.

papers.title: Towards Highly-Constrained Human Motion Generation with Retrieval-Guided Diffusion Noise Optimization
papers.summary: Generating human motion that satisfies customized zero-shot goal functions, enabling applications such as controllable character animation and behavior synthesis for virtual agents, is a critical capability. While current approaches handle many unseen constraints, they fail on tasks with very challenging spatiotemporal restrictions, such as severe spatial obstacles or specified numbers of walking steps. To equip motion generators for these highly constrained tasks, we present a retrieval-guided method built on the training-free diffusion noise optimization framework. The key idea is to search within large motion datasets for guidance that can potentially satisfy difficult constraints. We introduce relational task parsing to group target constraints and identify the difficult ones to be handled by retrieved reference. A better initialization for diffusion noise is then obtained via a reward-guided mask that combines random noise with retrieved noise. By optimizing diffusion noise from this improved initialization, we successfully solve highly constrained generation tasks. By leveraging LLM for relational task parsing, the whole framework is further enabled to automatically reason for what to retrieve, improving the intelligence of moving agents under a training-free optimization scheme.

ai.tldr: 提出一种检索引导的扩散噪声优化方法，解决高度约束的人体运动生成问题，如严重空间障碍或指定步数。
ai.motivation: 现有方法在处理具有挑战性时空约束（如严重空间障碍或指定步数）的任务时失败。
ai.method: 利用大型运动数据集检索参考，通过关系任务解析分组约束并识别困难约束，结合随机噪声和检索噪声获得更好的初始化，再优化扩散噪声。
ai.result: 成功解决了高度约束的生成任务，并利用LLM自动推理检索内容，提高了智能性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户生成满足复杂时空约束的人体运动，如角色动画和虚拟代理行为。

idea_spark.idea: 结合LLM进行任务解析，自动识别困难约束并检索相关参考，提升生成质量。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖大型运动数据集的质量和覆盖范围，可能对罕见约束效果有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将检索与扩散模型结合，通过关系解析和奖励引导初始化，为训练自由优化提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes a retrieval-guided diffusion noise optimization method to solve highly-constrained human motion generation tasks, such as severe spatial obstacles or specified step counts.
en.motivation: Existing methods fail on tasks with very challenging spatiotemporal constraints, such as severe spatial obstacles or specified numbers of walking steps.
en.method: Searches large motion datasets for guidance, uses relational task parsing to group constraints and identify difficult ones, obtains a better initialization via a reward-guided mask combining random and retrieved noise, then optimizes diffusion noise.
en.result: Successfully solves highly-constrained generation tasks and leverages LLM for automatic reasoning about what to retrieve, improving agent intelligence.
en.help_to_user: Helps users generate human motions satisfying complex spatiotemporal constraints for applications like controllable character animation and behavior synthesis.

idea_spark.idea: Combine LLM for task parsing to automatically identify difficult constraints and retrieve relevant references, improving generation quality.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the quality and coverage of large motion datasets, may be limited for rare constraints.
idea_spark.inspiration: Integrating retrieval with diffusion models via relational parsing and reward-guided initialization provides a new direction for training-free optimization.

papers.title: XiYOLO: Energy-Aware Object Detection via Iterative Architecture Search and Scaling
papers.summary: Object detection on heterogeneous edge devices must satisfy strict energy, latency, and memory constraints while still providing reliable perception for downstream autonomy. Existing energy-aware NAS methods often target limited deployment settings, while real energy remains difficult to optimize because it is highly device-dependent and costly to measure. We address these challenges with an energy-adaptive framework that combines an energy-aware XiResOFA search space, a two-stage energy estimator, and iterative search to identify a single energy-efficient base architecture. We then apply compound scaling to transform this base design into the XiYOLO family across deployment budgets, enabling interpretable accuracy-energy tradeoffs under sparse hardware measurements. Experiments on PascalVOC, COCO, and real-device deployment show that XiYOLO achieves a stronger energy-accuracy tradeoff than YOLO baselines. On PascalVOC, the medium XiYOLO model reaches 86.15 mAP50 while reducing energy relative to YOLOv12m by 20.6% on GPU and 35.9% on NPU. On COCO, XiYOLO reduces energy relative to YOLOv12 by up to 53.7% on GPU and 51.6% on NPU at the small scale. The proposed two-stage estimator also improves sample efficiency over a joint predictor under few-shot adaptation with only 2-20 target-device samples.

ai.tldr: 提出一种能量感知的目标检测框架XiYOLO，通过迭代架构搜索和复合缩放，在异构边缘设备上实现精度与能耗的平衡。
ai.motivation: 现有能量感知NAS方法局限于少数部署场景，且真实能耗难以优化，因为能耗高度依赖设备且测量成本高。
ai.method: 结合能量感知搜索空间XiResOFA、两阶段能量估计器和迭代搜索，找到单一节能基础架构，再通过复合缩放生成XiYOLO系列模型。
ai.result: 在PascalVOC和COCO上，XiYOLO相比YOLOv12能耗降低20.6%~53.7%，两阶段估计器在少量样本下提升采样效率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在资源受限的边缘设备上设计低能耗、高精度的目标检测模型。

idea_spark.idea: 将两阶段能量估计器与迭代搜索结合，减少对大量真实能耗测量的依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 搜索空间和缩放策略可能无法泛化到全新硬件平台，需要重新校准。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从硬件测量稀疏性出发，用少量样本估计能耗，启发其他资源约束下的模型优化。

idea_spark.risk: 搜索空间和缩放策略可能无法泛化到全新硬件平台，需要重新校准。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从硬件测量稀疏性出发，用少量样本估计能耗，启发其他资源约束下的模型优化。

en.tldr: Proposes XiYOLO, an energy-aware object detection framework that balances accuracy and energy via iterative architecture search and compound scaling for heterogeneous edge devices.
en.motivation: Existing energy-aware NAS methods are limited to few deployment scenarios, and real energy is hard to optimize due to device dependence and high measurement cost.
en.method: Combines an energy-aware XiResOFA search space, a two-stage energy estimator, and iterative search to find a single energy-efficient base architecture, then applies compound scaling to produce the XiYOLO family.
en.result: On PascalVOC and COCO, XiYOLO reduces energy by 20.6%–53.7% over YOLOv12; the two-stage estimator improves sample efficiency under few-shot adaptation.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to design low-energy, high-accuracy object detection models for resource-constrained edge devices.

idea_spark.idea: Combining a two-stage energy estimator with iterative search reduces reliance on extensive real energy measurements.
idea_spark.risk: The search space and scaling strategy may not generalize to entirely new hardware platforms without recalibration.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging sparse hardware measurements for energy estimation inspires model optimization under other resource constraints.

papers.title: 6D Pose Estimation via Keypoint Heatmap Regression with RGB-D Residual Neural Networks
papers.summary: In this paper, we propose a modular framework for 6D pose estimation based on keypoint heatmap regression. Our approach combines YOLOv10m for object detection with a ResNet18-based network that predicts 2D heatmaps from RGB images. Keypoints extracted from these heatmaps are used to estimate the 6D object pose via the PnP RANSAC algorithm. We compare different keypoint selection strategies to assess their impact on pose accuracy. Additionally, we extend the baseline by incorporating depth data using a cross-fusion architecture, which enables interaction between RGB and depth features at multiple stages. We further explore general training improvements, such as experimenting with activation functions and learning rate scheduling strategies to improve model performance. Our best RGB-only model achieved a mean ADD-based accuracy of 84.50%, while the RGB-D fusion model reached 92.41% on the LINEMOD dataset. The code is available at https://github.com/ameermasood/HeatNet.

ai.tldr: 提出基于关键点热图回归的6D姿态估计模块化框架，结合YOLOv10m检测与ResNet18热图预测，通过PnP RANSAC求解姿态，并引入RGB-D交叉融合架构提升精度。
ai.motivation: 现有6D姿态估计方法在复杂场景下精度不足，且对深度信息的利用有限。
ai.method: 使用YOLOv10m检测目标，ResNet18预测2D关键点热图，经PnP RANSAC估计6D姿态；通过交叉融合架构整合RGB与深度特征，并优化激活函数与学习率策略。
ai.result: RGB-only模型在LINEMOD数据集上平均ADD精度84.50%，RGB-D融合模型达92.41%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种模块化、可扩展的6D姿态估计方案，代码开源，便于复现与改进。

idea_spark.idea: 探索更高效的关键点选择策略或轻量化骨干网络以提升实时性。
idea_spark.risk: 深度数据质量对融合模型性能影响较大，低质量深度图可能导致精度下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受多模态特征交互启发，可进一步融合语义或几何先验信息。

zh.result: RGB-only模型在LINEMOD数据集上平均ADD精度84.50%，RGB-D融合模型达92.41%。
zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种模块化、可扩展的6D姿态估计方案，代码开源，便于复现与改进。

idea_spark.risk: 深度数据质量对融合模型性能影响较大，低质量深度图可能导致精度下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受多模态特征交互启发，可进一步融合语义或几何先验信息。

en.tldr: Proposes a modular 6D pose estimation framework via keypoint heatmap regression, combining YOLOv10m detection and ResNet18 heatmap prediction with PnP RANSAC, and extends to RGB-D cross-fusion for improved accuracy.
en.motivation: Existing 6D pose estimation methods suffer from limited accuracy in complex scenes and underutilization of depth information.
en.method: Uses YOLOv10m for object detection, ResNet18 to predict 2D keypoint heatmaps, then estimates 6D pose via PnP RANSAC; integrates RGB and depth features through a cross-fusion architecture with optimized activation functions and learning rate schedules.
en.result: RGB-only model achieves 84.50% mean ADD accuracy on LINEMOD, while RGB-D fusion reaches 92.41%.
en.help_to_user: Provides a modular and extensible 6D pose estimation solution with open-source code for easy reproduction and improvement.

idea_spark.idea: Explore more efficient keypoint selection strategies or lightweight backbones for real-time performance.
idea_spark.risk: Depth data quality significantly affects fusion model performance; low-quality depth maps may degrade accuracy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multimodal feature interaction, further integration of semantic or geometric priors could be beneficial.

papers.title: SplatWeaver: Learning to Allocate Gaussian Primitives for Generalizable Novel View Synthesis
papers.summary: Generalizable novel view synthesis aims to render unseen views from uncalibrated input images without requiring per-scene optimization. Recent feed-forward approaches based on 3D Gaussian Splatting have achieved promising efficiency and rendering quality. However, most of them assign a fixed number of Gaussians to each pixel or voxel, ignoring the spatially varying complexity of real-world scenes. Such uniform allocation often wastes Gaussian primitives in smooth regions while providing insufficient capacity for fine structures, complex geometry, and high-frequency details. This motivates us to predict region-dependent primitive cardinalities rather than impose a fixed primitive budget everywhere, enabling a more expressive yet compact 3D scene representation. Therefore, we propose SplatWeaver, a generalizable novel view synthesis framework that is able to dynamically allocate Gaussian primitives over different regions in a feed-forward manner. Specifically, SplatWeaver introduces cardinality Gaussian experts and a pixel-level routing scheme, wherein each expert specializes in producing a specific number of primitives from 0 to M, and the routing scheme coordinates these experts to adaptively determine how many Gaussian primitives should be allocated to each spatial location. Moreover, SplatWeaver incorporates a high-frequency prior with attendant guidance module and routing regularization to stabilize expert selection and promote complexity-aware allocation. By leveraging high-frequency structural cues, the routing process is encouraged to assign more Gaussian primitives to fine structures, complex geometry, and textured regions, while suppressing redundant primitives in smooth areas. Extensive experiments across diverse scenarios show that SplatWeaver consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, delivering more faithful novel-view renderings with fewer Gaussian primitives.

ai.tldr: 提出SplatWeaver框架，通过动态分配高斯原语实现可泛化的新视角合成，无需逐场景优化。
ai.motivation: 现有方法为每个像素或体素分配固定数量的高斯原语，忽略了真实场景的空间复杂度变化，导致平滑区域浪费、精细结构容量不足。
ai.method: 引入基数高斯专家和像素级路由方案，每个专家生成0到M个原语，路由协调专家自适应决定每个位置的高斯数量；结合高频先验和路由正则化稳定专家选择。
ai.result: 在多种场景下，SplatWeaver以更少的高斯原语实现更忠实的新视角渲染，优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过动态分配高斯原语提升可泛化新视角合成的效率和质量。

idea_spark.idea: 利用高频结构线索引导路由，使复杂区域获得更多原语，平滑区域减少冗余。
idea_spark.risk: 路由专家可能过拟合训练数据中的高频模式，导致泛化到未见场景时分配不稳定。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从图像压缩中的自适应比特分配获得启发，将类似思想引入3D表示学习。

zh.tldr: 提出SplatWeaver框架，通过动态分配高斯原语实现可泛化的新视角合成，无需逐场景优化。
zh.motivation: 现有方法为每个像素或体素分配固定数量的高斯原语，忽略了真实场景的空间复杂度变化，导致平滑区域浪费、精细结构容量不足。
zh.method: 引入基数高斯专家和像素级路由方案，每个专家生成0到M个原语，路由协调专家自适应决定每个位置的高斯数量；结合高频先验和路由正则化稳定专家选择。
zh.result: 在多种场景下，SplatWeaver以更少的高斯原语实现更忠实的新视角渲染，优于现有方法。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过动态分配高斯原语提升可泛化新视角合成的效率和质量。

idea_spark.idea: 利用高频结构线索引导路由，使复杂区域获得更多原语，平滑区域减少冗余。
idea_spark.risk: 路由专家可能过拟合训练数据中的高频模式，导致泛化到未见场景时分配不稳定。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从图像压缩中的自适应比特分配获得启发，将类似思想引入3D表示学习。

en.tldr: Proposes SplatWeaver, a framework that dynamically allocates Gaussian primitives for generalizable novel view synthesis without per-scene optimization.
en.motivation: Existing methods assign a fixed number of Gaussians per pixel or voxel, ignoring spatially varying complexity, wasting primitives in smooth regions and lacking capacity for fine structures.
en.method: Introduces cardinality Gaussian experts and a pixel-level routing scheme, where each expert produces 0 to M primitives, and routing adaptively decides allocation; incorporates high-frequency prior and routing regularization.
en.result: Across diverse scenarios, SplatWeaver achieves more faithful novel-view renderings with fewer Gaussian primitives, outperforming state-of-the-art methods.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how dynamic Gaussian allocation improves efficiency and quality in generalizable novel view synthesis.

idea_spark.idea: Use high-frequency structural cues to guide routing, allocating more primitives to complex regions and suppressing redundancy in smooth areas.
idea_spark.risk: Routing experts may overfit to high-frequency patterns in training data, causing unstable allocation on unseen scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by adaptive bit allocation in image compression, applying similar ideas to 3D representation learning.

papers.title: Not All Tokens Learn Alike: Attention Entropy Reveals Heterogeneous Signals in RL Reasoning
papers.summary: Reinforcement-learning-based post-training has become a key approach for improving the reasoning ability of large language models, but its token-level learning signals remain poorly understood. This work studies their heterogeneity through attention entropy, which measures how concentrated or diffuse the contextual support is for each response token.   We first show that token-level RL objectives are sparsely estimable: uniformly random 20 percent token subsets preserve much of the full-token held-out performance, suggesting substantial redundancy in token-level updates. However, entropy-structured subsets behave very differently. Low-attention-entropy tokens, which we call anchors, rely on concentrated support, produce stable gradients aligned with full-token updates, and provide a reliable optimization backbone, but tend to plateau on harder benchmarks. High-attention-entropy tokens, which we call explorers, aggregate more diffuse context and induce larger but more volatile gradients. Explorer-only training is unstable on average, though rare successful runs suggest that these tokens may contain useful hard-reasoning signals when optimization remains stable.   We support this anchor-explorer spectrum with evidence-gathering analyses, entropy dynamics, gradient-geometry diagnostics, and controls showing that position, predictive entropy, and loss normalization do not explain the observed asymmetry. Finally, a dynamic entropy-aware soft-reweighting intervention improves Qwen3-8B-Base from 34.39 to 37.40 held-out average in the strongest setting. These findings suggest that attention entropy reveals optimization-relevant structure in token-level RL signals, and that uniform token averaging can obscure meaningful heterogeneity in reasoning post-training.

ai.tldr: 通过注意力熵揭示强化学习后训练中令牌级信号的异质性，提出锚点-探索者谱系，并设计动态软重加权方法提升推理性能。
ai.motivation: 强化学习后训练能提升大语言模型推理能力，但令牌级学习信号的结构和异质性尚不明确。
ai.method: 利用注意力熵度量每个响应令牌的上下文支持集中度，将令牌分为低熵锚点和高熵探索者，分析其梯度特性，并设计动态熵感知软重加权干预。
ai.result: 随机20%令牌子集保留大部分性能，但熵结构子集行为迥异：锚点提供稳定优化基础，探索者包含硬推理信号但训练不稳定；动态重加权将Qwen3-8B-Base平均性能从34.39提升至37.40。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解RL后训练中令牌级信号的角色，指导更高效的训练策略设计。

idea_spark.idea: 结合锚点和探索者的动态加权策略，可能进一步优化推理模型的训练效率。
idea_spark.risk: 探索者令牌的梯度波动可能导致训练不稳定，需谨慎控制加权系数。
idea_spark.inspiration: 注意力熵作为信号异质性的代理，启发我们重新审视令牌级优化中的冗余与关键信息。

en.tldr: Attention entropy reveals heterogeneous token-level signals in RL post-training, proposing an anchor-explorer spectrum and a dynamic soft-reweighting method that improves reasoning performance.
en.motivation: RL-based post-training enhances LLM reasoning, but token-level learning signals and their heterogeneity are poorly understood.
en.method: Use attention entropy to measure contextual support concentration per response token, categorize tokens into low-entropy anchors and high-entropy explorers, analyze gradient properties, and design a dynamic entropy-aware soft-reweighting intervention.
en.result: Random 20% token subsets retain most performance, but entropy-structured subsets behave differently: anchors provide stable optimization, explorers contain hard-reasoning signals but are unstable; dynamic reweighting improves Qwen3-8B-Base from 34.39 to 37.40 held-out average.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand token-level signal roles in RL post-training and guides more efficient training strategies.

idea_spark.idea: Combining anchors and explorers with dynamic weighting may further optimize reasoning model training efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Explorer token gradient volatility may cause training instability; careful weighting coefficient control is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Attention entropy as a proxy for signal heterogeneity inspires rethinking redundancy and key information in token-level optimization.

papers.title: Semantic-Aware Adaptive Visual Memory for Streaming Video Understanding
papers.summary: Online streaming video understanding requires models to process continuous visual inputs and respond to user queries in real time, where the unbounded stream and unpredictable query timing turn memory management into a central challenge. Existing methods typically compress visual tokens via visual similarity heuristics, or augment compression with KV-cache-level retrieval. However, compression decisions rarely incorporate semantic signals, and retrieval is often added after compression is finalized, making the two stages hard to coordinate. We present SAVEMem, a training-free dual-stage framework that brings semantic awareness into memory generation and lets the retrieval scope adapt per query. In Stage~1, SAVEMem builds a three-tier streaming memory online under a constant memory budget. A fixed pseudo-question bank provides a lightweight semantic prior, so that long-term retention is shaped by semantic salience rather than visual similarity alone. In Stage~2, SAVEMem performs query-aware retrieval over this memory. An anchor-conditioned recency gate adapts the retrieval scope from short-term to mid- and long-term memory based on whether the query targets the present or the distant past. Within this scope, late interaction between query and memory tokens selects candidate frames for answering. Applied to Qwen2.5-VL without training, SAVEMem improves the OVO-Bench overall score from 52.27 to 62.69 and yields consistent gains on StreamingBench and ODV-Bench, while reducing peak GPU memory by 48\% at 128 frames over the backbone.

ai.tldr: 提出SAVEMem，一种无需训练的双阶段框架，通过语义感知内存生成和查询自适应检索，显著提升在线流视频理解性能。
ai.motivation: 现有方法在内存压缩和检索阶段缺乏语义信号协调，导致长视频流中关键信息丢失。
ai.method: 第一阶段利用伪问题库构建三层流式内存，按语义显著性保留长期信息；第二阶段通过锚定条件门控动态调整检索范围，并采用查询-令牌晚期交互选择候选帧。
ai.result: 在OVO-Bench上总分从52.27提升至62.69，StreamingBench和ODV-Bench上一致提升，峰值GPU内存降低48%。
ai.help_to_user: 为流视频问答提供高效内存管理方案，无需额外训练即可适配现有视觉语言模型。

idea_spark.idea: 将伪问题库替换为在线聚类生成的动态语义锚点，进一步自适应视频内容变化。
idea_spark.risk: 伪问题库的固定性可能限制对罕见场景的语义覆盖。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类记忆机制启发，将短期、中期、长期记忆与查询意图结合。

en.tldr: Proposes SAVEMem, a training-free dual-stage framework that enhances online streaming video understanding via semantic-aware memory generation and query-adaptive retrieval.
en.motivation: Existing methods lack semantic coordination between memory compression and retrieval stages, causing loss of critical information in long video streams.
en.method: Stage 1 builds a three-tier streaming memory using a pseudo-question bank for semantic salience; Stage 2 adapts retrieval scope via an anchor-conditioned recency gate and selects candidate frames through late interaction.
en.result: Improves OVO-Bench overall score from 52.27 to 62.69, yields consistent gains on StreamingBench and ODV-Bench, and reduces peak GPU memory by 48% at 128 frames.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient memory management solution for streaming video QA without requiring additional training, compatible with existing vision-language models.

idea_spark.idea: Replace the fixed pseudo-question bank with online clustering to generate dynamic semantic anchors that adapt to video content changes.
idea_spark.risk: Fixed pseudo-question bank may limit semantic coverage for rare scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human memory mechanisms, combining short-term, mid-term, and long-term memory with query intent.

papers.title: Towards Closing the Autoregressive Gap in Language Modeling via Entropy-Gated Continuous Bitstream Diffusion
papers.summary: Diffusion language models (DLMs) promise parallel, order-agnostic generation, but on standard benchmarks they have historically lagged behind autoregressive models in sample quality and diversity. Recent continuous flow and diffusion approaches over token embeddings have narrowed this gap, suggesting continuous state spaces are highly effective for language. In this work, we further close the autoregressive gap by modeling text as a continuous diffusion process over fixed-width binary bitstreams. Our approach represents semantic tokens as analog bit sequences and utilizes a matched-filter residual parameterization to isolate contextual learning from analytic independent-bit posteriors. Crucially, we adopt a stochastic sampler that applies Langevin-type corrections gated by the entropy-rate profile, automatically concentrating stochasticity in high-information regions while remaining nearly deterministic elsewhere. On the One Billion Word Benchmark (LM1B), our 130M-parameter bitstream model reaches a generative perplexity ($\GenPPL$) of $59.76$ at matched real-data entropy ($4.31$) using 256 neural function evaluations (NFEs), decisively outperforming prior DLM baselines and reaching the autoregressive reference. On OpenWebText (OWT), our stochastic sampler establishes a new continuous-DLM Pareto frontier, achieving $\GenPPL=27.06$ at an entropy of $5.26$ using $4\times$ fewer steps than previous 1024-NFE baselines. As an additional architectural benefit, bitstream diffusion removes the $\mathcal{O}(V)$ vocabulary scaling bottleneck shared by standard DLMs. By predicting $\mathcal{O}(\log V)$ bitwise logits via semantic bit-patching, our model yields a reduced memory footprint and higher throughput, demonstrating a scalable paradigm for language generation as vocabulary sizes grow.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于固定宽度二进制比特流的连续扩散语言模型，通过熵门控随机采样器在信息高区域集中随机性，在LM1B和OpenWebText上接近自回归模型性能。
ai.motivation: 扩散语言模型在样本质量和多样性上落后于自回归模型，现有连续流方法虽缩小差距但仍需改进。
ai.method: 将文本建模为固定宽度二进制比特流上的连续扩散过程，采用匹配滤波残差参数化分离上下文学习与分析独立比特后验，并使用熵率门控的Langevin型校正随机采样器。
ai.result: 130M参数模型在LM1B上达到生成困惑度59.76（熵4.31），在OpenWebText上以更少步骤建立新帕累托前沿，同时消除词汇表缩放瓶颈。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、可扩展的语言生成范式，尤其适用于词汇表规模增长场景，降低内存占用并提高吞吐量。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将熵率门控机制应用于多模态扩散模型，在图像或音频生成中自适应控制随机性。
idea_spark.risk: 比特流表示可能丢失部分语义结构，需验证在复杂语言任务上的泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受信息论中熵率概念启发，将随机性集中在高信息区域，类似人类注意力机制。

en.tldr: Proposes a continuous diffusion language model over fixed-width binary bitstreams, using an entropy-gated stochastic sampler to concentrate stochasticity in high-information regions, achieving near-autoregressive performance on LM1B and OpenWebText.
en.motivation: Diffusion language models lag behind autoregressive models in sample quality and diversity; continuous flow methods narrow the gap but still need improvement.
en.method: Models text as a continuous diffusion process over fixed-width binary bitstreams, employs a matched-filter residual parameterization to isolate contextual learning from analytic independent-bit posteriors, and uses an entropy-rate-gated Langevin-type correction stochastic sampler.
en.result: A 130M-parameter model achieves generative perplexity 59.76 (entropy 4.31) on LM1B, establishes a new Pareto frontier on OpenWebText with fewer steps, and removes the vocabulary scaling bottleneck.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and scalable language generation paradigm, especially for growing vocabulary sizes, reducing memory footprint and increasing throughput.

idea_spark.idea: Explore applying entropy-rate gating to multimodal diffusion models for adaptive stochasticity control in image or audio generation.
idea_spark.risk: Bitstream representation may lose some semantic structure; generalization on complex language tasks needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the concept of entropy rate in information theory, concentrating stochasticity in high-information regions, analogous to human attention mechanisms.

papers.title: SAM 3D Animal: Promptable Animal 3D Reconstruction from Images in the Wild
papers.summary: 3D animal reconstruction in the wild remains challenging due to large species variation, frequent occlusions, and the prevalence of multi-animal scenes, while existing methods predominantly focus on single-animal settings. We present SAM 3D Animal, the first promptable framework for multi-animal 3D reconstruction from a single image. Built on the SMAL+ parametric animal model, our method jointly reconstructs multiple instances and supports flexible prompts in the form of keypoints and masks which enable more reliable disambiguation in crowded and occluded scenes. To train such a model, we further introduce Herd3D, a multi-animal 3D dataset containing over 5K images, designed to increase diversity in species, interactions, and occlusion patterns. Experiments on the Animal3D, APTv2, and Animal Kingdom datasets show that our framework achieves state-of-the-art results over both existing model-based and model-free methods, demonstrating a scalable and effective solution for prompt-driven animal 3D reconstruction in the wild.

ai.tldr: 提出首个可提示的多动物3D重建框架，结合SMAL+模型和Herd3D数据集，在野外图像中实现多实例重建。
ai.motivation: 现有方法主要针对单动物场景，难以处理物种多样、遮挡频繁和多动物共存的野外图像。
ai.method: 基于SMAL+参数化动物模型，联合重建多个实例，支持关键点和掩码提示以消除歧义；并构建Herd3D多动物3D数据集。
ai.result: 在Animal3D、APTv2和Animal Kingdom数据集上达到最先进水平，优于现有基于模型和无模型的方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户从单张野外图像中重建多个动物的3D形状，支持交互式提示。

idea_spark.idea: 将提示机制与参数化模型结合，实现灵活的多目标重建。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖SMAL+模型，可能不适用于非四足动物；Herd3D数据集多样性有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用用户提供的稀疏提示（如关键点）引导复杂场景下的3D重建。

en.tldr: First promptable multi-animal 3D reconstruction framework using SMAL+ model and Herd3D dataset for in-the-wild images.
en.motivation: Existing methods focus on single animals, failing in diverse species, occlusions, and multi-animal scenes.
en.method: Jointly reconstructs multiple instances via SMAL+ parametric model, supports keypoint and mask prompts for disambiguation; introduces Herd3D multi-animal 3D dataset.
en.result: State-of-the-art on Animal3D, APTv2, and Animal Kingdom, outperforming both model-based and model-free methods.
en.help_to_user: Enables 3D reconstruction of multiple animals from a single wild image with user prompts.

idea_spark.idea: Combine promptable interaction with parametric models for flexible multi-object reconstruction.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on SMAL+ model, may not generalize to non-quadrupeds; Herd3D dataset may have limited diversity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage sparse user prompts (e.g., keypoints) to guide 3D reconstruction in cluttered scenes.

papers.title: Hierarchical Perfusion Graphs for Tumor Heterogeneity Modeling in Glioma Molecular Subtyping
papers.summary: Precise molecular subtyping of gliomas, including isocitrate dehydrogenase (IDH) mutation and 1p/19q codeletion, directly guides surgical and therapeutic decisions, yet currently relies on invasive tissue sampling. Deep learning on structural MRI has emerged as a non-invasive alternative, but anatomy-only approaches cannot capture the hemodynamic signatures that distinguish molecular subtypes. Radiogenomics based on dynamic susceptibility contrast (DSC) MRI holds immense potential for non-invasively characterizing glioma molecular subtypes, yet clinical deployment has been hindered by inter-site variability and the limitations of voxel-wise analysis. We introduce HiPerfGNN, a framework that first learns discrete hemodynamic representations from raw time-intensity curves using a vector-quantized variational autoencoder (VQ-VAE). These quantized perfusion codes define coarse-level graph nodes representing functional tumor habitats, each of which is hierarchically subdivided into fine-level subregions guided by structural MRI. A hierarchical graph neural network then propagates information across scales for molecular prediction. On an internal cohort (n=475), the model achieved AUCs of 0.96 (IDH), 0.89 (1p/19q), and 0.84 (WHO grade), and maintained robust IDH performance (AUC 0.89) on an independent external cohort (n=397) without recalibration. Gradient-based saliency analysis confirms biologically grounded attention patterns aligned with known glioma pathophysiology. Our results demonstrate the added value of integrating perfusion dynamics into radiogenomic pipelines for glioma molecular subtyping. Code is available at https://github.com/janghana/HiPerfGNN.

ai.tldr: 提出HiPerfGNN框架，利用层次化灌注图建模肿瘤异质性，实现胶质瘤分子亚型的非侵入性预测。
ai.motivation: 现有基于结构MRI的深度学习方法无法捕获血流动力学特征，而基于DSC MRI的放射基因组学受限于站点间差异和体素级分析。
ai.method: 使用VQ-VAE从原始时间强度曲线学习离散血流动力学表示，定义粗粒度图节点（功能肿瘤栖息地），再按结构MRI细分为子区域，通过层次图神经网络跨尺度传播信息。
ai.result: 内部队列AUC达0.96（IDH）、0.89（1p/19q）、0.84（WHO分级）；外部独立队列IDH AUC为0.89，无需重新校准。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种非侵入性、鲁棒的胶质瘤分子亚型分类方法，辅助手术和治疗决策。

idea_spark.idea: 结合多模态MRI（如扩散加权成像）与灌注图，进一步提升亚型分类精度。
idea_spark.risk: 外部验证仅针对IDH亚型，其他亚型泛化性需更多数据验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用离散编码自动发现功能栖息地，启发无监督肿瘤区域分割新思路。

idea_spark.risk: 外部验证仅针对IDH亚型，其他亚型泛化性需更多数据验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用离散编码自动发现功能栖息地，启发无监督肿瘤区域分割新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes HiPerfGNN, a hierarchical perfusion graph framework for non-invasive glioma molecular subtyping by modeling tumor heterogeneity.
en.motivation: Existing structural MRI-based deep learning methods fail to capture hemodynamic signatures, and DSC MRI radiogenomics suffer from inter-site variability and voxel-wise limitations.
en.method: Uses VQ-VAE to learn discrete hemodynamic representations from raw time-intensity curves, defines coarse graph nodes (functional tumor habitats), hierarchically subdivided by structural MRI, and propagates information via a hierarchical graph neural network.
en.result: Achieves AUCs of 0.96 (IDH), 0.89 (1p/19q), 0.84 (WHO grade) on internal cohort; robust IDH AUC of 0.89 on external cohort without recalibration.
en.help_to_user: Provides a non-invasive, robust method for glioma molecular subtyping to guide surgical and therapeutic decisions.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate multimodal MRI (e.g., diffusion-weighted imaging) with perfusion graphs to further improve subtyping accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: External validation only for IDH subtype; generalizability for other subtypes requires more data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Discrete encoding automatically discovers functional habitats, inspiring new unsupervised tumor segmentation approaches.

papers.title: Proxy3D: Efficient 3D Representations for Vision-Language Models via Semantic Clustering and Alignment
papers.summary: Spatial intelligence in vision-language models (VLMs) attracts research interest with the practical demand to reason in the 3D world.Despite promising results, most existing methods follow the conventional 2D pipeline in VLMs and use pixel-aligned representations for the vision modality. However, correspondence-based models with implicit 3D scene understanding often fail to achieve spatial consistency, and representation-based models with 3D geometric priors lack efficiency in vision sequence serialization. To address this, we propose a Proxy3D method with compact yet comprehensive 3D proxy representations for the vision modality. Given only video frames as input, we employ semantic and geometric encoders to extract scene features and then perform their semantic-aware clustering to obtain a set of proxies in the 3D space. For representation alignment, we further curate the SpaceSpan dataset and apply multi-stage training to adopt the proposed 3D proxy representations with the VLM. When using shorter sequences for vision information, our method achieves competitive or state-of-the-art performance in 3D visual question answering, visual grounding and general spatial intelligence benchmarks.

ai.tldr: 提出Proxy3D方法，通过语义聚类和空间对齐为视觉语言模型生成紧凑的3D代理表示，仅需视频帧输入即可在多项3D任务上达到先进水平。
ai.motivation: 现有VLM在3D场景理解中，基于对应的方法缺乏空间一致性，基于表示的方法序列化效率低，亟需一种紧凑且全面的3D视觉表示。
ai.method: 使用语义和几何编码器提取场景特征，通过语义感知聚类得到3D空间中的代理点；构建SpaceSpan数据集并采用多阶段训练对齐代理表示与VLM。
ai.result: 在3D视觉问答、视觉定位和通用空间智能基准上，使用更短的视觉序列即达到竞争性或最优性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何用更少的视觉信息实现高效3D推理，适用于资源受限的机器人、自动驾驶等场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将3D场景压缩为少量语义代理，替代密集像素或体素表示，大幅降低VLM的序列长度。
idea_spark.risk: 聚类质量依赖语义编码器，在复杂场景中可能丢失细节；多阶段训练需要精心设计对齐损失。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受点云下采样和视觉Transformer中token压缩启发，将3D几何与语义信息融合为可学习的代理。
